**⚡ AMENDMENT — 2026-07-22, D518: ARC 9 SLICE 4 SHIPS STRICT SESSION-BOOKMARK POINTERS OVER THE EXISTING NAMED-SLOT AUTHORITY; LANE-020 REMAINS CHATGPT/CODEX DRIVE ONLY FOR THE CONTRACTED SLICE-5 BLOCKER RELEASE.** `src/91-save-slots.js` remains the sole named-slot owner. At most three pointers live only in the existing `G.settings.arc9SessionBookmarks` settings envelope. Every pointer has exactly six scalar fields—cleaned nonempty `label`, integer `slot` 0–2, string `runId`, `side`, exact ruleset id, and fingerprint—with unique slots and no campaign, settings, save, timestamp, focus, action, or other authority snapshot. No localStorage key, save field, schema, `_SAVE_VER`, autosave, undo, import, export, or Ironman bypass was added.

The target fingerprint is exact: deep-clone the already-valid target save; delete only own top-level `slotName` and `when`; recursively sort object keys lexicographically while preserving array order and normal JSON scalar encoding; then emit `v1:<canonical UTF-16 length>:<8-hex hashStr("arc9-a|"+canonical)>:<8-hex hashStr("arc9-b|"+canonical)>`. Object-key order and those two display-only top-level fields are therefore neutral; every nested or authoritative change is binding. Creation and open require exact current live/target run, side, ruleset, non-Ironman authority. Pointer metadata, including `runId`, is type-strict; malformed bookmark settings inside either the live envelope or target save fail closed without repair or unrelated rewrite.

`_slRead` remains authoritative. One shared `_slLoadSlot` now owns both ordinary named-slot loading and bookmark opening: read and guard, request the existing replacement confirmation, re-read and re-guard after that blocking prompt, deep-clone and validate, and only then call `applySave`, initialize War Career, clear undo, and save. A slot deleted or replaced during confirmation cannot cross the apply boundary. Missing, deleted, changed, foreign, side/ruleset/run-mismatched, fingerprint-mismatched, corrupt, malformed, or Ironman targets produce zero apply, save, undo, or partial live-state mutation.

The existing Save & Load manager exposes Bookmark/Update beside qualifying named slots and a Session Bookmarks list with native Open/Remove buttons, complete visible/accessibility names, semantic `role=status` feedback, keyboard/pointer parity, and return focus after rerender. The proactive WCAG 2.2 AA audit corrected two issues before shipment: accessible names now begin with the visible “Bookmark”/“Update Bookmark” label, and long bookmark metadata wraps with `overflow-wrap:anywhere`. Native focus indication, contrast, status semantics, 200%-zoom reflow, reduced-motion independence, and the existing modal keyboard path remain safe. Inspected normal and zoom PNGs show a stable hierarchy with no overlap, clipping, or horizontal overflow.

The focused contract grows exactly 21→31 and is green 31/31 with `ok:true`, zero failed/page/real errors, and fresh normal/zoom PNGs. Its ten new teeth prove exact pointer-only shape and three-slot/save-v1 identity; valid create/open plus object-key-order invariance; top-level rename/time tolerance; missing/deleted failure; current/target run, side, ruleset, and authoritative fingerprint failure; corrupt target, malformed target settings, malformed pointer, and non-string run-id rejection; post-confirmation deletion with zero partial apply/write; Ironman plus undo/import/export independence; malformed-live-settings/unrelated/raw-slot preservation; and pointer/Enter/Space names/status/focus/storage parity. The independent fingerprint oracle carries a non-ASCII nested authoritative scalar and separately computes canonical UTF-16 length plus both FNV-1a passes. The adversarial audit found the malformed-target, scalar-run-id, oracle, and post-confirmation gaps in the first candidate; all four were corrected and locked before final evidence.

Bind S4 bypasses only fingerprint equality and returns the required 30/31 expected red with only `foreign and changed bookmark authority fails closed before apply`, exit 1, and zero page/real errors. Source and generated HTML then restore byte-for-byte and the final clean focused rerun returns 31/31. SHA-256 is `8af9f69da1ecb5a7f68a18d945ae1b64059993805bfc870cc7aea439405ba193` for `src/91-save-slots.js` and `858ce48499b966b27ee775df1964bab87e47d1791e828655f424eb7f27da47a7` for the generated game; MD5 is `dec6e4665b5cd6a0c9deedb5ce5646ed` and `2249daa32d6d2f5981519d7b894de396`. Adjacent save-slot, H0 main-menu, accessibility, play-style, and War Career gates pass 17/17, 5/5, 27/27, 14/14, and 47/47 with fresh artifacts and no exposed failed/page/real errors. Plans pass 9/9, 13/13, and 24/24. Mechanical pins move generated game `de2770b2`→`2249daa3` and source tree `6a385aa2`→`6950a862`; data 65, modules 111 with 115 last, suite 140, three named slots, twenty Desk tabs, Chief cap three, manifest, frozen base, save shape, `_SAVE_VER=1`, Historical/Mayhem authority, and public readiness hold. Normal build is `GATE OK`; syntax and diff checks pass; the focused probe remains suite-excluded and no full battery is owed before Slice 5.

**EXACT NEXT:** make the contracted standalone blocker-only relay commit after this D518 implementation is committed, pushed, fetched, and proven clean. It changes no runtime, probe, generated deliverable, count pin, or suite row; runs no browser/full release battery; claims neither Slice 5 nor LANE-020 `SHIPPED`; and moves LANE-020's first live fields to `CONTRACT` / `none`. Record that production exposes no independent sequential strategic-turn entrypoint before the next real battle: `campaignAdvance` calls `_t1Resolve` only after a real battle result, so invented result data or silent delegated combat would violate the exact-resolver, stop-before-battle, historical-consequence, and D74 laws. Leave Slice 5 unchecked and unenrolled at 31→43 future focused steps and 140→141 future suite rows. Then reload the ledger, take the highest-value dependency-ready ARC 7 non-road conquest item under its own committed lane, and continue D514. No simultaneous edits.

**⚡ AMENDMENT — 2026-07-22, D517: ARC 9 SLICE 3 SHIPS ONE LIVE-REGISTRY-VALIDATED DESK PREFERENCE AND BOTH EXISTING RETURN PATHS BECOME ONE CLICK; LANE-020 REMAINS CHATGPT/CODEX DRIVE FOR SLICE 4.** `src/99-h0-president-desk.js` remains the sole live Desk shell and owner of the lexical twenty-id `H0_DESK_TABS` registry. `h0DeskTabKnown(k)` validates only against that registry; `h0DeskLandingTab()` admits a remembered id only after validation and otherwise preserves `psDefaultDeskTab()`; `h0DeskRememberTab(k)` writes only a changed valid `G.settings.arc9DeskTab` through existing `saveLocal`. No second registry, settings owner, storage key, repair writer, or save field exists.

The native tab route remembers the safe selected id immediately before its existing `_wdRefresh()`. The existing main-menu and between-battle Desk controls still call only `openWarDept()`, but the opener now lands directly on the safe remembered tab. Both measured paths therefore change from Desk-plus-tab in two clicks to Desk-on-target in one. Pointer, Enter, and Space each yield one save, one refresh, the same target/preference/stored value, retained focus, and matching `aria-pressed=true`. Old saves without the preference preserve the play-style `command` default without a write; an invalid or removed value also falls neutral to `command` without repair.

Both A/B paths begin pre-onboarded. Campaign bytes, saved-campaign bytes, owner identity, and storage identity remain equal to the existing landing behavior. Slice 3 adds no entity id, pending action, focus node, campaign mutation, decision bypass, campaign/save authority, RNG draw, receipt, alternate registry, second localStorage key, or `_SAVE_VER` change. The proactive WCAG 2.2 AA audit found no defect and made no edit: native tab semantics, focus indication, `aria-pressed`, contrast, non-color meaning, wrapped reflow, reduced motion, and pointer/keyboard parity all remain safe. The normal screenshot shows Treasury active and focused with a stable Desk hierarchy. At 200% CSS zoom in a 780-pixel viewport, the tab row wraps with zero shell/tab/sheet horizontal overflow; the active button remains fully visible, focused, and pressed, reduced motion is true, and animation/transition dependence is absent.

The focused contract is exactly 21/21 with `ok:true`, empty failed/page/real-error arrays, and fresh normal/zoom PNGs. Its seven new teeth prove measured 2→1 return paths, the sole validated setting write through only `gor_save`, old-save default, invalid/removed neutral fallback without repair, pre-onboarded campaign/save authority identity, native pointer/Enter/Space/focus/pressed parity, and 200% reflow/reduced-motion safety. The first zoom screenshot exposed only Playwright's font-readiness wait hanging after all product assertions; the harness now captures directly through CDP and preserves every tooth. The first post-amendment bind then exposed an expected-red cleanup leak: the intentionally failing invalid-preference fixture left its sheet open and a later native-click row timed out. Moving only that fixture cleanup into `finally` removed the collateral. Bind S3 then bypassed only the allowlisted-tab predicate and produced the required 20/21 expected red with only `invalid or removed preference falls neutral without repair`, zero page/real errors, and exit 1.

After the bind, source and generated HTML restored byte-for-byte. The required H0 main-menu gate initially exposed one parent-identical baseline red: both Slice 3 and an exact temporary sparse D516 `a0e4579e0e0c77eb06b7e76fd5028a1da218b6a0` worktree failed only `chainVisibleFirstViewport` at chain-bottom 904 / sheet-bottom 825 while all other teeth passed. Diagnosis found dynamically injected Command Utilities cards made the desktop grid row taller than the frozen sheet. The lane was amended rather than weakening the tooth: `src/98-h0-main-menu.js` now gives only that desktop column a 520-pixel internal scroller, with tablet/phone flow, DOM order, ids, injectors, actions, state/save behavior, and D74 unchanged; `tools/probe-h0-main-menu.mjs` retains the original fold assertion and adds keyboard reachability/inner containment. The inspected saved-desktop PNG shows the rail above the fold with no overlap or clipping. A second WCAG audit found no defect or needed edit; focus contrast is approximately 13.8:1, native buttons remain reachable without making the region itself tabbable, overscroll containment creates no trap, and the fresh accessibility gate remains green 27/27.

**⚡ AMENDMENT — 2026-07-22, D516: ARC 9 SLICE 2 SHIPS ONE PURE, LIVE-TAB-VALIDATED CHIEF-OF-STAFF NEXT ACTION; LANE-020 REMAINS CHATGPT/CODEX DRIVE FOR SLICE 3.** `src/109-chief-of-staff.js` remains the only changed runtime owner. Existing `cosBriefLines(C)` still owns deterministic severity-descending, rule-id-tiebroken facts and its cap of three. New `cosNextAction(C)` reads only those facts, returns a copy only when the top severity is unique and structurally valid, and returns no authority for zero facts, an equal top severity, or malformed input. It calls no domain renderer and owns no state, save field, setting, storage key, queue, decision, resource spend, time advance, hidden-state prediction, RNG draw, receipt, or focus move.

The H0 Desk's 20-tab registry remains private and single-owned in `src/99-h0-president-desk.js`, which did not move. Brief facts render inert first; only the unique candidate carries a noninteractive marker. After the live H0 shell mounts, `cosWireBrief(C)` validates the actual `#wdTabs[role=group]` membership and native `#wdTab_<id>` button before upgrading exactly one fact to a native `type=button` action. Lower facts stay plain. Missing, tied, invalid, absent, or stale authority exposes the neutral orientation “Review the briefing below; no single live office is available as the next action.” with no live action. If a target disappears after wiring, the click guard demotes the stale control back to an inert fact, strips its action authority, and does not manufacture or force a replacement focus target. A valid action routes only by setting the existing `_wdTab` and calling existing `_wdRefresh`; the brief remains outside `#wdContent`, so Enter, Space, and pointer activation retain focus while the native destination gains `aria-pressed=true`.

The proactive WCAG 2.2 AA audit found one genuine 2.5.3 Label-in-Name issue in the first implementation: its author label reordered visible text. The final accessible name begins with the visible `Label: fact` in the same order, then adds “Open this office.” Native focus indication, 11.16:1 label contrast, 3.02:1 boundary contrast, semantic region/button structure, non-color meaning, and no-focus-theft behavior pass. The final 1366×850 screenshot was visually inspected: one clear focused action sits above two plain facts without overlap, clipping, or hierarchy loss.

The focused contract is exactly 14/14 with `ok:true`, empty failed/page/real-error arrays, and fresh JSON/PNG. Its six new teeth prove exact-one priority, deterministic tie/invalid/all-quiet fallback, deep campaign/save/settings/storage/RNG purity with zero domain calls, the sole valid route, initial and post-wiring stale-target demotion without forced replacement focus, and native pointer/Enter/Space/name/focus parity. The first adversarial run exposed a harness-cleanup flaw: the intentional stale failure skipped fixture removal and polluted the later keyboard row. Moving cleanup to `finally` preserved the tooth and removed collateral. Bind S2 then weakened only `_cosLiveDeskTab`; it returned the required 13/14 expected red with only `Chief stale target fails neutral with no action`, zero page/real errors, and exit 1. The probe now uses the repo's bounded hard-close pattern after Chrome once vanished during teardown; no product tooth was weakened.

After the bind, source and generated HTML restored byte-for-byte. SHA-256 is `352a326661a3ee1b21db863357a5da794a7a913a512f88b306ac17e274a32b38` for `src/109-chief-of-staff.js` and `604521531913701c4ed26b0b5c9e497a237a9b5725f29bdcc26ead7fff0a507f` for the generated game; MD5 is `50512d2471de7d22fbf40704599d7285` and `593d03fe24410047bfd880fc8a1c4813`. Adjacent Chief, Desk, H0 Desk, and accessibility browser gates pass 10/10, 13/13, 3/3 viewports, and 27/27 with fresh artifacts and empty exposed error/pageerror arrays; inspected Desk and H0 PNGs have the declared desktop/tablet/phone dimensions. Mechanical pins move only generated-game `09cc00e6`→`593d03fe` in the Mayhem and War Career plans and source-tree `f6d83f59`→`899a4408` in War Career. Data 65, modules 111 with 115 last, suite 140, three named slots, twenty H0 Desk tabs, Chief cap three, manifest, frozen base, save shape, `_SAVE_VER=1`, Historical/Mayhem authority, and public readiness hold; the focused probe remains suite-excluded through Slice 4. Normal build is `GATE OK`, syntax and diff checks pass, and no full battery is owed before the Slice-5 enrollment boundary.

**EXACT NEXT:** execute LANE-020 Slice 3 without another routing take. **⚡ AMENDMENT — 2026-07-22, D515: ARC 9 SLICE 1 SHIPS MEASURED, OUTCOME-INERT RESOLVER TIMING AND AN HONEST LONG-WORK DESK STATUS; LANE-020 REMAINS CHATGPT/CODEX DRIVE FOR SLICE 2.** The clean filesystem-first ARC 9 plan boundary is `dc1654750079920f365b40ce9eb7e9595483f586`. Profiling happened against that untouched head before runtime edits: 60 fresh-campaign samples measured p95 25.2 ms and 36 sequential samples measured p95 15.4 ms. Both are below the declared 50 ms threshold, and isolated maxima did not repeat. The optimization verdict is therefore `NOT_NEEDED`; do not invent a speed fix without new measured evidence.

The runtime extends only the existing owners. `src/90-president-register.js` keeps the sole `_t1Resolve` and its exact 30-hook order. Five `performance.now()` groups measure calendar/politics, economy/logistics, human-cost/theaters, leadership/people, and decisions/outcome. The snapshot is ephemeral and copy-safe; a new or invalid run clears stale data, and an unsupported clock disables the result instead of falling back to wall time. No timing enters `C`, `G.settings`, receipts, RNG, serialization, saves, `_SAVE_VER`, hook inputs, hook order, or outcomes. Deterministic enabled/disabled A/B fixtures prove campaign, settings, receipt, save, `RND`, and `Math.random` identity.

`src/99-h0-president-desk.js` reads only the latest complete finite five-group snapshot. At or above 50 ms it renders one line immediately below the Desk header and outside `#wdContent`: “Strategic resolution complete. N ms across five ordered system groups.” Short, disabled, incomplete, malformed, and unsupported-clock work renders nothing. The line uses native `role=status` implicit polite semantics with `aria-atomic=true`; it carries no duplicate `aria-live` or author label, percentage, spinner, animation, focusability, autofocus, or focus move. The proactive WCAG audit found and removed the initial redundant live-region labeling that could obscure or repeat the visible text.

The focused contract is exactly 8/8 with `ok:true`, empty failed/page/real-error arrays, and a fresh 1366×850 PNG. Visual inspection shows readable non-color status, no overlap, no horizontal overflow, and stable Desk hierarchy/focus. Bind S1 removes exactly the `human-cost-theaters` mark and runs the isolated static bind path: 2/3 with only `five contiguous marks are exact and timing remains ephemeral` red, empty page/real errors, and exit 1 as expected. Re-adding the mark and rebuilding restores source and generated HTML byte-for-byte by `cmp` at SHA-256 `3e9f44a1b9c78e7ff66cab1c4ed79cc839b55914d49fe075fe0ae3ea73e1ba19` and `b148894f247fa94619563ed7ee23f9a9127e717a43c5a58256509f442c8efd9d`; the post-restore normal probe returns 8/8.

The ARC 9 plan is 9/9 in runtime mode, the Mayhem plan is 13/13, and the War Career plan is 24/24. The declared adjacent browser gates are green at Desk 13/13, H0 Desk 3/3 viewports, accessibility 27/27, decisions 19/19, full campaign 4/4, and campaign-link 19/19; each fresh artifact is `ok:true` with no failed/page-error rows (their older schemas expose no `realErrors` field). Mechanical pins move only as declared: generated-game MD5 `4c775fd1`→`09cc00e6` in both dependent plans and source-tree MD5 `9711d3d7`→`f6d83f59` in War Career. Syntax and normal build are green. Data stays 65; modules stay 111 with 115 last; suite stays 140 and the focused pacing probe remains excluded; three named slots, twenty Desk tabs, Chief cap three, manifest, frozen base, save shape, and `_SAVE_VER=1` hold. No full battery is owed before Slice 5 because no suite enrollment occurs.

**EXACT NEXT:** execute LANE-020 Slice 2 without another routing take. Its source allowlist is only `src/109-chief-of-staff.js`; the growing `tools/probe-desk-pacing.mjs`, generated HTML through the normal build, declared mechanical pins, and milestone documents may follow. Derive zero or one action from existing deterministic `cosBriefLines` priority; validate that its target id is currently present in `H0_DESK_TABS`; route only through `_wdTab/_wdRefresh`. Derivation must never call a domain renderer, create/clear a decision, spend resources, advance time, write campaign/settings/storage, predict hidden enemy state, duplicate a queue, or move focus. Missing, tied, invalid, or stale authority yields a neutral orientation line with no action. Grow the focused probe exactly 8→14 for exact-one priority, deterministic fallback, deep state/save/RNG purity, valid route, stale-target neutral failure, and native keyboard/label/focus behavior. Bind S2 weakens live-tab validation so only the stale-target tooth reds; restore source/generated bytes exactly, rerun clean gates, sync docs, commit/push/fetch/clean parity, then continue Slice 3 under D514. No simultaneous edits.

**⚡ AMENDMENT — 2026-07-22, D514: THE WORK-CONSERVING COMPLETION LOOP SUPERSEDES THE 2–4-SLICE STOP CADENCE; ALL REMAINING FEATURES AND ISSUES ARE AUTHORIZED FOR AUTONOMOUS ADJUDICATION AND DELIVERY.** Aaron's instruction is explicit: make the best reversible decisions, log them, and continue until the project queue is exhausted. D171/D307 remain history for why clean commits and load-bearing relays exist, but a phase change, browser/full-suite gate, ordinary design fork, milestone count, or completion of one bundle no longer ends a healthy session. Every feature still moves through a committed Contract Relay take with complete acceptance criteria and probe design; every implementation still gets focused verification, artifact readback, docs synchronization, an intentional commit, push, and clean `HEAD == origin/main` before the loop selects again.

The completion ledger is `V1-CHECKLIST.md` plus unresolved `REVIEW-QUEUE.md` findings and open lane contracts. D514 authorizes every unchecked outstanding, formerly parked, and formerly v2/deferred item for autonomous research, adjudication, contracting, implementation, verification, or evidence-based closure. This does not reopen shipped work or permit speculative churn: an item closes only as `SHIPPED`, `FIXED`, `SUPERSEDED`, `NOT_NEEDED` with proof, or `BLOCKED_EXTERNAL` with the exact missing authority/evidence and no viable independent remedy. A blocked item is quarantined in the ledger while the loop immediately advances another independent item. The run stops only when every ledger item is closed, or when every remaining item shares the same hard blocker.

The loop's priority is: (0) discharge any owed verification at the clean head; (1) ARC 9 pacing under new LANE-020 because it improves every later development/play session without historical invention; (2) ARC 7's playable conquest ladder using the already Verified rail/water/bounded-sea substrate; (3) ARC 8 soldier depth and citation-grade LANE-002 batches; (4) unresolved review findings and the formerly parked set by severity and teaching/player impact; (5) formerly deferred/v2 depth after its prerequisite owners are stable; then (6) a full release battery, artifact audit, accessibility/history/code review, and repair loop until no live finding remains. Within a lane, dependency order overrides this list. The main loop may reorder only with a written why-line based on dependency, risk reduction, or user value.

Aaron's blanket authorization supplies the explicit boundary decision D511 required, but it does not lower the two-family historical floor. LANE-019 may now contract and build source-backed rail, inland-water, and the two bounded sea services while rendering road service unavailable and preserving every explicit non-link. Road evidence can continue as a parallel research item; no road edge, capacity, interchange, or eligibility may be guessed. Thus missing roads no longer block honest non-road conquest work, while Historical and Mayhem authority remain source-bound.

Decision forks inside authorized work are resolved by the top loop and written to `DECISIONS.md`; do not ask Aaron to choose among reversible in-scope options. Hard stops remain only: destructive/irreversible action not recoverable through git; spending, credentials, accounts, unclear licensing, or new publication authority; a real contradiction with locked source/design law that cannot be solved conservatively; or all remaining work sharing one external blocker. Context or provider capacity is a relay trigger, not a project stop: close at a clean boundary with a zero-context prompt that preserves the completion loop and exact next item.

**EXACT NEXT:** LANE-020 is under the full-access ChatGPT/Codex 5.6 Sol Ultra DRIVE lock after the fresh D513 debt run passed 47/47 browser steps plus static 30/30 with `ok:true`, zero failed/page/real errors, and a decodable 200%-zoom reflow PNG. The live owner/pin inventory now contracts Slices 1-4 over `src/90`, `src/99`, `src/109`, and `src/91` with no new subsystem; Slice 5 is dependency-stopped because production has no standalone turn outside a real battle result. Commit/push the green filesystem-first plan boundary with runtime untouched, then profile the untouched resolver and execute Slices 1-4 with focused proof and clean pushes. After Slice 4, make a standalone blocker commit that releases LANE-020 to `CONTRACT`/unowned without a SHIPPED claim, suite enrollment, or release battery; take ARC 7 under its own lane and continue the D514 loop until a lawful independent sequential-turn owner permits LANE-020's later re-take. Do not fabricate result authority or allow simultaneous edits.
<!-- D516 SUPERSEDED HANDOFF HEAD D514 (BYTE-VERBATIM):END -->

<!-- D515 SUPERSEDED HANDOFF HEAD D513 (BYTE-VERBATIM):BEGIN -->

## ⚡ CONTINUE HERE — 2026-07-08 **D329: BATTLE-RESEARCH COVERAGE IS NOW COMPLETE (RESEARCH/DOCS ONLY).** D329 closes the gap between D327 (five forward lanes) and D328 (the ten shipped battles): every buildable Civil War battle/campaign that is neither shipped nor already covered now has a durable, citation-grade packet. Six new packets in `docs/design/battle-build-research/` — **`eastern-1862`** (Second Bull Run + the Seven Days beyond the built Malvern Hill), **`shenandoah-1862`** (Jackson's Valley), **`shenandoah-1864`** (Sheridan's Valley), **`western-gaps`** (Perryville + Stones River), **`trans-mississippi`** (Wilson's Creek, Pea Ridge, Glorieta, Red River), **`appomattox-campaign`** (Five Forks, the Apr 2 breakthrough, Sailor's Creek, the surrender) — plus a README rewrite and an extended guard (**15/15**, +1 HYGIENE step catching leaked JSON-escape artifacts across all 21 packets). No data/runtime/registry change; `civil_war_generals.html` byte-unchanged (SHA-256 identical pre/post build, not staged). Built via 6 × [Opus researcher → Opus adversarial verifier] + Opus 4.8 final verify; 12 agents, 0 errors. **All six lanes are READY_FOR_SPEC** — leads: **Gaines' Mill** (single-phase defender-hold, the exact inverse of the built Malvern Hill), **Cross Keys + Port Republic**, **Cedar Creek**, **Stones River**, **Pea Ridge**, **Five Forks**. **⚠ ONE LANE IS BLOCKED ON AARON: `trans-mississippi` sits behind D183's verbatim "M8 battle-build still needs Aaron go/no-go"** — a two-tier gate (Tier 1 = the whole Trans-Mississippi build, even the Native-free Elkhorn Tavern axis; Tier 2 = any Leetown Native OOB, which D183/D178 expressly barred). Its READY_FOR_SPEC means "writable once Aaron clears the gate," NOT "start now." **Do not self-clear it.** The adversarial layer again earned its keep: it **refuted the run prompt's own false claim** that Jackson was never a lieutenant general (he was, from Oct 10 1862 — a blanket "any year" probe guard would have falsely failed a correct future Chancellorsville build), and the main-loop verify then caught Jackson's wounding (May 2 1863) vs death (May 10 1863) conflation. Other fixes: ABT Second Manassas shows Union **125,000 *total*** not engaged and prints an anachronistic "Lt. Gen. Longstreet"; Cedar Creek's 7,682 total is **NPS**'s, not ABT's 8,824 or Wikipedia's 8,575. **Rank flip resolved:** Polk and Hardee were **Maj. Gens. at Perryville (Oct 8 1862)** but **Lt. Gens. at Stones River (Dec 31 1862)** — appointed Oct 10-11, two days after Perryville; Stand Watie was a **Colonel** at Pea Ridge (BG only in 1864); Sterling Price was a **Missouri State Guard** major general, not Confederate. **D74/D92 discovery:** Stones River, Perryville, Wilson's Creek and McDowell **invert** the "winner bleeds less" assumption, so a Stones River spec must guard **near-parity, explicitly NOT US < CS**. **Teaching-only (dignity/engine):** "The Burning," the Leetown scalpings, Sand Creek, the Appomattox surrender, Glorieta Pass (a wagon-train raid decided it), Brandy Station and all cavalry raids, the Valley Campaign's operational maneuver, the Red River river-war. Gate green: `probe-battle-build-research` **15/15**, JSON `ok=true`, hygiene `checked:21 clean:true`; build **GATE OK** (no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓; known raw-embed soft warning); `git diff --check` clean. **NEXT:** the library is complete, so a battle-build slice now starts from assembled evidence. The most-ready implementation slice is still **Kennesaw Mountain (Atlanta/March)** per D327; **Gaines' Mill** and **Stones River** are the strongest new additions. Codex may still apply the single Antietam `us_richardson.commander` label fix from D328 at any time.

## ⚡ PREVIOUS — 2026-07-07 **D291: PHASE I SOLDIER'S STORY SCALE-OUT — THREE NEW CITATION-GRADE CS WESTERN REPLACEMENTS (23-25), A SECOND THEATER-BALANCING SLICE. The lane grows 22→25 `Verified` records; side balance 14US/8CS → 14US/11CS; all three Confederate AND Western, deepening Chickamauga (→3 CS), Vicksburg (→2 CS), and Shiloh (→2 CS).** New records in `data/soldier-replacements.json`: **Val C. Giles** (`ss:chickamauga:CS:cs_law_gap:nco` — Sgt, 4th Sergeant from May 1863, Co. B "Tom Green Rifles", 4th Texas, Robertson's Texas Bde / Hood's Division (Law commanding 20 Sep) / Longstreet's Corps on loan, Chickamauga 19-20 Sep 1863; memoir *Rags and Hope*; captured Wauhatchie 29 Oct 1863) · **William Pitt Chambers** (`ss:vicksburg:CS:cs_smith_stockade:nco` — Sgt, rose private→first sergeant, Co. B, 46th Mississippi, Baldwin's Bde / M.L. Smith's Division, Vicksburg garrison; a Covington County schoolteacher; journal *Blood and Sacrifice* — the **2nd Vicksburg-CS** record) · **Sam Houston Jr.** (`ss:shiloh:CS:cs_bragg_corps:pvt` — Pvt, Co. C "Bayland Guards", 2nd Texas, Jackson's Bde / Withers' Div / Bragg's Corps, Shiloh; wounded + left for dead on the second day (7 Apr 1862), a Bible from his mother stopped a ball, POW Camp Douglas; the eldest son of the Unionist governor Sam Houston, removed for refusing the Confederate oath — echoes the fluid-loyalties theme beside Stanley). **Honesty (D92):** Giles ships his documented Sergeant grade (NCO in an NCO slot; "Sergeant Val Giles" is quoted on the field), no portrait; Chambers does NOT claim the 46th held the Stockade Redan salient itself (its parent Smith's Division held that northern sector — the salient proper was Shoup's Louisianans), ships his first-sergeant grade as generic "Sergeant" (transparently disclosed, a within-NCO generalization not a cross-line deflation), no portrait; Houston ships his enlisted Private rank, the Bible/left-for-dead details attributed to sources not asserted, the 7-April wound-day pinned with a bio hedge that some accounts place it on the first day, placed for his own service not his father, no portrait. **Every load-bearing URL was independently verified IN THE MAIN LOOP** (inline `WebSearch`/`WebFetch` — the D289 workflow-hang lesson applied; Giles's rank was raised `:pvt`→`:nco` and Houston's wound-day pinned DURING that verification). ## ⚡ PREVIOUS — 2026-07-06 **D288: E59 FIXED — THE PROCEDURAL SKIRMISH-FORCES CLAMP NO LONGER COMPRESSES AUTHORED ODDS TO A COIN FLIP; AN ODDS-PRESERVING JOINT BRIGADE SCALE (SYMMETRIC CAP 1.8), SHARED BY THE FOUGHT PROCEDURAL PATH AND THE PM3 DELEGATED RESOLVE.** E59 (filed at D277) is closed. `_fldCampaignSkirmishParams` (`src/tactical/T2-campaign-link.js`) used to clamp brigade count [2,5] and men [1300,2100] INDEPENDENTLY per side, destroying the authored `bd.us:bd.cs` ratio — Sumter 6:1 → 2600-vs-2600 (a coin flip a fresh delegate needed up to 9 tries to win); Fredericksburg 1.58:1 → 10500-vs-10500 (both saturated, 1:1). Now the two sides are derived JOINTLY (`_skForces`/`_skDecomp`) so the ratio SURVIVES — proportionally, up to a symmetric cap `SK_MAX_ODDS=1.8`: `effBig=clamp(3400+big*0.13,3400,10500)`, `ratio=min(1.8,big/small)`, `effSmall=effBig/ratio` (floored 2600, lifting effBig), decompose to `{count=clamp(2,5,ceil(eff/2100)), men=clamp(1300,2100,round(eff/count))}`. Pure deterministic; no RNG; `_arSimSeed` untouched; INPUT-fidelity ceiling, NOT a D74 output gate; `_skCnt`/`_skMen` deleted. **The cap is MEASURED (cap-sweep A/B `.tmp/ab-e59-cap.json`):** full ~4:1 preservation strands the delegated US at Sumter and the CS at Kernstown (the campaign advances only on a WIN); **1.8 is the UNIQUE cap** keeping the delegated US completing (mcl 1) and the CS honestly stalling at Spotsylvania (idx 17, inward from the old Nashville/Bentonville). Mean odds distortion 0.391→0.218 (−44%); the coin-flip class is gone; standalone/sandbox path byte-identical (probe-field 23/23, explicit skirmish objects). Honest probe re-baselines: probe-auto-resolve PL-3 flip re-homed Wilderness→Fort Donelson (10/10); probe-full-campaign CS wall 20→16 (4/4). Focused gate GREEN: campaign-link 19/19 · auto-resolve 10/10 · full-campaign 4/4 · bridge 6/6 · field 23/23 · **0 pageerrors** · diff-check clean. **Panel `wf_a0945f25-a3c` (read-only default-refute Opus ×4, D281 lesson): 4× SAFE_TO_COMMIT, 0 BLOCK** (every D74/D69 refutation failed first-hand — pure symmetric input ceiling, seed untouched, shipped `_skForces` byte-for-byte == the A/B harness; STANDS notes all advisory/adopted).** Decide-&-log (not surfaced): the cap preserves every D277 invariant, contradicts no teaching, is symmetric (no D69 cushion), reversible. Full detail: DECISIONS D288 · RUN-LOG D288 · REVIEW-QUEUE E59→fixed-in-D288. **NEXT = Priority-1 Soldier's Story scale-out (another bounded citation-grade named-person slice). M8/Q5/Q6 locked; Phase H parked; Phase D deferred.**

The copy rejects false equivalence: prisoner suffering on both sides is named, but cartel collapse is tied to slavery, Black soldier status, and Confederate policy.
>

Relief can mitigate deaths/returns and add a small returned-prisoner manpower boost, but it cannot reopen the cartel. `C.prisoners` is additive/sanitized with no save-version bump.

The format requires a stable sourced `pid`, generated `ss:` `replacePid`, side/rank/branch/year/team, full persona attributes, `Verified` or `Disputed` provenance, and at least two independent sources.
>
> **REGISTRY SEAM:** `src/37-loot-survival.js` now validates replacement packs before applying them. A valid future record can replace exactly one current generated row, preserve registry length, move the row from Generated/Inferred to sourced Verified/Disputed, keep old-id alias lookup through `ssFindPerson`, and show `Sourced` in the Army Register. Empty or invalid canonical data leaves the current 603-person registry unchanged.
>

The repo now has one consistent model-routing contract, a real frontend redesign milestone because Aaron does not want broadsheet as the global shell, and a normal no-regression runner that includes the D143 full-campaign probe. **NEXT = Phase J save/mod/share hardening, then Phase I loot/survival; Q5 Chattanooga + Q6 USCT playable battles remain LAST.**

Token-efficient helpers are only for bounded packets; every helper/subagent/workflow call must explicitly set model and effort; smaller models never own architecture, combat balance, historical claims, UX direction, accessibility judgment, or final integration.
>

Broadsheet remains for press/editorial/dispatch/provenance surfaces. Constraints stay strict: Civil War gravity, anti-Lost-Cause framing, WCAG 2.2 AA, keyboard support, `reduceMotion`, CVD-safe cues, no unclear-license assets, and Intel UHD-617 performance. Research notes are saved at `docs/design/ui-redesign-research.md`; `V1-CHECKLIST.md` now has H0 with target screens/probes/screenshots/acceptance criteria.

The runner serializes the existing probe suite, enforces probe JSON `ok`/pageerror summaries, checks `diag-classic`, writes `.tmp/vet-no-regression-*.log`, includes `tools/probe-full-campaign.mjs`, and kills timed-out child process groups instead of hanging forever. Heavy visual probes have explicit larger budgets.

The audit found and fixed real harness/gate issues instead of weakening probes: accessibility static-scan now strips comments/strings; `bootprobe` has bounded browser cleanup; `probe-phased-3d` isolates each battle page and has a 90s evaluate timeout; `probe-render-richness` isolates each scene in a fresh browser; `vet-no-regression` has per-probe timeout budgets and process-group cleanup. ## ⚡ EARLIER — 2026-06-26 **PHASE J · FULL-CAMPAIGN PLAYTHROUGH PROBE COMPLETE (D143).** This is a **probe-only** milestone: no gameplay/source/data/build/combat/save behavior changed. It does not create a second campaign simulator and does not touch `src/`, `data/`, `build/base.html`, or the generated HTML by hand.

Each battle must win, increment campaign stats, append to `completed[]`, advance `idx`, clear recovery flags, and show the President's interstitial before continuing. The final chain win must render `#wwReport` / `Final Reckoning` and clear `G.campaign`.

## ⚡ EARLIER — 2026-06-26 **PHASE H · H5 increment 4 — FORTIFICATIONS DEPTH COMPLETE (D142), closing the locked D138 UG:G-fidelity modern-engine push.** This is a **D74 gameplay carve-out**, not presentation-only: the modern engine now has staged entrenchments (**rifle pits / hasty cover → full parapet → redoubt / earthwork**), facing-aware works (front strongest, flank reduced, rear bypass still matters), stronger/clearer prepared abatis when tied to own parapets/redoubts, visible 2D/3D earthworks, HUD status, a player charge-impetus commit lock, and H5-i3's swamp/town/fort arrays now feed the universal T0 cover/move hooks (no per-battle fudge). **NEXT = H2 reenactment-footage decision or Phase I/J polish; Q5 Chattanooga + Q6 USCT battles remain LAST.**

> **WHAT SHIPPED (D142):** `src/tactical/T13-engineering.js` now interprets `u.entrench` as staged works: early rifle pits/hasty cover, full parapet, then redoubt/earthwork. `fldEngCover(u, fromX, fromZ)` is stage + facing aware: frontal fire gets the earned bonus, flank fire is reduced, rear fire bypasses the works. Units that march off their own works lose the cover while the visible works fade. Abatis built by a brigade in its own parapet/redoubt is marked prepared, starts stronger, slows more, bites morale/disorder/fatigue harder, clears slower, and renders with clearer stakes/backing. 2D and 3D draw hasty/parapet/redoubt shapes; the HUD names the stage and front-cover multiplier; the end card teaches the Hess/Mahan fortification lesson.
>
> **CHARGE + TERRAIN SIM WIRING:** `src/tactical/T20-order-feel.js` adds the deferred player-only charge impetus lock: a 1.2s correction grace, then move/hold/queue/re-aim orders are refused until contact, target loss, fog loss, own rout, or own death. AI inline charges stay unchanged. `src/tactical/T0-field-sandbox.js` now wires D141 terrain through `fldCoverAt`/`fldMoveFactor`: fort strong cover + attacker friction, town hard cover + street friction, swamp soft concealment + major slow, with the old wall/woods/hill ladder retained. This is ONE universal combat model; no scenario outcome gate.
>
> **BUG-HUNT FINDING FIXED:** direct adversarial audit found the charge-lock predicate promised "rout/death releases" but did not test the charging unit's own state. Fixed `fldChargeLocked`/HUD to release on own rout/death and extended `probe-order-feel` to assert it (`releasedOnOwnRoutDeath:true`).
>
> **VET (GREEN, post-fix):** `node tools/build.mjs` → **GATE OK**; focused probes `probe-engineering-corps` 23/23, `probe-terrain-readability` 29/29, `probe-order-feel` 13/13, `probe-bullrun` 15/15, all 0 pageerrors; `probe-presets` 26/26; `probe-phased-ab` 20 comparisons / 0 diffs; full no-regression battery rerun after the final charge-lock fix passed (`bootprobe`, `t1probe`, core tactical, all current battle probes, visual/3D probes, and strategy probes); `diag-classic` reports `nonBlank:346`; `git diff --check` clean. Known diagnostic noise: `bootprobe ok=true errors=10` with no real failure bucket, and `diag-classic` prints two tolerated 404 console lines while still reporting the required nonblank result.

## ⚡ EARLIER — 2026-06-26 **PHASE H · H5 increment 3 — TERRAIN READABILITY: the modern (non-hex) engine now makes high/low ground — and EVERY topography type — EXPLICIT (D141), the THIRD step of the locked D138 arc.** Three player-chosen **elevation modes** (default **HILLSHADE** relief = T21's, no double-count · **CONTOURS** = iso-lines + elevation labels · **COLOR-BY-HEIGHT** = a CVD-safe **Viridis** hypsometric tint), cycled from the in-battle settings drawer + the **R** key + the legend's mode chip; an **always-on compact LEGEND** (mode chip · viridis gradient · a live **on-hover elevation+type readout** · a topography key of the types present); the 2D top-down finally shows relief it never had; and **swamp / town / fort** are now distinct render types. All PRESENTATION-ONLY → byte-identical. The hex engine IS Classic — untouched.

> **⭐ THE i3 DISCIPLINE (read first — the SAME as i2, NOT the i1 carve-out).** PRESENTATION-ONLY, held to the FULL D74 byte-identity law. NEW `src/tactical/T22-terrain-readability.js` wraps the render seams BY ASSIGNMENT (fld3dInit / fld3dBuildTerrain / fld2dInit / fldPointerMove / fldExit; carries the ._wx/._atmo/._rr/._vf markers forward), reads-only, never writes sim/`fldRng`/`_SAVE_VER`. The elevation mode is a sim-UNREAD `G.settings.fldElevMode` (the renderRich pattern — kept in `G.settings` only, NOT localStorage, so the documented "hillshade" DEFAULT holds every fresh session). The new **swamp/town/fort** terrain arrays (`__FIELD.terrain.swamps/towns/forts[]`) are **combat-INERT — read by NO sim/combat function** (a probe static-scan enforces it), so combat stays byte-identical even though terrain was ADDED → proven `probe-presets` 26/26 + `probe-phased-ab` 20/0-diff + `probe-bullrun` 15/15.
>
> **WHAT SHIPPED (D141) — T22 (≈630 lines) + thin T0/T6 seams.** (1) **3 modes**: HILLSHADE (3D = T21's slope-AO relief, T22 adds NOTHING to the 3D ground here so relief is never double-counted; 2D paints its own cached hillshade — the top-down had none); CONTOURS (a 3D `LineSegments` of marching-squares iso-lines + elevation-label sprites; 2D = offscreen-cached polylines); COLOR-BY-HEIGHT (a 3D viridis-tinted overlay mesh; 2D = cached viridis canvas). The 3D hypso+contour are PREBUILT once and `.visible`-toggled per mode (snappy, no rebuild, no camera jerk). (2) **Legend + hover** — `#fldElevLegend` (mode chip · viridis gradient bar · `#fldElevHover` live readout · topography key). (3) **swamp/town/fort** distinct in 2D + 3D (swamp = wet-teal disc + reeds; town = clustered buildings; fort = angular earthwork berm + bastions); a demo set in the sandbox showcase. T0 seams: `fldBuildTerrain` gains the demo arrays; `fld2dDraw` calls the guarded `fldTrDrawGround2d` after the field fill; `fldKey` gains the `R` branch. T6: the drawer gains an "Elevation display" cycle control.
>
> **AARON'S 3 FORKS (AskUserQuestion):** palette = **Viridis** · legend = **always-on compact panel** · topography = **add swamp/town/fort** (visual-now / sim-cover-wiring-later — that's what keeps i3 byte-identical).
>
> **BUG-HUNT (43 agents, 6 lenses × 3-skeptic refute + completeness critic):** 4 confirmed of 12 raw + 2 critic-new, **ALL FIXED before commit:** (perf) the 2D contour mode re-stroked ~600–1600 segments EVERY frame → now rendered ONCE to an offscreen canvas (2 batched strokes) + blitted; (a11y MED) the legend mode/collapse buttons dropped keyboard focus to `<body>` on the innerHTML rebuild → focus restored in the click handlers; (MED) the legend topography key + height-range went STALE on a phase advance → a topography-aware legend signature now refreshes on any terrain change in BOTH renderers; (LOW) dropped the localStorage round-trip; (LOW) removed a redundant double-dispose of the cloned hypso mesh; (LOW) the per-pointer hover classify early-outs on a coarse cell; (robustness) the base-called 2D hook gained a full-body try/catch. Critic independently confirmed combat-inertness + the fldExit dispose ORDER + the 3D contour orientation are all correct.
>
> **VET (GREEN — presentation-only, FULL D74):** `GATE OK`; `probe-terrain-readability` **26/26** (NEW); `probe-presets` 26/26 + `probe-phased-ab` 20/0-diff + `probe-bullrun` 15/15 (byte-identity + the golden snapshot's new combat-inert guard); `probe-visual-fidelity` 26/26 (T21 coexists, post-fix) + flags 36/36 + render-richness 30/30 + weather 30/30 + atmospherics 18/18 (3D health); `bootprobe` realErrors:[]; `diag-classic` nonBlank:346 + m3dActive:false (Classic untouched); 0 pe; `wcag-auditor` **AA** (15/15; gradient-bar border lifted for 1.4.11; `aria-live="off"` on the per-move readout confirmed correct). `probe-tactical-visuals` 8-scene stalled partway on the 8 GB Mac (documented heaviest-swiftshader memory stall, NOT a regression — its completed scenes incl. a clean 3D one). **Committed + pushed.**
>
> **⏳ NEXT — the rest of H5:** **i4 FORTIFICATIONS DEPTH** (deepen entrench/abatis/redoubt earthworks + **the charge-impetus commit-lock deferred from i1**; also the natural home to give swamp/town/fort their sim cover/move effects). **Q5 Chattanooga + Q6 USCT battles LAST** (Aaron's locked queue). **Resume map:** START-HERE → this block → DECISIONS D141/D140/D139/D138/D74/D54 → V1-CHECKLIST "H5" → `src/tactical/T22-terrain-readability.js` + `tools/probe-terrain-readability.mjs` + memory `civilwar-terrain-readability` / `civilwar-modern-engine-ugg-fidelity`.

> **⭐ THE i2 DISCIPLINE (read first — the INVERSE of i1).** i1 was the deliberate D74 byte-identity CARVE-OUT (gameplay). **i2 is the opposite: PRESENTATION-ONLY, held to the FULL D74 byte-identity law.** NEW `src/tactical/T21-visual-fidelity.js` wraps the render seams BY ASSIGNMENT (the T16/T17/T18 pattern, OUTERMOST), writes vertex COLOUR only (never a position/sim field/`fldRng`/`_SAVE_VER`), and the headless AI-vs-AI stepper never enters a 3D seam → the per-battle outcomes stay **byte-identical: `probe-presets` 26/26 + `probe-phased-ab` 20/0-diff (final build)**.
>
> **WHY ADDITIVE, NOT A TONE-MAP FLIP (the r128 decision):** THREE is r128 (0.128.0), PRE the r152 colour-management overhaul. A grounded 5-agent recon proved a global ACES/sRGB flip would SPLIT the pipeline (the T16 smoke + T17 precip raw-`gl_FragColor` ShaderMaterials don't tone-map → smoke darkens / rain warms, a visible seam) + force re-tuning every hardcoded colour across T0/T10/T17/T18 → EXCLUDED. Real shadow maps (r128 InstancedMesh depth-pass blob + UHD-617 cost) + MeshStandard terrain (renders DARKER without an envMap under the linear pipeline) also excluded. The win is purely ADDITIVE + palette-preserving.
>
> **WHAT SHIPPED (D140) — five layers in T21, all gated by `fldVfOff()` (T18's `renderRich="off"` reverts the whole stack):** (1) **ground RELIEF** — a slope-AO recolour of the terrain VERTEX COLOURS after T18 (low ground darkens / crests lift, normalized to the battle's height range, + a steep-slope soil tint; latch `colA._vfAO`; positions untouched → units seat on analytic `fldTerrainH`); (2) per-brigade soft **CONTACT SHADOWS** (a MeshBasic ground pool, glued + slightly wider than the block); (3) gradient **SKY DOME** (a BackSide MeshBasic sphere, horizon re-tinted from the live weather fog colour each frame; `scene.background` RETAINED as the far-clip fallback) + a free **CSS VIGNETTE** (below the HUD, `pointer-events:none`, `aria-hidden`); (4) a stylized **rank MAP** (white-based CanvasTexture multiplied on each slab → massed-infantry read, CVD-safe); (5) Max-tier **PEG RANKS** (a per-brigade InstancedMesh, `!fldLow()` only, laid out once + x-scaled per frame, bounding-sphere-cullable). Aaron's 3 forks (AskUserQuestion): scope = **Max**, colour = **stay linear**, relief = **Moderate**.
>
> **BUG-HUNT (19 agents, 6 lenses × 3-skeptic refute + completeness critic → SAFE TO COMMIT, 0 confirmed, D74 confirmed STRUCTURALLY):** 4 raw findings — 2 refuted (mid-battle `renderRich`/`gfxQuality` toggles aren't reachable through the fld3d UI; `gfxQuality` drives the SEPARATE legacy `__M3D` engine), 2 perf nits APPLIED anyway as forward-looking polish (the peg IM gained a bounding sphere so r128 frustum-culls it off-camera — scaling to the bigger Q5/Q6 OOBs; a redundant per-frame shadow-opacity re-pin dropped).
>
> **VET (GREEN — presentation-only, FULL D74):** `GATE OK`; `probe-visual-fidelity` **26/26** (NEW — wrappers+markers · dome+fog-matched horizon+retained bg · vignette · relief reads high>low + positions untouched · glued shadow gap ~0.5 + rank map + peg IM count 22 + sphere · `fldLow()` peg gate · seed/sim invariance + `errN===0` · `renderRich="off"` full revert · 0 pe/texWarn · screenshot VISUALLY CONFIRMED); `probe-presets` **26/26** + `probe-phased-ab` **20/0-diff** (byte-identity, final build); `probe-render-richness` **30/30** + `probe-weather` **30/30** (two stale assertions ROOT-CAUSE-fixed, not weakened — the rr combat-scan now excludes T21 [it rides T18's public `fldRrOff()` opt-out]; T21 RETAINS `scene.background` rather than nulling it); `probe-flags` 36/36 + `probe-atmospherics` 18/18 + `probe-tactical-visuals` ok (3D stack coexists); `bootprobe` realErrors:[]; `t1probe` ok; `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346` + `m3dActive:false` (Classic untouched); 0 pageerrors; `wcag-auditor` N/A by construction (the only new DOM is the aria-hidden/pointer-events:none vignette below the HUD; CVD-safe; nothing animates). **Committed + pushed.**
>
> **⏳ NEXT — the rest of H5 (the D138 arc, in order):** **i3 TERRAIN READABILITY** (explicit high/low — default HILLSHADE relief; toggle to contour-lines+labels / color-by-height hypsometric tint; an elevation legend + on-hover readout; all topography distinct; **presentation-only → D74 byte-identical**, prove via probe-presets + probe-phased-ab; base = `fldBuildTerrain`/`fld3dBuildTerrain` + T12 + the new T21 relief + `tcDecorateMap`) · **i4 FORTIFICATIONS DEPTH** (deepen entrench/abatis/redoubt earthworks + **the charge impetus commit-lock deferred from i1**). Asset budget = self-contained core + optional HD pack. **Q5 Chattanooga + Q6 USCT battles LAST** (Aaron's locked queue). **Resume map:** START-HERE → this block → DECISIONS D140/D139/D138/D74/D54 → V1-CHECKLIST "H5" → `src/tactical/T21-visual-fidelity.js` + `tools/probe-visual-fidelity.mjs` + memory `civilwar-modern-engine-ugg-fidelity` / `civilwar-terrain-readability`.

## ⚡ EARLIER — 2026-06-25 **PHASE H · H5 increment 1 — MOVEMENT & ORDERING FEEL: the modern (non-hex) engine moves & obeys like Ultimate General: Gettysburg (D139) — the FIRST step of the locked D138 arc.** Point + facing-handle move, **drag-onto-enemy charges THAT brigade**, **immediate + shift-queue** waypoint routes, a live ORDER GHOST in 2D + 3D, and **faster digging** (T13 70→42s). The deliberate D74 byte-identity CARVE-OUT (vetted as gameplay).

> **⭐ THE CARVE-OUT THAT MADE IT SAFE (read first).** H5-i1 is the deliberate **D74 byte-identity CARVE-OUT** (D138): movement/order changes alter the sim, so they're vetted as GAMEPLAY (a synthetic-gesture probe), not via byte-identity. The discipline that kept it clean: I changed ONLY the **player control surface** (a new module + guarded T0/T13 seams) and the dig RATE — never `fldAiUnit`/`fldSimStep`/`fldStepMovement`'s shared logic. Because no AI/scenario unit ever calls `fldOrderCharge` (the AI sets `u.order` inline), ever gets a `.queue` (only the player shift-drag creates it; the queue-advance guard is a strict no-op otherwise), or ever digs (T13 invariant), the **headless AI-vs-AI per-battle outcomes stay BYTE-IDENTICAL** — proven by `probe-presets` 26/26 + `probe-phased-ab` 20 comparisons / 0 diffs (vs HEAD, final build) + all 9 per-battle probes unchanged. The carve-out's room is the human's controls, not the AI's.
>
> **WHAT SHIPPED (D139):** NEW `src/tactical/T20-order-feel.js` — `fldResolveOrderGesture(sel, gst)` (the testable gesture→orders core: place+face · drag-to-charge · shift-queue · re-aim), the waypoint queue (`fldApplyOrder`/`fldEnqueueOrder`/`fldOrderQueueAdvance`), `fldEnemyAt`/`fldOrderHasHandle`/`fldHandleHit`, and the live ORDER GHOST — 2D (`fldOrderGhost2d`: dashed destination footprint + facing-handle arrow + stem + queued-route dots; charge = filled double-arrow + foe-ring; queue = dashed stem + "+" tick) and 3D (`fld3dEnsureGhost`/`fld3dSyncDrag`: a persistent group — ring + facing arrow + a charge foe-ring + a queue "+" tick, `depthTest:false`+`depthWrite:false`, per-mesh `renderOrder` 13/14). **T0 seams** (`src/tactical/T0-field-sandbox.js`): `fldOrderCharge(u,target)` (optional target; `target==null` → the VERBATIM nearest-enemy scan); `fldStepMovement` arrival → `fldOrderQueueAdvance` (queue hook, no-op without `.queue`); `fldPointerDown`/`fldPointerUp` rewired to the resolver + handle-hit; `fld2dDraw`/`fld3dInit`/`fld3dRender`/`fld3dDispose` ghost hooks; the HUD help line. **T13**: `DIG_TIME_BASE` 70 → **42** (full parapet ~42s @ Balanced; Arcade ~29s / Historian ~55s).
>
> **AARON'S 4 FORKS (AskUserQuestion):** (1) order model = **immediate + shift-queue**; (2) charge = **drag-onto-enemy** (no impetus commit-lock yet — deferred to i4); (3) facing UX = **point + facing handle**; (4) fortify = **faster/cheaper digging**.
>
> **BUG-HUNT (31 agents, 6 lenses × 3-skeptic refute + completeness critic → SAFE TO COMMIT, 0 must-fix, 0 HIGH, no byte-identity leak):** 7 confirmed render/pointer/UX/a11y findings, all minor; I fixed 4 in-increment under the standing AA/CVD-by-shape bar — **RR-1** 3D ghost `depthWrite:false` (was polluting the transparent depth buffer — the T12 convention); **RR-2** per-mesh `renderOrder` (group-level was a no-op); **A11Y-1** queue-vs-immediate 2D drag now reads by SHAPE (dashed stem + "+" glyph) not the CVD-collapsing amber/green hue; **A11Y-2** 3D ghost shape redundancy (charge foe-ring + queue "+" tick). Left **UX-2** (select-radius can shadow a handle in dense clusters) documented-acceptable for the 2-brigade sandbox → revisit with denser OOBs.
>
> **VET (GREEN — gameplay, NOT byte-identity, per the carve-out):** `GATE OK`; `probe-order-feel` **12/12** (the gameplay vetting — place+face, tap-keeps-facing, drag-onto-the-FAR-enemy charges that one, default-charge-still-nearest, shift-queue, queue-advance, re-aim, handle hit-test, fog-gated `fldEnemyAt`, dig-timing **42s vs 70s** logged, the no-AI-queue/no-AI-dig invariant over a 1936-step AI battle, 2D ghost render); `probe-presets` **26/26** + `probe-phased-ab` **20/0-diff** (byte-identity of the AI sim); 9 per-battle probes + field + fog all ok; `probe-engineering-corps` **22/22** (dig retune — relationship-based assertions); `bootprobe` realErrors:[]; `probe-flags` 36/36 + `probe-tactical-visuals` ok (3D scene healthy with the ghost group); `t1probe` 14/14; `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`; 0 pageerrors; 2D ghost visually confirmed. **Committed + pushed.**
>
> **⏳ NEXT — the rest of H5 (the D138 arc, in order):** **i2 visual fidelity** (the polished HYBRID look — richer 3D terrain/lighting/smoke/unit detail, reuse run-h PBR/HDRI/post-FX) · **i3 terrain readability** (explicit high/low ground — default HILLSHADE, toggle to contour-lines+labels / color-by-height, legend + hover readout, all topography distinct; **presentation-only → D74 byte-identical**, prove via probe-presets + probe-phased-ab) · **i4 fortifications depth** (deepen entrench/abatis/redoubt earthworks + **the charge impetus commit-lock deferred from i1**). Asset budget = self-contained core + optional HD pack. **Q5 Chattanooga + Q6 USCT battles LAST** (Aaron's locked queue). **Resume map:** START-HERE → this block → DECISIONS D139/D138/D74/D54 → V1-CHECKLIST "H5" → `src/tactical/T20-order-feel.js` + the T0/T13 seams + `tools/probe-order-feel.mjs` + memory `civilwar-modern-engine-ugg-fidelity`.

The real-time engine base to push = `fldLaunch*` / `tactical/T*` (drag-to-maneuver); terrain base = `fldBuildTerrain`/`fld3dBuildTerrain` + T12 + T18 + `tcDecorateMap`.

>
> **COVERAGE (accuracy over completeness):** **6 of 9 marquee battles imaged** (bullrun1=Sudley Ford · antietam=Gardner Bloody-Lane dead · fredericksburg=Rappahannock pontoons · chancellorsville=Russell Marye's-Heights stone-wall dead · vicksburg=Shirley-House siege lines · gettysburg=O'Sullivan Harvest-of-Death). **3 IMAGELESS + documented** (no clean PD wartime photo): malvern, shiloh, chickamauga. **The VIEW discipline caught + rejected 3 non-photographs** (a 1917 birdseye panorama, the McElroy battle map, a book engraving) + a bullrun1 caption/image mismatch (stone-bridge→Sudley-Ford).

> **DIGNITY-SENSITIVE (the D135 Fort-Pillow precedent):** three plates are photographs of the dead — descriptive alt + a visible `<figcaption>` naming the cost plainly, anti-Lost-Cause, never glorifying; the right plate matched to the right field.

Proven: `probe-presets` **26/26** + `probe-phased-ab` **20 comparisons / 0 diffs**. No combat/T*/data/base/save file touched (`git diff --stat`).
>
> **BUG-HUNT (SAFE TO COMMIT, 27 agents — PD-licence + anachronism/dignity-VIEW + D74-purity + a11y + robustness lenses × adversarial-refute + a completeness critic that re-VIEWed all 6 plates):** 1 confirmed MED, FIXED — the gettysburg "Harvest of Death" credit was "Library of Congress" but the embedded plate is the **Smithsonian American Art Museum** scan (matching the provenance) → corrected; the fix did not over-reach onto fredericksburg (genuinely LoC). a11y AA: caption 7.59:1, credit 5.93:1.

Carney with the colors, Col. Shaw, Frederick Douglass, Harriet Tubman, the Fort Pillow massacre engraving, and the "Come and Join Us Brothers" recruitment broadside. > **THE 7 IMAGES (all PD, all VIEW-verified period-accurate) → codex ids:** `united-states-colored-troops` = Company E, 4th USCI at Fort Lincoln (LoC); `54th-massachusetts-infantry` = Sgt. William H. Carney with the colors; `robert-gould-shaw` = the uniformed Union colonel; `frederick-douglass` = an 1860s CDV; `harriet-tubman` = the Powelson CDV; `nathan-bedford-forrest` = the **1864 Harper's Weekly "Massacre at Fort Pillow" engraving** (the dignity/teaching choice — centers the atrocity against surrendering USCT, anti-Lost-Cause, NOT a Forrest portrait); `system-manpower-pool-usct` = the 1863 "Come and Join Us Brothers" recruitment broadside (also shown in "The Ranks" once USCT organized). **The VIEW discipline earned its keep during sourcing:** the first Shaw pin resolved to "Robert Gould Shaw **2d**" — a striped-suit early-1900s NAMESAKE, not the colonel — caught on VIEW + re-sourced before embed.

## ⚡ EARLIER — 2026-06-25 **PHASE H ("make it come to life") · H1 increment 1 — THE OFFLINE PORTRAIT TIER + THE D71 ASSET-INGESTION PIPELINE: the single-file deliverable's public-domain portraits now show even when it is opened STANDALONE (no `assets/` folder beside it) — the build inlines a small compressed tier into `__ASSETS`, and a `window.portraitFor` override prefers base hi-res when present, else the embedded tier, else the engraving. Aaron chose "Go on H1/H2 now" (AskUserQuestion). Combat byte-identical, proven. NEXT in H1 = a NEW-asset category (weapons/flags/scenes/USCT) or H2 footage — SURFACE each per-asset for licence + anachronism sign-off** (overnight run, D133)

> **THE GATED FORK — surfaced + cleared (AskUserQuestion).** H1/H2 (PD imagery + footage) is the ONE external-asset / network-fetch / IP-licensing / single-file-stressing roadmap item every doc flags as NOT unattended-safe. Aaron chose **"Go on H1/H2 now"** (present to review assets). **Increment 1 builds the D71 ingestion pipeline + proves it on the ALREADY-PD-vetted, in-repo portraits** — no NEW external fetch this increment (the 155 `assets/portraits/*.jpg` were downloaded + Commons-PD-gated in run-h); each subsequent category (weapons/flags/scenes/USCT/footage) fetches NEW assets → SURFACE each for per-asset sign-off.
>
> **THE GAP CLOSED:** the frozen base.html PD-photo layer (`_warmPhotos`) loads `assets/portraits/<key>.jpg` by RELATIVE PATH → the real photos showed only when served beside `assets/`; opened standalone they 404'd to the procedural engraving. (The in-game "131 portraits" were the engravings; 155 real PD photos sat unembedded on disk.)
>
> **WHAT SHIPPED (3 files + the tier):** `tools/prep-embed-assets.mjs` (NEW — macOS `sips` ≤128px/q64 → `assets/embed/portraits/`, 155 files/953KB, committed source) + `tools/build.mjs` (a stage Base64-inlines `assets/embed/**` → bare-name `var __ASSETS` with a HARD 3.0MB / SOFT 1.5MB budget + a dup-key gate + a `withFileTypes` non-file skip + a `"data:…"`-mask before the HEX_BOMB textual scan since `node --check` already proves parseability) + `src/21-photo-embed.js` (NEW module after `20-president-render.js` — overrides `window.portraitFor`: base hi-res relative photo → this embedded 128px tier (offline-safe, framed by a pixel-faithful copy of base `_framePhoto`) → the engraving; LAZY per-key framing with eager-warm ONLY when an offline probe-image 404s; + a `.lead-badge` MutationObserver upgrade). File 3.1MB→4.3MB.
>
> **ARCHITECTURE — combat byte-identical BY CONSTRUCTION (D74):** touches NO combat/data/save file; reads only the new `__ASSETS` global + read-only DOM, never `fldRng`, no save-version bump — only swaps an `<img>` source. Proven: `probe-presets` 26/26 + `probe-phased-ab` 20/20 (0 diffs).
>
> **Bug-hunt (6 diverse-lens Opus finders; D74-purity lens 0 findings → byte-identity clean; the workflow's verify/critic legs dragged on the 8 GB Mac → substituted by inline code-verification + a standalone completeness-critic agent on the FIXED code, the D124/D128/D132 precedent; critic VERDICT: SAFE TO COMMIT):** **(MED, standout)** `_pheUpgrade` DOWNGRADED a base hi-res lead-badge to the 128px tier when served-from-root (a badge built after base hi-res warms carries no `data-portrait-photo` flag) → FIXED to skip any `<img>` already showing a JPEG (a src sniff), locked by a new NO-DOWNGRADE probe assertion; **(MED/LOW)** eager-warming all 155 at boot was pure waste served-from-root → FIXED to LAZY framing + an offline-gated eager warm; **(LOW ×3)** EISDIR on a stray sub-dir → `withFileTypes` skip; silent extension-collision key → a dup-key die; no per-file cap → a prep warn; **(MED honesty)** "offline-portable" scoped to PORTRAITS (3D terrain/HDRI/GLB still relative-path) → documented.
>
> **Vet (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK`; `probe-photo-embed` **22/22** (offline portability proven by route-blocking `assets/portraits/` → photo still renders; NO-DOWNGRADE guard; the fallback chain; 0 pe); `probe-presets` **26/26** byte-identity; `probe-phased-ab` **20/20 (0 diffs)**; `probe-phased-3d` 64/64 · `probe-atmospherics` 18/18 · `probe-weather` 30/30 · `probe-render-richness` 30/30 · `probe-audio-ambience` 22/22 · `probe-flags` 36/36 · `probe-tactical-visuals` 10/10 — all coexist; `bootprobe` realErrors:[]; `t1probe` ok; `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`. VISUALLY CONFIRMED (Lincoln's PD photo framed in the period brass mount, byte-confirmed offline). `wcag-auditor` N/A by construction (no new DOM — only an `<img>` src swap; alt text set by the callers, untouched). **Committed + pushed.**

## ⚡ EARLIER — 2026-06-25 **PHASE H ("make it come to life") · BASE-ENGINE FIX — MULTI-PHASE 3D SCENE REBUILD: the live 3D battlefield now rebuilds when a multi-phase battle advances a phase — the new brigades become visible, the prior phase's meshes are disposed (no orphans, no GPU leak), the woods/walls/objective-beacon move to the new sector, and the camera re-frames it. Fixes the PRE-EXISTING base bug D131's bug-hunt surfaced. Combat byte-identical, proven 3 ways. NEXT in H = H1/H2 PD imagery + footage (SURFACE first — external assets)** (overnight run, D132)

> **The combat-path candidate Aaron chose (AskUserQuestion) over the H1/H2 external-asset layer (deferred — it's the one network/IP-licensing item that needs Aaron present to review each fetched asset, a poor fit for an unattended run).** **The bug (pre-existing, git-traced to predate T10/H1b — D131's bug-hunt SURFACED it):** the 4 multi-phase battles (Antietam · Gettysburg · Chickamauga · Vicksburg — 4 of 9 shipped; recon confirmed each phase declares its OWN distinct sector terrain + objective) advanced their phase in 3D by swapping in a FRESH unit cast + terrain + objective (`_fldAdvancePhase`→`_fldBuildPhase`, T8) but NEVER rebuilt the live 3D scene → new brigades INVISIBLE (no `_u3d` group), the prior phase's unit/officer/supply/arms/eng meshes ORPHANED, and the woods/walls/objective-beacon stranded at the OLD sector. (2D + the headless sim were unaffected → only real 3D play of a multi-phase battle showed it.)
>
> **Confirmed EMPIRICALLY first** (`tools/probe-phased-3d.mjs`, new): run against the pre-fix build it FAILED decisively (Antietam phase-1 `coverage=0` / `orphans=6`; the beacon `dz=65` off the new objective at phase 2) — both symptoms proven before a line changed.
>
> **What shipped:** new `fld3dRebuildPhaseScene()` (`src/tactical/T0-field-sandbox.js`), called from `_fldAdvancePhase` (`src/tactical/T8-phases.js`) ONLY when `__FIELD.mode3d` — re-runs the same per-phase build sequence as `fld3dInit` (terrain: fresh ground/objective-ring+beacon/walls/markers/woods/water + units + officers/supply/arms/flags/eng), then `_fld3dReaimPhase()` re-frames the survey camera onto the new objective (B-6 side-aware). The scene-level per-phase terrain meshes now route through a `_fld3dTrackPhase` tracker into `__FIELD._phaseScene[]`, disposed by `_fld3dDisposePhaseScene()` at the top of `fld3dBuildTerrain`; renderer/camera/lights/scene/groups persist; every unit-scoped builder already self-disposes.
>
> **ARCHITECTURE — combat byte-identical BY CONSTRUCTION (D74):** the headless stepper (renderer 'none' → `mode3d` false) NEVER enters the rebuild → the sim is untouched. No combat/data/save file touched; no `_SAVE_VER`; never `fldRng`. **Proven THREE ways:** `probe-presets` 26/26; a new `tools/probe-phased-ab.mjs` headless A/B (4 battles × 5 seeds, committed pre-fix build vs fixed) = **20 comparisons, 0 diffs** (winner/casualties/phase-score/log/steps identical number-for-number; historically faithful — Antietam CS-tactical, Gettysburg/Vicksburg US, Chickamauga CS); + all 4 per-battle outcome probes green. Diff = `src/tactical/T0-field-sandbox.js` + `src/tactical/T8-phases.js` + `src/tactical/T13-engineering.js` (the bug-hunt fixes) + 2 new probes + the generated HTML.
>
> **Bug-hunt (6 diverse-lens Opus finders → 4 findings, ALL one GPU-leak class the rebuild introduced, ALL FIXED):** **(HIGH + MED, same)** `fld3dBuildEng` (T13) detached its group without disposing the berm/abatis/pontoon geometries+materials → the Vicksburg siege leaked them every advance → FIXED self-disposing (`_fld3dDisposeGroup` first; they're the group's children; does NOT touch water); **(MED)** `fld3dBuildWater` (T13) disposed its prior river only AFTER the no-river early-return → a river→no-river advance leaked it → FIXED by moving the dispose above the return; **(LOW)** the T8 catch fully swallowed exceptions, masking the very invisible-brigade failure → FIXED to `console.warn`. A completeness self-check confirmed EVERY builder the rebuild calls now self-disposes — the leak surface is fully closed. (The hunt's automated verify+critic legs were env-blocked — a hung 6th finder + an OOM on the 8 GB Mac during the focused re-run — so substituted by inline code-verification of all 4 findings + a standalone completeness-critic agent + the empirical re-vet; the D124/D128 precedent.)
>
> **Vet (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN, re-confirmed AFTER the T13 fixes):** `GATE OK`; `probe-phased-3d` **64/64** (all 4 battles, every phase: coverage=1 · 0 orphans · groupsChildren==units · the beacon re-seats on the new objective at every phase · woods rebuilt per sector · non-blank · 0 pe · 0 tex warnings); `probe-presets` **26/26** (byte-identity); `probe-phased-ab` **20/20** (0 diffs → **combat byte-identical**); `probe-atmospherics` 18/18 · `probe-weather` 30/30 · `probe-render-richness` 30/30 · `probe-audio-ambience` 22/22 · `probe-flags` 36/36 · `probe-tactical-visuals` 10/10 (T16–T19 + flags coexist); `bootprobe` realErrors:[]; `t1probe` ok; `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`. ## ⚡ EARLIER — 2026-06-25 **PHASE H ("make it come to life") · H1b — FLAGS & INSIGNIA DEEPENING: every brigade now flies the flag its history records (First National at Bull Run, the ANV Southern Cross mid-war East, the Hardee disc in the West) and the Army-of-the-Potomac corps badges appear as their real Kearny-patch SHAPES (disc/trefoil/diamond/Maltese-cross/Greek-cross) — but ONLY on the 1863 fields where they were actually worn. Deepens the existing `src/tactical/T10-flags.js`. NEXT in H = H1/H2 PD imagery + footage (SURFACE first — external assets)** (overnight run, D131)

> **H1b deepens the flags layer that already existed (`src/tactical/T10-flags.js`; the D91 deepen-not-replace ethic) — and fixes three real defects found on recon:** the 2D path drew blank colour rectangles (it built an SVG + an `Image()` it never drew); corps-badge SHAPES were never rendered (only a text label, with a wrong map — II Corps a circle, V and VI both a generic "cross"); and badges showed on every battle regardless of date (an anachronism on the 1861-62 fields), with a roman-numeral substring bug mis-reading "II Corps" as "I Corps".
>
> **What shipped:** (1) **battle-AWARE flag selection** (`_FLD_BATTLE_META`) — **First National "Stars and Bars"** at First Bull Run (the ANV battle flag did NOT exist on 21 Jul 1861 — the smoke-confusion of the Stars and Bars with the U.S. flag THAT day is what prompted it) and in the early West (Shiloh); the **ANV battle flag (Southern Cross)** mid-war East; the **Hardee pattern** (blue + white disc) for the native Army of Tennessee; with **Longstreet's transferred Eastern brigades (Hood/Kershaw/Law) flying the ANV flag even at Chickamauga**; (2) **new patterns drawn for real** — first-national, hardee, polk (upright St-George cross, distinct from the ANV diagonal saltire), US cavalry guidon; (3) **corrected Kearny-patch corps-badge SHAPES as glyphs** — I=disc, II=trefoil, III=diamond, V=Maltese (a flared cross pattée), VI=Greek cross (distinct from V), XI=crescent, XII=star — division-coloured (1st=red/2nd=white/3rd=blue), with a light parchment **halo** so the shape silhouette reads on any background, and ALWAYS paired with a text label (CVD-safe: shape + label, never colour alone); (4) the **anachronism GATE** — corps badges (Hooker's Circular, 21 Mar 1863) appear ONLY at the Eastern post-Mar-1863 fields (Chancellorsville + Gettysburg) and NEVER on Bull Run / Malvern Hill / Antietam / Fredericksburg (where the corps existed but wore no badge yet — the HUD names the adoption date there as a teaching beat); (5) a **working 2D draw** (cached canvas blit, fog-gated) + a deepened **HUD** (flag thumbnail + badge swatch + a citation-grade provenance caption).
>
> **ARCHITECTURE — combat byte-identical BY CONSTRUCTION (D74):** touches NO combat file. The render fns (`fldDrawFlags`/`fld3dBuildFlags`/`fldFlagHudSelected`) are reached only through the EXISTING T0 `typeof` seams (probes drive the sim headlessly and never call them). Reads sim READ-ONLY (`u.name/side/x/z/facing/alive/state`, `__FIELD.scenario/fog/units`) + `G.settings`; writes NO sim field, NEVER `fldRng`, no save-version bump. The install block wraps ONLY `fld3dBuildUnits`/`fld3dDispose` (texture disposal + a reinforcement re-skin) via a generic `_fldFlagKeep` marker-propagation helper (the D130 lesson). Diff = `src/tactical/T10-flags.js` (deepened) + `tools/probe-flags.mjs` (new) + the generated HTML. No combat/data/save file touched → 9 battles byte-identical seed-for-seed.
>
> **CITATION-GRADE (research+verify Workflow, 25 agents).** The fact-sheet CONFIRMED the core code and added the Chickamauga ANV-transplant split + the Vicksburg Western-flag default, and softened the badge-order citation ("General Orders No. 53" → "Hooker's Circular of 21 March 1863" — the surviving primary doc is a circular; the date is solid). ≥2 reputable sources per flag/badge/date (Encyclopedia Virginia · Smithsonian NMAH · Britannica · confederateflags.org · Tennessee State Museum · crwflags.com FOTW · Washington Times · Gettysburg Flag Works · ABT); anti-Lost-Cause.
>
> **Bug-hunt (6 diverse-lens Opus finders × 3-skeptic default-refute + a completeness critic; 46 agents; critic HIGH) → 1 confirmed + 6 critic areas → 3 in-scope fixes + 1 base bug SURFACED + 2 noted:** **(confirmed MED — a PRE-EXISTING base T0/T8 bug, git-traced to predate T10 → SURFACED, NOT fixed in this presentation-only milestone)** a phased-battle 3D phase-advance (`_fldBuildPhase`/`_fldAdvancePhase` in the combat-path T8) swaps in new unit ids but never rebuilds the `_u3d` 3D groups → new-phase units get no 3D group + the prior groups orphan (H1b only marginally enlarges the orphaned-mesh footprint by adding CanvasTextures). **Recommended dedicated fix:** call `fld3dBuildUnits()`+`fld3dBuildFlags()` from `_fldAdvancePhase` when `mode3d`. **(in-scope fixes, applied + re-vetted)** a reinforcement re-skin (reinforcements now fly textured flags), a badge silhouette halo (a dark-blue badge read poorly on the dark HUD), fog-gate probe coverage (no flag/badge betrays a hidden enemy). **(noted polish)** batteries fly a brigade flag; the US-guidon/CS-cavalry asymmetry.
>
> **Vet (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK`; `probe-flags` **36/36** (battle-aware flags · the date gate · corrected shapes II=trefoil + V/VI distinct · roman parse · division colours · CS-never-badged · the Chickamauga split + Vicksburg flag · CVD-safe HUD + the teaching note · live 3D textured flags + badge meshes at Gettysburg · the Antietam gate builds NO badge mesh · **fog-of-war: no flag/badge betrays a hidden enemy** · byte-identity sim seed + fields invariant across a 200-frame burst · 0 tex warnings · 0 pe); `probe-presets` **26/26** (byte-identity → **combat byte-identical**); `probe-atmospherics` **18/18** · `probe-weather` **30/30** · `probe-render-richness` **30/30** · `probe-audio-ambience` **22/22** (T16/T17/T18/T19 all coexist); `probe-tactical-visuals` **10/10**; `bootprobe` realErrors:[]; `t1probe` 14/14; `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`; `wcag-auditor` AA (no changes). **Committed + pushed.**
>
> **⏳ NEXT — H1/H2 (PD imagery + reenactment footage):** the `tools/build.mjs` tier/compress asset-ingestion pipeline (D71) + linked-assets-with-offline-fallback. **SURFACE for approval BEFORE starting** — it is the one external-asset / network-touching / IP-licensing / single-file-stressing item, not an unattended job; an external-audio-file/linked-asset layer is the same kind of fork → SURFACE first too. Also: a **per-phase weather** candidate (Antietam dawn-fog→mid-morning→afternoon via `_fldPhaseView` in T8 — a combat-path file, so prove byte-identity via probe-presets), and the discovered **phased-3D base bug** (above) as a dedicated base-engine fix. **Q5 Chattanooga + Q6 USCT battles LAST** (Aaron's locked queue). **Resume map:** START-HERE → this block → DECISIONS D131/D130/D91/D74/D54 → V1-CHECKLIST Phase H (H3 i1✅ i2✅ i3✅ · H4✅ · H1b✅) → `src/tactical/T10-flags.js` + `tools/probe-flags.mjs` + `.tmp/phase-h-design.md` (item 6 = H1/H2 imagery).
