## ⚡ EARLIER — 2026-06-25 **PHASE H ("make it come to life") · H4 — BATTLE-AMBIENCE AUDIO DEEPENING: a default-OFF, opt-in, stereo-spatialized procedural battle-soundscape bed — the "one continuous roar" of massed musketry, the ground-shaking bass of a cannonade, and the firing brigades' reports placed in the stereo field where the smoke actually rises — layered UNDER the existing T9 din+SFX, fog-gated so it never betrays a hidden foe. NEXT in H = H1b flags/insignia polish** (overnight run, D130)

> **H4 ships the audio "come-to-life" increment — and gives the battlefield its voice.** New `src/tactical/T19-battle-ambience.js`: a spatialized procedural bed UNDER T9's din+SFX that pairs 1:1 with the T16 gunsmoke (sound comes from where the smoke is) — (1) a continuous **rolling-fire wash** (looping band-passed noise + a slow swell-LFO, gain tracking battle intensity), (2) a **cannonade sub-bass rumble** (gated up by the count of brigades actually working their guns), (3) **located cannon/musket reports** (cannon = crack + body + rolling tail; musket = a volley "rip") panned in stereo by the firing brigade's field-x and **fog-gated** (a hidden foe makes no located sound). DEFAULT OFF; discoverable via a new accessible "Battle ambience" row on T9's audio panel; one-time captioned.
>
> **ARCHITECTURE — combat byte-identical BY CONSTRUCTION (D74):** touches NO combat file. WRAPS `fld3dRender`/`fld2dDraw`/`fldExit`/T9's `_fldAudioPanelRows` BY ASSIGNMENT (a generic `keep()` propagates EVERY prior marker `._atmo`/`._wx`/`._t9audio`/T18's so sibling probes still see the chain). OWNS a separate AudioContext (the AUDIO_SCORE one-context-per-subsystem convention). Reads firing state READ-ONLY (`u._artFlash`/`u.targetId`/`u.ammo`/`u.state`/`u.x`/`u.id`/`u.side`/`u.alive`, `__FIELD.phase/fog/units`); writes NO sim field, NEVER `fldRng`, no `_SAVE_VER` (the `battleAmbience` flag is lazy-defaulted). The scheduling DECISIONS (intensity/fog-gate/pan) record in a `FLDAMB` state object whether or not a real audio device exists → fully testable headless. Diff = `src/tactical/T19-battle-ambience.js` (new) + `src/00-manifest.json` (T19 after T18) + `tools/probe-audio-ambience.mjs` (new) + the generated HTML.
>
> **ACCESSIBILITY / PERF (Intel UHD-617 floor):** **DEFAULT-OFF** (`battleAmbience !== true` → zero sound, never built — the byte-identical-mix default), **fully suppressed under `reduceMotion`** (the calm default), volume scaled by `battleLoud` (off→silent) + master capped low (≤0.20, under the din's 0.26) so it never overwhelms the cues ("never jarring"). Reports defer until the AudioContext is running (no first-gesture stampede); the bed self-silences on the 'over' screen (no idle-graph drain); source-voice pool bounded + all nodes disposed + the context suspended on `fldExit`. CVD-safe; the toggle row reuses T9's AA-vetted markup → `wcag-auditor` N/A by construction.
>
> **Bug-hunt (6 diverse-lens Opus finders × 3-skeptic default-refute verify + a completeness critic; 19 agents; critic confidence HIGH; "NO byte-identity violation, NO fog leak, marker-chain correct") → 1 confirmed + 2 critic edge-bugs + 1 LOW, ALL FIXED:** **(confirmed MED)** the one-time caption flag was burned BEFORE the caption rendered → a user enabling captions mid-battle never saw the cue → consumed only inside the captions-on branch; **(critic MED)** located reports scheduled against a SUSPENDED context (pre-gesture) → a stacked "stampede" on first click → gated on `ctx.state==="running"`; **(critic LOW)** the live graph idled on the 'over' screen → self-silence on `phase==="over"`; **(critic LOW)** StereoPanners diluted the voice cap → a separate panner pool (cap counts voices only). A lifecycle "stale per-phase state" finding was REFUTED 3/3 (singleton `__FIELD`, men-delta clamps, cooldowns expire). Probe strengthened 19→22.
>
> **Vet (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK`; `probe-audio-ambience` **22/22** (opt-in activates + beds built + reports scheduled · pan ∈[-1,1] · separate panner pool · suspended-ctx defers · 'over' self-silence · sim-seed INVARIANT (240 pure ticks) · `errN===0` · accessible row · DEFAULT-OFF silent · reduceMotion suppressed · `battleLoud="off"` silent · fog-gated 5→0 control 3 · `fldExit` disposes); `probe-presets` **26/26** (byte-identity → combat byte-identical); `probe-atmospherics` **18/18** (T16 + marker chain) · `probe-weather` **30/30** (T17) · `probe-render-richness` **30/30** (T18) — all coexist; `probe-tactical-visuals` **10/10** (0 tex warnings); `bootprobe` realErrors:[]; `t1probe` 14/14; `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`. (Audio can't be screenshotted — the probe verifies the WebAudio graph builds + every scheduling decision; the visual-confirmation analog for a sound layer.) **Committed + pushed.**
>
> **⏳ NEXT — H1b (flag/insignia polish, extend `src/tactical/T10-flags.js`):** broader CSA/US flag set + AotP corps badges on the selected-brigade HUD; CVD-safe (shape+label, never flag-colour alone). Then **H1/H2** PD imagery + reenactment footage (the asset-ingestion pipeline — **SURFACE for approval BEFORE starting**; external assets / network / licensing-IP, stresses the single-file deliverable; also note an external-audio-file/linked-asset layer is the same kind of fork → SURFACE first). **Per-phase weather** remains a candidate (Antietam dawn-fog→afternoon via `_fldPhaseView` in T8 — a combat-path file, so prove byte-identity via probe-presets). **Q5 Chattanooga + Q6 USCT battles LAST** (Aaron's locked queue). **Resume map:** START-HERE → this block → DECISIONS D130/D129/D91/D74/D54 → V1-CHECKLIST Phase H (H3 i1✅ i2✅ i3✅ · H4✅) → `src/tactical/T9-audio.js` + `src/tactical/T19-battle-ambience.js` (the audio seam) + `src/tactical/T10-flags.js` (the H1b seam) + `.tmp/phase-h-design.md` (item 5 = H1b).

## ⚡ EARLIER — 2026-06-24 **PHASE H ("make it come to life") · H3-i3 — TERRAIN & UNIT RENDER RICHNESS: the battlefield itself made to feel alive — procedural ground texture (grass / dry-ochre / mud / tilled-furrow patches), woods that sway in the wind, banner cloth that ripples, marching columns that bob, selected brigades that pulse, and a fallen brigade that fades into the ground instead of snapping out; 2D + 3D. NEXT in H = H4 audio deepening** (overnight run, D129)

> **H3-i3 ships the next "come-to-life" increment — the field, the woods, and the men all gain texture + motion.** New `src/tactical/T18-render-richness.js`: (1) **3D ground vertex-colour enrichment** — procedural grass mottle + dry-ochre / damp-lush / bare-earth patches + tilled crop-row corduroy (VERTICES recoloured only, never positions → units still seat on the analytic `fldTerrainH`, never float/sink); (2) **woods wind-sway** (per-cone tilt-about-base on the woods `InstancedMesh`); (3) **banner cloth ripple** (per-unit flag twist/dip, whichever "flag" mesh is present — base or the T10 textured flag); (4) **selection-ring pulse** + a **march bob** for actively-moving brigades; (5) a **casualty fade-out** on death (the brigade settles toward the ground + fades over ~0.85s). Plus 2D-canvas equivalents: a CACHED soft field-grain (dry/lush/earth patches), a pulsing selection halo, the same casualty fade.
>
> **ARCHITECTURE — combat byte-identical BY CONSTRUCTION (D74):** touches NO combat file. WRAPS `fld3dBuildTerrain` (enrich + capture) / `fld3dRender` (frame hook) / `fld3dSyncUnit` (per-unit polish) / `fld2dDraw` (2D) / `fldExit` (dispose) BY ASSIGNMENT (the T9/T16/T17 seam; propagates the `._wx`/`._atmo` markers so the chain stays introspectable). All variation is **analytic value-noise + wall-clock sines — NEVER `fldRng`**; reads ONLY render state + read-only sim fields (`u.x`/`u.z`/`u.alive`/`u.state`/`u.id`, `__FIELD.sel`); writes NO sim field, no `_SAVE_VER`. Proven by `probe-presets` **26/26** byte-identity AND a NEW per-burst sim-field-invariance assertion (x/z/men/morale/facing/formation unchanged across 200 render frames). **Byte-identical default: `renderRich="off"` (or a fresh launch with it set) → un-enriched ground + unswayed woods + flat flags.** Diff = `src/tactical/T18-render-richness.js` (new) + `src/00-manifest.json` (T18 after T17) + `tools/probe-render-richness.mjs` (new) + the generated HTML.
>
> **ACCESSIBILITY / PERF (Intel UHD-617 floor):** every MOTION effect (woods sway · flag ripple · selection pulse · march bob · casualty fade) is suppressed under `reduceMotion` — the STATIC ground texture STAYS (faithful detail, not motion; the T17 precedent). `renderRich="off"` disables the whole layer (motion reverts mid-battle; the 3D ground tint reverts on the next launch — the T16/T17 apply-time pattern, documented inline). Woods sway recomputes each instance matrix from a cached base (no drift); `fldLow()` trims sway amplitude + grain cell size; the **2D field-grain is cached to an offscreen canvas** (built once per field/scale, blitted with ONE `drawImage` — no per-frame fillrate on the weakest 2D-fallback GPU). CVD-safe (no colour-encoded info); no new DOM/text → `wcag-auditor` N/A by construction.
>
> **Bug-hunt (6 diverse-lens Opus finders × 3-skeptic default-refute verify + a completeness critic; 37 agents; critic confidence HIGH) → 1 substantive + 7 real LOWs, ALL FIXED:** **(the one substantive, MED/HIGH across 4 lenses, 3/3)** the casualty-fade `g.position.y -= (1-rec.fade)*5` was CUMULATIVE — the base `fld3dSyncUnit` early-returns for a dead unit and never re-seats its Y, so the per-frame decrement compounded to **~125 yards of sink** (frame-rate dependent), plunging the fading brigade through the terrain → **rewritten ABSOLUTE** off `fldTerrainH`, bounded ~5yd (probe now asserts `maxSink ≤ 8`; measured 4.96). Pure visual — no sim write, D74 intact. **(LOW fixes)** a **fog-ghost guard** (`rec.wasVis` — a fog-hidden enemy that dies no longer pops into view as a fading block); the **selection ring now fades with the corpse**; `_rrSetGroupOpacity` no longer flips the T10 flag's `transparent` flag (alpha-corruption fix); the **2D grain offscreen cache** (perf); `renderRich="off"` **mid-battle motion revert**; the probe **sim-field-invariance** snapshot; a counted + once-warned **`FLDRR_S.errN`** the probe asserts is 0. Probe strengthened **24→30**.
>
> **Vet (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK`; `probe-render-richness` **30/30** (ground enriched [meanAbsDelta 0.0178, 92% verts] + positions untouched · woods sway/reset · flag ripple/reset · selection pulse · casualty fade visible+opacity-in-(0,1)+**BOUNDED-sink**+**fog-ghost-guarded**+hidden-when-done · sim seed + **sim-field INVARIANT** · `errN===0` · off/reduceMotion correct · 2D paints); `probe-presets` **26/26** (byte-identity → combat byte-identical, re-confirmed post-fix); `probe-atmospherics` **18/18** (T16 coexists); `probe-weather` **30/30** (T17 coexists); `probe-tactical-visuals` **10/10** (0 tex warnings); `bootprobe` realErrors:[]; `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`; `t1probe` 0 fails. VISUALLY CONFIRMED. **Committed + pushed.**
>
> **⏳ NEXT — H4 (audio deepening, per D91/`.tmp/phase-h-design.md` item 4):** layer a battle-intensity-driven musketry/cannon ambience bed under the existing `src/tactical/T9-audio.js` SFX (default-off, captioned, accessible) — pairs with the T16 gunsmoke. Then **H1b** flag/insignia polish (extend `T10-flags.js`) → **H1/H2** PD imagery + reenactment footage (the asset-ingestion pipeline — **SURFACE for approval BEFORE starting**; external assets / network / licensing-IP, stresses the single-file deliverable). **Q5 Chattanooga + Q6 USCT battles LAST** (Aaron's locked queue). **Per-phase weather** also remains a candidate H3 add (Antietam dawn-fog → mid-morning → afternoon via `_fldPhaseView` in T8 — a combat-path file, so prove byte-identity). **Resume map:** START-HERE → this block → DECISIONS D129/D128/D127/D74/D54 → V1-CHECKLIST Phase H (H3 i1✅ i2✅ i3✅) → `src/tactical/T9-audio.js` (the H4 seam) + `.tmp/phase-h-design.md` + (for reference) the T16/T17/T18 wrap-by-assignment pattern.

## ⚡ EARLIER — 2026-06-24 **PHASE H ("make it come to life") · H3-i2 — WEATHER + TIME-OF-DAY ATMOSPHERE: procedural sky/light re-tint (background · fog · sun · hemisphere) + rain/snow precipitation per a citation-grade per-battle `weather` hint; 2D + 3D. The marquee visible win is Antietam's dawn Cornfield ground-fog. NEXT in H3 = i3 terrain/unit render richness** (overnight run, D128)

> **H3-i2 ships the next "come-to-life" increment — and gives every battle a sky matched to its history.** New `src/tactical/T17-weather.js` re-tints the 3D `scene.background`/`fog`/sun/hemisphere AND washes the 2D canvas to a resolved `{sky, time}` palette (sky ∈ clear/overcast/rain/fog/haze/snow; time ∈ dawn/morning/midday/afternoon/dusk), plus a procedural rain/snow `THREE.Points` cloud (the T16 shader pattern) + a 2D precip hatch, and a one-time accessible weather note via the existing role=status `fldScenarioBanner`. **The marquee: Antietam's pale warm dawn Cornfield ground-fog** (distant brigades fading into the mist, the objective still readable) — the one genuinely non-clear sky; the rest carry faithful time-of-day light (warm afternoons, cool mornings).
>
> **CITATION-GRADE DATA (research+verify Workflow, 18 agents).** The verifiers REFUSED to fabricate drama — Gettysburg `haze→clear` (Rev. Jacobs' instrument log: bright at the 2pm Pickett's-Charge window), Fredericksburg `fog→clear/afternoon` (the Rappahannock fog lifted ~10am, before the noon-to-dark assault — the note still teaches it), Shiloh `clear/morning` (the rain was OVERNIGHT), a misattributed Vicksburg source removed. **The honest finding: the "fog of war" at most of these battles was POWDER-SMOKE (already shipped via T16), not weather** — only Antietam's dawn fog is a real non-clear sky. Final hints (in `data/*.json`): bullrun1 clear/afternoon · malvernHill clear/afternoon · **antietam fog/dawn** · fredericksburg clear/afternoon · chancellorsville clear/morning · gettysburg clear/afternoon · shiloh clear/morning · vicksburg clear/midday(no-op) · chickamauga clear/afternoon.
>
> **ARCHITECTURE — combat byte-identical BY CONSTRUCTION (D74):** touches NO combat file. WRAPS `fld3dInit`/`fld3dRender`/`fld2dDraw`/`fldExit` by ASSIGNMENT (the T9/T16 seam; propagates T16's `._atmo` marker so the chain stays introspectable). Reads ONLY the scenario's `weather` hint (`_scenTop.weather` multi-phase, else `scenData.weather`) + render state; OWN LCG — **never `fldRng`** (sim seed probe-proven unchanged across the wx render loop); no sim write, no `_SAVE_VER`. **THE BYTE-IDENTICAL DEFAULT: a missing hint — or `clear`/`midday` — resolves to `null` and applies ZERO change** (every shipped look preserved). Diff = `src/tactical/T17-weather.js` (new) + `src/00-manifest.json` (T17 after T16) + 9 `data/*.json` weather blocks + `tools/probe-weather.mjs` (new) + the generated HTML.
>
> **ACCESSIBILITY / PERF (Intel UHD-617 floor):** the static tint is motion-free → applies under `reduceMotion`; only PRECIPITATION (motion) is suppressed under it (probe-verified: tint kept, no `wxPrecip`); `G.settings.weather==="off"` disables the whole layer. Fog far-plane held **≥1500** in every palette so the objective never vanishes. One precip draw call + `fldLow()` budget (520/200) + a `gl_PointSize` driver clamp. CVD-safe; no new DOM/text (the note reuses the AA-vetted banner) → `wcag-auditor` N/A.
>

>

Neutral grayscale cue (CVD-safe); no new DOM/text → `wcag-auditor` N/A by construction.

## ⚡ EARLIER — 2026-06-24 **E3-i2 THE DEDICATED FULL WCAG 2.2 AA AUDIT SWEEP — the exhaustive per-surface contrast/ARIA/focus/keyboard pass + root-cause fixes across every shipped surface (desk tabs · menus · battle HUD · overlays · tactical 2D/3D readouts). This COMPLETES E3 / S4 accessibility (i1+i2); NEXT = Phase F / I / H / J** (overnight run, D126)

> **The full AA sweep ships — and E3 closes.** i1 added the modes + the var-driven core; i2 is the exhaustive inline-hex/ARIA/focus sweep i1 deliberately deferred. **PURE presentation/markup — byte-identical combat BY CONSTRUCTION (D74)** (`probe-presets` 9-baseline byte-identity GREEN; no combat/RNG/save/`_SAVE_VER` touched). Diff = 29 `src/` files + `tools/probe-accessibility.mjs`.
>
> **KEYSTONE — two TEXT-ONLY token redefines.** Both `--rule #8a7350` (3.5–4.4:1 as the standard secondary-label, used ~40× across FROZEN base.html classes + module-inline `color:var(--rule)` headings) AND `--blood-lt #a83d33` (2.5–3.0:1 — the FIRE order button `.obtn.fire`, `#log .e.hit`, `.tagn`, `.verdict.lose`) are text-only in base.html, so an **always-on injected `:root{--rule:#a89066;--blood-lt:#d8745c}`** (in `src/97-accessibility.js` `_a11yCss`, applied at boot) fixes EVERY text use at once — sidestepping the inline-specificity no class override can beat, and fixing frozen base.html classes without editing base.html (`#a89066` = the exact tone base.html already uses for the same fix in its loot/readability cards). **LIGHT-MENU SAFE (verified):** `.gn-paper` defines its own `.gn-col-head{#5c3c18}`/masthead classes and references NEITHER token; HC mode re-redefines both at higher specificity so it still wins. Plus: a **universal `:focus-visible` fallback ring**, a **`.upg[aria-pressed=true]:focus-visible` !important override** (the inline pressed outline hid the focus delta), the base **`#toast` made a polite `role=status` region** (4.1.3), and **HC per-surface extensions** reaching `#wdContent`/`#wdTabs`/`#fldHud`/`#fldBar`/`#fldRoot`/sheet dialogs/control borders (dark-scoped → light menu untouched).
>
> **~50 module-inline colour fixes** (canonical status palette: green `#4a6b3a`→`#639452`/`#6a9a58`/`#6f9e5a`; red `#9c3b2e`→`#d07060`/`#d06862`/`#d8745c`/`#da6a5a`; fills→3:1 tones; rarity `#7a5cff`→`#9a86f0`/`#8a8276`→`#9a9184`) + **~16 ARIA/focus/keyboard fixes** (aria-pressed on the desk-tab toolbar [chosen over a 18-tab role=tablist to avoid an unimplemented roving-tabindex expectation] + lever/wildcard/prep-order/blockade toggles; aria-expanded+controls on every disclosure; the tutorial `#tutTitle` focus ring; the help "?" aria-label + the tactical-help modal background-inert + focus-return-to-trigger; the T0/T6 speed buttons value-in-name; the T0 2D unit side-letter glyph dark stroke-halo → ~15.7:1; a 2.5.8 target-size bump on the save-slot buttons). Authored by me + 4 disjoint-file Sonnet editors.
>
> **A real CSS group-opacity compositing bug** (a child `opacity:1` cannot escape a parent row's `opacity:.85`) was caught by self-review + the bug-hunt and fixed PROPERLY by moving the dimming to the LABEL span only in `_brgBar` (85) / `_morMeter` (33) / blockade `meter()` (60); the disabled weapon/artillery cards dim an inner wrapper, the unavailable REASON rendering as a full-opacity sibling.
>
> **Layered adversarial verification — each layer caught deeper misses:** a 28-agent audit Workflow (13 file-batch finders × default-refute verify × synthesis × critic) found **127 violations**; an 11-agent bug-hunt (6 diverse-lens finders × default-refute × completeness critic) found **3 confirmed + a HIGH critic gap** (the un-propagated opacity fix, 2 unlifted blockade greens, an `aria-hidden`-on-the-focused-trigger in the help modal, a vacuous probe step); a **report-only `wcag-auditor` confirm + two whole-`src/` contrast scans caught 7 MORE DOM-text misses** (`_cmdLeadWord` rating words, `_armRarityCol`/`_artRar` rarity labels, the T11 `.fld-cb-status.bad` error border, command's `#d66040` 3.92:1 on the lighter `#2e2816` card overlay) → ALL fixed + re-probed. Canvas/3D mesh/flag fills + decorative aria-hidden triple-encoded grade dots (D96) correctly held EXEMPT (DOM-AA scope).
>
> **`probe-accessibility` strengthened 17→25 steps** (the token-redefine cascade computed on `<html>` + reaching FROZEN base classes; the always-on + HC rules present; the toast live region; a numeric canonical-palette battery; a **RENDER-SAMPLE that instantiates `_brgBar`+`_morMeter` and reads each value span's EFFECTIVE composited opacity + ratio**; the HC desk/HUD black-ground lift; the HC-must-not-touch-`.gn-paper` guard). **Vet (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK`; `probe-accessibility` **25/25** 0 pe; `probe-presets` **26/26** (byte-identity → combat byte-identical); `bootprobe` realErrors:[]; `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`; `t1probe` 14/14; ~30 UI/teaching/command/tactical probes PASS, 0 pe. **Committed + pushed.**
>
> **⏳ NEXT — Phase F / I / H / J (E is now COMPLETE: E1✅ E2✅ E3✅ E4✅).** Pick the next pillar: **F** content systems / **I** loot + the Soldier's Story (D93 prosopography — the EA-style play-as-anyone career mode), **H** the graphics/footage/audio "come-to-life" pass, **J** polish/meta. The symmetric AI-GM is the [hard] §12 capstone (surface only if it forks). **Q5 Chattanooga + Q6 USCT battles LAST** (Aaron's locked queue). **Resume map:** START-HERE → this block → DECISIONS D126/D125/D124/D74/D54 → V1-CHECKLIST (Phase E ALL ✅; pick F/I/H/J) → the relevant `src/*.js` + `tools/probe-*.mjs` for the chosen pillar. The accessibility seam is mature: `src/97-accessibility.js` (`_a11yCss` always-on AA block + HC extensions) + `tools/probe-accessibility.mjs` (25 steps incl. the per-surface AA assertions) — extend these as new UI lands.

## ⚡ EARLIER — 2026-06-24 **E2-i4 PLAY-STYLE PRESETS + the D39 HISTORIAN SETTINGS LAYER: a "Play Your Way" hub — President (owner-mode focus) / General-Commander (battle focus) / Free Command, plus the teaching/realism toggles — surfaced as a President's-Desk tab AND a main-menu overlay. E2 (S4 education) continues; NEXT = E2-i5 difficulty/realism sliders** (overnight run, D123)

> **The play-emphasis + teaching-settings layer ships.** New `src/95-playstyle.js` — a **"Play Your Way"** hub: (1) **play-style presets** — **President** (owner-mode focus) / **General-Commander** (battle focus) / **Free Command** (the shipped neutral); (2) the **D39 Historian teaching layer** — the alt-history emergent-only toggle + a read-only Command-&-Realism read-out + hand-offs to the existing pickers. ONE panel body (`_psPanelHTML(ctx)`) on TWO surfaces: an **18th President's-Desk tab** ("Play Style", inline like Codex, ctx="desk") AND a **main-menu overlay** ("⚙ PLAY YOUR WAY", `openSheet`, ctx="menu"). Period-broadsheet styled, CVD-safe, full keyboard/ARIA, reduce-motion-safe.
>
> **D39.1 FAITHFUL — play-style is an EMPHASIS, never a hard gate** (both layers always available — the locked charter). Its genuine pure-UI consumer: `psDefaultDeskTab` sets which Desk tab the desk first lands on (Commander→Command; President/Free→the War Effort overview) — **unset/unknown → "economy" → byte-identical** for existing players (deskTabs whitelist). `openWarDept`'s `_wdTab="economy"` became `_wdTab=psDefaultDeskTab()`.
>
> **ARCHITECTURE — PURE SETTINGS LAYER; byte-identical combat BY CONSTRUCTION:** writes ONLY `G.settings.playStyle` (new) + `altHistoryEmergentOnly` (a SECOND surface for the divergence-tab flag) + a private `cw_playstyle` localStorage bundle (defaults-only boot, the T6 pattern). NO combat knob (the tactical levers stay owned by T6 — `fldPresetResolve` read-only; "Adjust the battlefield" hands off to `fldPresetMenu`; motion/CVD/sound hand off to the frozen `openSettings`). NO RNG, NO `_SAVE_VER` bump. **No tactical file in the diff; no combat/tick/resolve/bridge path references `ps*`** (grep + probe static-scan over `src/tactical/*.js` + 85/86/87/80) → 9 battles byte-identical. ## ⚡ EARLIER — 2026-06-23 **E4-i2 THE STRATEGIC WAR-END: a reached victoryReady (a negotiated peace / a recognized independence) now CONCLUDES the war through the graded after-action report — for a victory that did NOT complete the battle chain. Phase E's strategic arc (E1 alt-history + E4 victory) is now COMPLETE; NEXT = E2 education** (overnight run, D119)

> **The strategic arc CLOSES.** Before D119 the war ended only at battle-chain completion (`campaignAdvance` → `warWonScreen` at `idx>=chainLen`). Now a reached **`C.strategy.victoryReady`** — a **negotiated peace** (`enemyWill≤30` in 1864+ / `≤18` any year) or a **recognized independence** (recognition ≥60, CS) — can CONCLUDE the war through the SAME D112 graded report, surfaced as a **NON-FORCED OFFER** on the between-battles interstitial ("The war can be concluded — accept the peace / claim independence", or fight on). Built via the authorized `warWonScreen` override pattern (the frozen-loop override; D116 authorized this autonomously).
>
> **ARCHITECTURE — additive OFFER + the existing warWonScreen; byte-identical combat BY CONSTRUCTION:** new pure helpers in `src/82-after-action.js` — `aarStrategicEndAvailable(C)` (`"will"` either side / `"recognition"` **CS-ONLY** / null), `_aarEndFraming(C,reason)` (side-aware title/subtitle/verdict/offer copy), `aarStrategicEndOffer(C)`, `aarConcludeWar(reason)`. `warWonScreen` frames via `_aarEndFraming`; at chain completion (`_aarEndReason` null) it is the **byte-identical D112 "The War is Won / Victory!"** screen (base Main-Menu wiring + `G.campaign=null` verbatim). The offer is one guarded IIFE in `_pdInterstitialHTML` (`src/20-president-render.js`; returns `''` → byte-identical interstitial when `victoryReady` null), wired (`#pdConcludeWar`) in `_pdShowTurnInterstitial` (`src/30-president-shell.js`). **No tactical file touched; no combat/tick/resolve/bridge path reads `aarStrategicEnd*`/`_aarEndReason`/`aarConcludeWar`** (grep-confirmed) → 9 battles byte-identical by construction. No new save state; NO `_SAVE_VER` bump; NO RNG.
>
> **SIDE-CORRECT (anti-Lost-Cause):** `"recognition"` gated CS-only (the Union never wins by the Confederacy gaining recognition); `"will"` side-aware (US: "the rebellion lays down its arms"; CS: "Washington consents to let the South go"); a CS strategic victory framed honestly as the survival of a slaveholding republic, never valorized (the graded report body carries the Reconstruction accounting, keyed on `victoryReady`).
>
> **Bug-hunt (5 diverse-lens Opus finders × default-refute Opus verify + a completeness critic; 10 agents) → invariantsHold=TRUE; 1 confirmed LOW + 2 critic gaps (1 MED + 1 LOW), ALL FIXED + probe-locked:** **(MED)** the pre-existing `vicOnResolve` "will" toast hardcoded "Northern resolve is breaking" for BOTH sides — wrong for a US player; D119 makes it a live cue → side-aware. **(LOW)** the strategic-conclude exit left `_pdTurnAck=true` (skipping the next campaign's first interstitial) → reset at the `warWonScreen` chokepoint. **(LOW)** `warWonScreen`'s `!C` early-return now clears `_aarEndReason`.
>
> **Vet (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓`; `probe-afteraction` **15/15** (+5 D119 steps), **0 pageerrors**; no-regression GREEN — `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`, `probe-desk` 13/13, `probe-divergence` 14/14, `probe-victory` 12/12, `bootprobe` ok, `t1probe` 14/14 (all 0 pe). No tactical file in the diff → 9 battles byte-identical by construction. `wcag-auditor` AA (offer banner #cbb27a 7.81:1 / body 8.94:1 / button 8.54:1 — clean, no edits).
>
> **OPERATING MODEL (locked, D116 [AARON] popup) — still in force:** EVEN FEWER interruptions; stop ONLY for an irreversible/money fork; LOG a contradiction and proceed. FULL 9-agent bug-hunt every milestone. Frozen-loop overrides proceed autonomously. Phase E in the locked order.
>
> **⏳ NEXT — E2 (the locked Phase-E order; E1 alt-history + E4 victory now COMPLETE):** **E2 — S4 education:** a multi-axis **codex/glossary** (the war's people, units, terms, systems) + an **inline glossary** (hover/tap definitions) + the full **guided tutorial** + **play-style presets** (President / General-Commander + the D39 Historian settings overlay) + **difficulty/realism sliders** (pairs with the B-5 effectiveness/realism sliders — memory `civilwar-directive-tactical-engineering-sliders`). E2 is LARGE — build it incrementally (one sub-system per gated milestone), likely a citation-grade content workflow for the codex entries (anti-Lost-Cause, ≥2 sources). Then **E3** — the dedicated FULL WCAG 2.2 AA pass + the 4 a11y modes (high-contrast, CVD-safe, SR turn/battle narration, dyslexia font). Then Phase F (content) / I (loot + Soldier's Story) / H (graphics/footage/audio) / J (polish). The symmetric AI-GM is the [hard] §12 capstone (surface only if it forks). **Q5 Chattanooga + Q6 USCT battles LAST** (Aaron's locked queue). **Resume map:** START-HERE → this block → DECISIONS D119/D118/D117/D116/D114/D91/D54 → V1-CHECKLIST Phase E (E1✅ E4✅; E2 next) → for E2: `src/30-president-shell.js` (the desk tabs + `_wdRefresh` dispatch — a codex tab slots here) + `src/92-help-overlay.js` (`_hpShowWelcome`/`_hpShowHelp`/`_hpShowTacticalHelp` — the tutorial/help spine) + `tactical/T6-presets.js` (`fldPresetMenu` — the existing tactical presets/sliders to extend) + `data/` (a new `codex.json` if the codex is data-driven, mirroring `decisions.json`).

## ⚡ EARLIER — 2026-06-23 **E1 INTERACTIVE HINGE-FORK DECISION CARDS: 4 citation-grade strategic forks at the war's turning points (the hard hand of war · the terms of reunion · the King Cotton gambit · peace or independence) whose choices STEER the war toward the now-complete alternate-ending spectrum — E1 (alt-history) is now substantially COMPLETE** (overnight run, D118)

> **The E1 "Remaining" item ships:** interactive **HINGE-FORK decision cards on the proven `decResolve`/`_decApply` engine** (the prompt's spec: *data/decisions.json, pure data → byte-identical by construction*). 4 new citation-grade strategic hinge cards (2 US, 2 CS — both-sides balance) in `data/decisions.json`, each a genuine fork at a war turning-point whose choices shape the war's TRAJECTORY toward the D114-D117 ending spectrum: **us-hard-war** ("The Hard Hand of War", 1864 — total war / conciliation / Grant's grind → the *hard-war collapse* road); **us-reconstruction-terms** ("The Terms of Reunion", 1864 — Ten Percent Plan / Wade-Davis / forty acres → *a Reconstruction that holds* / *forty acres enforced*); **cs-king-cotton** ("The King Cotton Gambit", 1861-62 — embargo / export / burn → *recognized independence* / *King Cotton*); **cs-peace-feelers** ("Peace or Independence", 1864 — negotiate / fight-on / husband → *a negotiated peace* / *Fabian survival*). All `trigger.hinge:true` (always surface, "⚐ a hinge of the war" badge), 3 options each, the full rich `card` multi-voice block + a **named-and-countered Lost-Cause MYTH voice**. The deck grows 8 → 12 (it had only ONE strategic hinge before — emancipation).
>
> **SCOPE (decide-&-log) — PURE DATA, no engine extension:** the ending-OPENER strategy levers (`armEnslaved`/`amnesty`/`enemyWill`/`recognition`) are combat-adjacent (feed manpower→bridge / auto-resolve) and already live on the Paths-to-Victory surface — writing them from `_decApply` would be a combat-adjacent engine change, contradicting the prompt's explicit "pure data." So the cards use ONLY the existing `_decApply` vocabulary (`weariness`/`capital`/`intervention`/`importFactor`/`strength` + `sets.emancipation`), which genuinely moves **momentum** (the dominant ending GATE — `vicMomentum` reads weariness+capital) + the foreign/blockade/manpower trajectory, and each option's prose NAMES the ending it steers toward. The wild/lever/recognition openers stay on the Paths-to-Victory surface (by design); decisions are the complementary narrative fork-surface. Only src touch = a 1-line `_decCAT_LABEL` extension (byte-identical for the 8 existing cards via the raw-key fallback; plain `&` in "Peace & Terms" avoids the D115 double-escape).

## ⚡ EARLIER — 2026-06-23 **§12.3 THE ELECTION-SUPPORT RELIEF-BIND: a commissioned UNION political general (Banks/Butler/Sigel/McClernand) costs extra political capital to relieve before the 1864 vote — the bind Lincoln lived — relaxing once the verdict is in** (overnight run, D113)

> **The GM §12.3 command-politics keystone ships** (the bind the Commission move D109 was built around, now unblocked by the 1864-election state E4/D112). A commissioned POLITICAL general delivers his constituency's votes while he serves, so relieving him before the 1864 election forfeits that support: an ADDITIVE surcharge on his relief cost (`_cmdReliefCost`) that rises year over year toward the vote (1862<1863<1864) and FALLS to zero the moment `clock.resolved1864` is set. `surcharge = round((politicalValue/100)·windowByYear[year]·surchargeMax=14)`, bounded. A teaching tell on the active-general card names the constituency + the `+N capital` cost. Teaches §12.3 concretely: a fielded Banks both fields a low commandLeadership (49 vs Grant 87, D109) AND can't be cheaply dumped before November 1864. **Picked for an autonomous tick** over the frozen-loop E4-i2 (contained command-economy, no frozen-loop, no design fork); Aaron then reordered the queue — **E1 hinge-forks next, fantastical tier first.**
>
> **ARCHITECTURE — UNION-ONLY, byte-identical until you field a political general (D74 no-fudge):** new pure helpers in `src/35-command.js` (`_cmdElectionBindCfg`/`_cmdPoliticalValue`/`_cmdElectionWindow`/`_cmdElectionSupportSurcharge`); `_cmdReliefCost` adds the surcharge. It is read ONLY by `cmdAppoint` (relief economy) + `_cmdActiveCard` (display) — **NEVER by `commandLeadership` or any combat/bridge/tick path** (grep-confirmed) → the bridge + 9 battles byte-identical by construction. The surcharge is EXACTLY 0 for every starting-roster general, the entire CS side, and outside the window. Data: `ratings.json` `electionReliefBind` (Inferred + 2 sources). No new save state, no `_SAVE_VER` bump, NO RNG.
>
> **Bug-hunt (5 diverse-lens Opus finders × default-refute verify + critic; 10 agents) → 3 confirmed, ALL FIXED:** **(MED, anti-Lost-Cause)** the bind + the "Lincoln lived this bind" tell had no side gate → a CS player fielding Floyd/Pillow got a fabricated Lincoln attribution (the CSA held no 1864 presidential election) → **gated Union-only** (`if (!C||C.side==="CS") return 0;`), CS now fully byte-identical (probe-locked). **(MED)** a tampered NaN `windowByYear` escaped the clamp (`typeof NaN==="number"`) → could poison `clock.capital` → restored the `isFinite` guard + hardened the surcharge guard (probe-locked; defensive — config is build-frozen). **(LOW)** "rises toward November 1864" prose overstated the year-granular window → softened.
>
> **Vet (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓`; `probe-command` **76/76** (+7 D113 steps incl. the Union-only gate + the NaN safety), **0 pageerrors**; `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`; `probe-desk` 13/13, `t1probe` 14/14 — 0 pe. No combat path reads the relief cost → 9 battles byte-identical by construction. `wcag-auditor` AA (`#cbb27a` tell 8.41:1, no change).
>
> **NEXT (Aaron-reordered queue):** **(1) E1 deepening** — curated alt-history HINGE-FORK cards on the proven `decResolve`/`_decApply` engine (`data/decisions.json`, pure data, byte-identical by construction), **fantastical tier FIRST** (Aaron's pick), against the **LOCKED 24-END-STATE alt-history catalog** Aaron just elicited (12 CSA + 12 Union end-states + 7 reward axes + a labeled plausible→fantastical spectrum — see the AskUserQuestion rounds; record as D114 when E1 builds). **(2) E4-i2** — wire `victoryReady` (negotiated peace/recognition) to an actual war-END (frozen campaign loop). **(3) E2** codex/tutorial/presets. **(4) E3** the full WCAG-AA pass. The symmetric AI-GM ([hard]) remains the §12 capstone; **Q5 Chattanooga + Q6 USCT LAST**. **Resume map:** START-HERE → this block → DECISIONS D113/D112/D111/D91/D54 → V1-CHECKLIST Phase E → `src/35-command.js` (the "§12.3 (D113)" block: `_cmdElectionSupportSurcharge`/`_cmdElectionWindow`/`_cmdReliefCost` + the `electionTell`) + `data/ratings.json` (`electionReliefBind`) + `src/32-decisions.js` (`decResolve`/`_decApply` — the E1 hinge-fork engine) + `data/decisions.json` (the E1 cards) + `tools/probe-command.mjs` (the D113 steps). **Honor the context-budget rule (≤25% → stop + hand off).**

## ⚡ EARLIER — 2026-06-23 **E4 / S5 VICTORY: THE GRADED AFTER-ACTION REPORT — an A–F report card on the whole war (battlefield · treasury · diplomacy · the home front + the 1864 election · the war's purpose · high command), the divergence ledger read back, the human cost vs the real toll, and a Reconstruction coda** (overnight run, D112)

> **The strategic arc's closing teaching payoff ships.** With E1's divergence ledger live (D111), I advanced to **E4 / S5 victory** at the named next item — **the rich graded after-action report** (D31.6). A new module `src/82-after-action.js` renders a period **"report card"** on the whole war: an **A–F letter grade** (the D94 scale — neutral 64 = C, A+ ≥90 "Legendary", CVD triple-encoded letter + word + colour) for each domain the President actually ran — **The Battlefield** (win-rate + the casualty exchange), **The Treasury** (the price level vs the 1861 dollar), **Diplomacy & the Blockade** (the recognition contest, side-aware), **The Home Front** (public will + the 1864-election verdict), **the war's PURPOSE** (US = emancipation, graded by *timing*: declined < late < 1863; CS = an honest, **NON-graded** "Confederate Cause" panel — slavery stated plainly in the founders' own words, never valorized, excluded from the GPA), and **High Command** (`commandLeadership`). Plus a headline **overall grade**, a **read-back of the D111 divergence ledger** (`divScan`/`divIndex` — the index + the top entries), the **human cost** beside the war's true ~750,000 toll (Hacker 2011), and a forward-looking **RECONSTRUCTION coda** keyed to the emancipation choice + the war's trajectory (Foner). **Pick rationale (decide-&-log):** chose E4 over the symmetric AI-GM ([hard]) and deeper E1 forks because it is the arc's payoff, it *summarizes the D111 ledger* (D111's logged foundation), and it is a low-risk pure read-out + one authorized frozen override. NOT a new fork.
>
> **ARCHITECTURE — a PURE READ-OUT + one authorized frozen override (byte-identical combat BY CONSTRUCTION):** `src/82-after-action.js` (`aarGrade`/`_aarDomains`/`aarOverall`/`_aarReconstruction`/`aarRenderReport`/`aarRenderTab` + the `warWonScreen` OVERRIDE) READS already-resolved aggregate state (`C.stats`/`C.economy`/`C.blockade`/`C.morale`/`C.clock`/`C.strategy`/`C.president.emancipation` + `commandLeadership`/`moraleCompute`) and WRITES NOTHING to the sim (the only write anywhere is the base-preserved `G.campaign = null` in the override). **No tick, no own state; NO tactical file touched; not in any `_t1Resolve`/`_t1InitAll`; `aar*` grep-confirmed in EXACTLY 3 files (the module, the shell tab dispatch, the probe) with ZERO combat/resolve/tick hits → the combat layer + all 9 battles are byte-identical by construction.** Surfaced TWO ways: a live **"After-Action"** desk tab (the **16th**, pure read-out) wired in `src/30-president-shell.js`, AND the **war-end FINAL report** — `warWonScreen()` is overridden (the `openWarDept`/`openUpgrade` frozen-override pattern, D30.2) to show the graded report + the Reconstruction coda instead of the bare base dispatch, reproducing the base's Main-Menu wiring + nullify verbatim. Manifest: module + `warWonScreen` override (collision 2× ✓). Citation-grade anti-Lost-Cause inline prose (Hacker, Foner, McPherson, Howard Jones, Dew — Stephens's Cornerstone Speech; failures named on both sides).
>
> **Bug-hunt (5 diverse-lens Opus finders × default-refute verify + a completeness critic; 7 agents) → critic invariantsHold = TRUE** (no-fudge + byte-identity + citations all independently confirmed). **1 confirmed LOW + 2 critic-additional LOW — ALL ONE ROOT CAUSE, FIXED + probe-locked:** the home-front domain read `moraleCompute().public` behind a `typeof`-only guard, but `typeof NaN === "number"` (and `_morClamp` is NaN-capable), so a non-finite public-will could (in an out-of-contract partial-save) leak "NaN of 100", desync the graded-domain count vs the GPA divisor, and mis-render the graded row as the NON-graded "honest accounting" panel → FIXED with an `isFinite` guard at the read (the D52.6 finite discipline) + the panel count hardened to match `aarOverall`; a new probe step stubs `moraleCompute → NaN` and locks it. The critic's one citation note (Gallagher's *Confederate War* is a popular-will thesis, off-target for "slavery was the cause") was tightened to McPherson alongside the primary Dew cite.
>
> **Vet (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓`; `probe-afteraction` **10/10** (+ the NaN regression step), **0 pageerrors**; full no-regression GREEN — `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`, `bootprobe` ok, `t1probe` 14/14, `probe-desk` 13/13, `probe-divergence` 14/14, `probe-victory` 12/12, `probe-command` 69/69, `probe-officers` 15/15 — all 0 pageerrors. **No tactical file in the diff** → the 9 battles byte-identical by construction (`probe-ratings`/`probe-camp` were the documented THREE-CDN `load`-stall, share no code path, GREEN at D111). `wcag-auditor` AA (1 contrast fix `#937b55`→`#9b8560`; all grade chips 5.67–9.62:1; CVD triple-encode confirmed).

Then **E1 deepening** (curated `trigger.hinge` alt-history fork cards in `data/decisions.json` on the proven `decResolve`/`_decApply` engine — no engine change), **E2** (S4 codex/glossary/tutorial/presets), **E3** (the dedicated FULL WCAG-AA pass). The symmetric AI-GM ([hard]) remains the §12 capstone; **Q5 Chattanooga + Q6 USCT LAST** (Aaron's queue). **Resume map:** START-HERE → this block → DECISIONS D112/D111/D91(roadmap)/D54(no-thumb) → V1-CHECKLIST Phase E → `src/82-after-action.js` (`aarGrade`/`_aarDomains`/`aarOverall`/`_aarReconstruction`/`aarRenderReport` + the `warWonScreen` override) + `src/81-divergence.js` (`divScan`/`divIndex` — read back) + `src/33-morale.js` (`moraleCompute` + the 1864 election state) + `src/35-command.js` (`commandLeadership`/`_cmdReliefCost` — the relief-bind hook) + `src/30-president-shell.js` (the "afteraction" tab) + `tools/probe-afteraction.mjs`. **Honor the context-budget rule (≤25% → stop + hand off).**

## ⚡ EARLIER — 2026-06-23 **E1 / S3 ALT-HISTORY: "YOUR WAR vs HISTORY" — a living divergence ledger of every point your war has left the documented record, with the history each entry departs from + an emergent-only toggle** (overnight run, D111)

> **The Phase-E strategic arc opens.** The GM §12 depth-chart arc is COMPLETE (Appoint/Relieve · Promote · Corps · Commission · Division). Per the locked roadmap (D91: after C breadth → **E strategic S3–S5**; Chattanooga + USCT are Aaron's-queue-LAST), I pivoted to **E1 / S3 alt-history** and shipped its named flagship first increment: a new **"Your War vs History"** President's-Desk tab (the 15th) — a living, dated **DIVERGENCE LEDGER** that records every point at which the player's war has left the historical record. Each entry carries a **category** (Emancipation / Foreign / Northern politics / The war's trajectory / Wild cards), a **tier** (minor △ / major ◆ / radical ★ — distance from the record, CVD-safe glyph+word+colour), a short **when**, and the **historical counterfactual** ("In history, …") that is the lesson. A headline **DIVERGENCE INDEX** (0..100, a saturating tier-weight sum + a 5-step word ladder) measures how far the war has strayed. Plus the **emergent-only toggle** (`G.settings.altHistoryEmergentOnly`): ON withholds the explicit ahistorical WILD-CARD gambits so the war diverges ONLY through play. **Pick rationale (decide-&-log):** chose E1's tracker over (A) the symmetric AI-GM ([hard], the §12 capstone) and (B) the §12.3 election-support relief-bind — it's lower-risk (a pure read-out), it's the named E1 flagship, it builds on the existing S1e `C.strategy` seeds, and it's the foundation the **E4 graded after-action report** will later summarize.
>
> **ARCHITECTURE — a PURE READ-OUT (the D106 OOB-board / D99 matchup pattern; byte-identical combat by construction):** new module `src/81-divergence.js` (`divScan` detector / `divIndex` / `divRenderTab` / `divWireTab` / `divEmergentOnly` + the `_divWILD_HIST` counterfactual map). `divScan` READS only existing strategic state (`C.strategy` / `C.president.emancipation` / `C.blockade` / `C.clock` / `C.stats`) and WRITES NOTHING; the ONLY write anywhere is `divWireTab` flipping the global setting + `saveLocal`. **No tick, no per-campaign state of its own; NO tactical file touched → the combat layer + all 9 battles are byte-identical by construction.** The emergent-only toggle is one guarded line at the top of `_vicWildSection` (80-victory.js) — default OFF → the wild-card section renders EXACTLY as before. Wired as one tab in `src/30-president-shell.js` (dispatch + `tabs[]` + `openWarDept` buttons + click-wiring). Detected hinges (all from reliable existing state): emancipation declined/early-1861/1862/**late-1864-65** (US); CS arm-the-enslaved; foreign recognition (achieved / window-open-past-Antietam); the 1864 election repudiation (US) **/ the CS home-front break** (side-correct); the CS war-trajectory; each wild card played (plausibility tier → divergence tier). Citation-grade, anti-Lost-Cause counterfactuals (McPherson/Foner/Howard Jones/Levine/Robertson/Weber/Du Bois — the inline-prose trust model of the sibling `_vicWILDCARDS`; failures named even-handedly both sides; the no-thumb-on-the-scale D54 framing explicit).
>
> **Bug-hunt (5 diverse-lens Opus finders × default-refute verify + a completeness critic; 11 agents) → critic invariantsHold = TRUE** (NO-FUDGE grep-confirmed: the div* symbols + the setting read appear in EXACTLY the 3 diff files, ZERO hits on any combat/resolve/tick path; BYTE-IDENTICAL-WHEN-OFF by git diff + probe). **2 confirmed + 4 critic-surfaced; resolved:** **(HIGH, FIXED)** `election-1864` lacked a `side==='US'` gate → a CS home-front break falsely fired the Northern-election entry with a Lincoln counterfactual → US-gated + a side-correct `cs-homefront-breaks` branch (probe-locked). **(MED, FIXED)** a **late** (1864/65) emancipation — reachable in play — was silently dropped → an `ey≥1864` "delayed" branch, a Union failing named plainly (probe-locked). **(MED, FIXED)** Davis's six-year term began **Feb 1862** (not "1861–1867") → CS counterfactual corrected. **(LOW, FIXED)** the toggle `aria-pressed` was inverted → a stable `aria-label` + correct state. **(MED, DOCUMENTED)** the inline-counterfactual citation-gate coverage gap → trust boundary documented in the module header. **(LOW, DECLINED w/ rationale)** a recognition side-gate — the recognition entry is side-neutral and historically correct for either player + unreachable for US by normal play → no defect.
>
> **Vet (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓`; `probe-divergence` **14/14** (+2 bug-hunt regression steps), **0 pageerrors**; full no-regression GREEN — `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`, `probe-desk` 13/13, `probe-victory` 12/12, `probe-command` 69/69, `t1probe` 14/14, `bootprobe` ok — all 0 pageerrors. **No tactical file in the diff** → the 9 battles byte-identical by construction (the headless `load` battle battery is the documented THREE-CDN env-stall, shares no code path). `wcag-auditor` AA (2 contrast fixes; CVD triple-encode + aria confirmed).
>
> **NEXT** (the E strategic arc + the GM capstone): **E1 deepening** — interactive HINGE-POINT forks (build on the decisions system) + curated alt-history scenarios; then **E4** — the rich graded after-action report (it summarizes THIS divergence ledger) + the §12.3 ELECTION-SUPPORT relief-bind (it needs the 1864-election state, now partly surfaced) → **E2** S4 codex/glossary/tutorial/presets → **E3** the dedicated FULL WCAG-AA pass. Or **(A) the symmetric AI-GM** ([hard], the §12 capstone — an enemy-command shadow wired to combat WITHOUT breaking byte-identity/no-fudge; T15 gives it a roster to draft over). **Q5 Chattanooga + Q6 USCT battles LAST** (Aaron's queue order). **Resume map:** START-HERE → this block → DECISIONS D111/D110/D91(roadmap)/D54(no-thumb) → V1-CHECKLIST Phase E → `src/81-divergence.js` (`divScan`/`divIndex`/`divRenderTab` + `_divWILD_HIST`) + `src/80-victory.js` (`C.strategy` seeds + `_vicWILDCARDS` + the `_vicWildSection` guard) + `src/30-president-shell.js` (the "warvshistory" tab) + `tools/probe-divergence.mjs`. **Honor the context-budget rule (≤25% → stop + hand off).**

## ⚡ EARLIER — 2026-06-23 **Q12: THE DIVISION SUB-TIER — seat your generals into the DIVISION billets nested under each corps; a corps is only as good as its divisions** (overnight run, D110 — Codex code + Claude vetting/doc close-out)

> **The GM depth-chart's next rung is shipped** (RATING-SYSTEM-DESIGN §12.1 — Army → Corps → **DIVISION** → Brigade), built on the Q10 corps chart (D108). Beyond seating I–IV Corps, the President now SEATS pool generals into each seated corps's **3 DIVISION billets**, spending **political capital** (`C.clock.capital`, the existing GM currency). A division can be staffed ONLY under a SEATED corps (the real chain of command), so the depth chart is a TREE built top-down. Teaches that a corps was only as good as its divisions — A. P. Hill's "Light Division" and Hood's/Pickett's divisions were the cutting edge of Lee's corps, **as were the Union's hard-fighting divisions in Hancock's II Corps**.
>
> **⚠ PROVENANCE — this milestone was a CODEX HAND-OFF, closed out here.** The Q12 CODE + DATA + PROBE were authored in an interrupted Claude-Code session and **finished + committed (`7b64d2e`) + pushed by Codex** — a sound, builds-`GATE OK`, byte-identical-to-source feature — **but Codex ran NO adversarial bug-hunt and wrote NO doc trail** (D110 was absent from DECISIONS/HANDOFF/WAKE-UP/RUN-LOG/V1-CHECKLIST/RATING-SYSTEM-DESIGN). This session is the **close-out**: ran the missing bug-hunt, fixed its one finding, and wrote the full doc trail. **No code reverted** — the commit is sound; the follow-up adds vetting + docs + one copy fix.
>
> **HOW IT REACHES THE FIGHT (byte-identical until you seat a division; D74/D94 no-fudge):** seated division commanders only add a small BOUNDED lift to `commandLeadership` — an INPUT the bridge already reads — via `_cmdDivLift` = clamp(Σ(`effDivRating`−64)×0.03, ±2). `effDivRating` = the general's GM OVR (carries the Q9 promotion) minus a `belowGradePenalty` (4) when his grade is below the **preferredGrade** — a division was a **Maj. Gen.'s** billet in BOTH armies (distinct from the corps tier where a CS corps was a Lt. Gen.'s), so any Brig. Gen. seated over a division leads stretched until promoted. The lift is deliberately SMALLER than the corps lift (cap 2 vs 4) so the hierarchy holds: **army ±17 > corps ±4 > divisions ±2.** No division seated → Σ over zero slots → lift 0 → the sandbox + all 9 battles identical to HEAD `7d86fcf`. **ONE BILLET PER MAN** (army/corps/division mutually exclusive; seating-higher clears the division); **vacating a corps CASCADES** (orphaned divisions stop contributing lift — enforced at the READ layer `cmdDivSeated`/`_cmdDivLift`, cleaned on load-sanitize). `cmd.divisions = {corpsIdx:{divIdx:id}}` rides the save (no `_SAVE_VER` bump); `cmdInit` sanitizes a tampered record on load. Writes ONLY `cmd.divisions` + capital (build-gate 4d scans `35-command.js`). NO RNG. `data/ratings.json` `divisionCommand` (Inferred + 2 sources: Glatthaar, Weigley).
>
> **Bug-hunt (5 diverse-lens Opus finders × default-refute verify + a completeness critic) → 4 lenses CLEAN, 1 confirmed LOW (FIXED):** the division teaching exemplars were **all-Confederate** (A. P. Hill / Hood / Pickett) while the rating mechanism is genuinely side-neutral and the sibling corps tier named both sides — the exact Lost-Cause over-celebration pattern CLAUDE.md #4 guards against. FIXED copy-only (byte-identical): added the Union counterpart (Hancock's II Corps divisions, Gibbon/Barlow) to the `divFoot` UI string + the `divisionCommand` `_note`. The save-tamper lens ran an 18-vector battery against `_cmdDivClean` (all sanitize correctly); the no-fudge lens grep-confirmed no combat path reads `cmd.divisions`; escaping confirmed safe.
>
> **Vet (offline gates GREEN; the headless `load` suite ENV-BLOCKED):** `GATE OK · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓`; committed HTML **byte-identical to the source build**; `probe-command` **69/69** (+11 Q12 steps), **0 pageerrors**; `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`; `t1probe` + `bootprobe` ok. **No tactical file is in the D110 diff** → the 9 battles are byte-identical to Q11 **by construction**. **ENV:** the strategic no-regression `load` battery (`probe-desk/ratings/camp/officers/field/oob` + the 9 battle probes) was blocked by the documented **THREE-CDN headless `load`-stall** (jsdelivr rate-limited the headless client after the first clean load; re-running the known-good `probe-command` reproduced the stall) — these probes share NO Q12 code path and were all GREEN in the Q11 vetting hours earlier. **The doc-trail + balance fix are committed in the close-out commit on top of `7b64d2e`.**
>
> **NEXT** (the GM §12 arc + the strategic arc remain): **(A) the symmetric AI-GM** (the opponent drafts/promotes/seats/commissions too, difficulty-scaled, NEVER extra strength — ⚠ [hard]: an enemy-command shadow wired to combat WITHOUT breaking byte-identity / no-fudge; surface if it forks; T15 gives it a roster to draft over). Cross-theater **Transfer** still needs authored `theater` fields in `generals.json` (all absent). The §12.3 **ELECTION-SUPPORT relief-bind** for the commissioned political generals (needs the 1864-election state — pairs with the E victory/election work). Or PIVOT to the **E strategic arc** (S3 alt-history · S4 codex · the dedicated WCAG-AA pass · victory + graded report + Reconstruction) → Phase F/I/H/J. **Q5 Chattanooga + Q6 USCT battles LAST** (Aaron's queue order). **Resume map:** START-HERE → this block → DECISIONS D110/D109/D108/D107/D106/D102(Q9)/D94*/D92/D74 → RATING-SYSTEM-DESIGN §12 (the depth-chart MOVES — Q9 promote · Q10 corps · Q11 commission · **Q12 division** shipped; AI-GM/Transfer/election-bind remain) → `src/35-command.js` (the "Q12 (D110)" block: `cmdSeatDivision`/`cmdVacateDivision`/`_cmdDivLift`/`_cmdDivClean`/`_cmdDivEffRating` + `cmdSeatCorps`/`cmdVacateCorps`/`cmdAppoint` — the GM spine) + `data/ratings.json` (`divisionCommand`) + `tools/probe-command.mjs` (the Q12 steps). **Honor the context-budget rule (≤25% → stop + hand off).**

## ⚡ EARLIER — 2026-06-23 **Q11: THE COMMISSION MOVE — bring the documented POLITICAL GENERALS (Banks · Butler · Sigel · McClernand · Floyd · Pillow) into your officer pool for political capital; rank and constituency are not competence** (overnight run, D109)

> **The GM "Commission" move is shipped** (RATING-SYSTEM-DESIGN §12.2/§12.3 — "bring a new officer into the pool"). Beyond the generals the war hands you, the President now COMMISSIONS the documented POLITICAL GENERALS — **US Banks · Butler · Sigel · McClernand**, **CS Floyd · Pillow** — into his officer pool for **political capital** (`C.clock.capital`, the existing GM currency; no new resource, §12), after which they are appointable / promotable / seatable like any general. A new President's-Desk **"Commission an officer — the political generals"** section lists each with his **LOW** combat OVR beside his **HIGH** political value, his constituency, an honest one-line record, and a Commission control (gated on capital + the `maxCommissions` 3-slate). It fills the GM layer's central TEACHING keystone — §12.3's command-politics bind: these men were cheap to commission and weak in the field (Lincoln/Davis kept them for the factions, votes, and immigrant regiments they delivered, not their generalship), so once you appoint or seat one his low OVR drags the army's leadership (a commissioned Banks fields commandLeadership **49** vs Grant's **87**).
>
> **PICK RATIONALE (decide-&-log within the cleared NEXT menu):** chose Commission (option C) over the symmetric AI-GM (B, [hard]) and a DIVISION sub-tier (lower-risk but thinner) because it fills the missing teaching keystone (the political-general bind the §12.3 anti-gamey constraints are built around), reuses the proven `cmdAppoint`/`cmdSeatCorps`/`cmdPromote` byte-identical-until-used spine + the §8 person concept, and adds 6 new citation-grade figures. Scoped to a **pure pool-addition** (no economy/election fork); the full §12.3 ELECTION-SUPPORT relief-bind is the logged next increment (it needs the 1864-election state — here the relief COST is already taught via each officer's `relief` class through `_cmdReliefCost`).
>
> **ARCHITECTURE (byte-identical until you commission):** the commissionable officers live in a SEPARATE data array (`data/generals.json` → `sides[side].commissionPool`), so `_cmdSideGenerals` (the starting roster) + every byte-identity-critical path are UNTOUCHED. `cmdCommission` writes ONLY `cmd.commissioned` (an id list) + `C.clock.capital` + a reputation seed — NEVER the scoreboard (build-gate 4d scans `35-command.js`). With nobody commissioned, `_cmdRosterPlusCommissioned`===the bare roster → the appoint/promote/corps pools, `commandLeadership`, and the 9 battles are identical to HEAD `8eb913a`. `_cmdById` was widened to ALSO resolve the commission pool (a commissioned-then-appointed officer resolves everywhere); `_cmdByIdRoster` keeps the roster-only lookup for the byte-identity-critical seeds + the commission guard. `cmd.commissioned` rides the save; `cmdInit` seeds `[]` once + SANITIZES on load (drop dupes/bogus/roster-ids/over-cap; non-array→array — the Q9/Q8b/Q10 idiom). NO `_SAVE_VER` bump. NO RNG.
>
> **CITATION-GRADE CONTENT (12-agent research+verify Workflow):** 6 Opus historians + adversarial source-check + a completeness critic; all 6 dossiers verified (sources real & on-claim, records honest on BOTH sides — Banks/Red River, Butler/Fort Fisher, Sigel/New Market, McClernand/Vicksburg, Floyd & Pillow/Fort Donelson; genuine contributions credited — Butler's 1861 "contraband", Banks's Louisiana free-labor — no fabrication). The verifier's one HARD correction applied: **Pillow → Brig. Gen.** (his "Maj. Gen." was a Mexican-War US-volunteer / TN-state-militia title; in CSA service he rose only to brigadier — CLAUDE.md #4). Ratings stamped **Inferred** (calibrated); bios carry ≥2 real sources (gate-4e clean); persona command/tactical/discipline calibrate so `fldOfficerSkillSeed` rounds EXACTLY to each entry skill (oracle verified for all 6).
>
> **Bug-hunt: 12 Opus agents (5 diverse lenses × default-refute verify + completeness critic) → invariantsHold = TRUE** (the critic grep-confirmed no combat path reads `cmd.commissioned`, `cmdCommission` writes only `cmd.commissioned`+capital+reputation, byte-identity-when-empty, Pillow Brig. Gen., every string escaped, zero id-collision). **5 confirmed (2 MED + 3 LOW), ALL FIXED + the behavior-affecting ones probe-locked:** **(MED)** the commission economy was bypassable at the function level — `cmdAppoint`/`cmdSeatCorps`/`cmdPromote` accepted an un-commissioned pool officer via the widened `_cmdById` → a `_cmdByIdRoster`+`cmdCommissioned` guard added to all three (byte-identical for roster ids; probe-locked). **(MED)** a seated commissioned officer always rendered "I Corps" (string for-in key → `_cmdNum`→0) → `parseInt(kk,10)` (probe-locked). **(LOW)** the shared `cap` gate idiom passed NaN (`typeof NaN==='number'`; tampered-save only, pre-existing) → `&& isFinite(clk.capital)` at all 6 capital-gate sites + cmdAppoint debits the rounded `cap`. **(LOW)** `maxCommissions` fallback default 4→3. **(LOW)** a commissioned officer past his service window now reads muted "Commissioned · no longer in service" (was a green false "in your pool"). (Critic-"missed" were all CLEAN confirmations: render purity, id-collision-free, the combat-default reads only the roster, the seed calibration, the documented `load`-stall probe-env note.)
>
> **Vet (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓`; **combat byte-identity STRUCTURAL + EMPIRICAL** — a 132-cell A/B (`.tmp/q11-ab.mjs` vs HEAD `8eb913a`) over (side × appointee × date) confirms `commandLeadership`/`bridgeArmy.overall`/`leadership`/the auto-resolve margin reads byte-identical with nobody commissioned, **re-confirmed after the bug-hunt guards** (inert for roster ids), 0 pe; `probe-command` **58/58** (+8 Q11 steps + a bug-hunt gate-lock step); FULL no-regression GREEN — `probe-oob` 16/16, `probe-desk` 13/13, `probe-ratings` 21/21, `probe-camp` 8/8, `probe-officers` 15/15, `probe-field` 13/13, `t1probe` 14/14, `bootprobe` ok, the 9 battles + `probe-csplayer` green (bullrun 15 · antietam 16 · fredericksburg 22 · gettysburg 16 · chancellorsville 24 · malvern 24 · shiloh 29 · vicksburg 18 · chickamauga 18 · csplayer 16); `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`; 0 pageerrors throughout; `wcag-auditor` AA (all new Commission-UI colours/opacities measured ≥ AA — no changes; the inactive-row nested-opacity note is covered by the WCAG inactive-component exemption). **ENV:** intermittent THREE-CDN headless `load`-stalls (vicksburg's 3D-readability step + the A/B) cleared by retry / a server restart — the documented gotcha; the `domcontentloaded` A/B harness is the authoritative byte-identity spine.
>
> **NEXT** (the GM §12 arc + the strategic arc remain): **(A) the symmetric AI-GM** (the opponent drafts/promotes/seats/commissions too, difficulty-scaled, NEVER extra strength — ⚠ [hard]: an enemy-command shadow wired to combat WITHOUT breaking byte-identity / no-fudge; surface if it forks). Cross-theater **Transfer** still needs authored `theater` fields in `generals.json` (all absent). A **DIVISION sub-tier** under each corps (extends Q10's byte-identical-until-moved pattern, lower-risk). The §12.3 **ELECTION-SUPPORT relief-bind** for the now-commissionable political generals (needs the 1864-election state — pairs naturally with the E victory/election work). Or PIVOT to the **E strategic arc** (S3 alt-history · S4 codex · the dedicated WCAG-AA pass · victory + graded report + Reconstruction) → Phase F/I/H/J. **Q5 Chattanooga + Q6 USCT battles LAST** (Aaron's queue order). **Resume map:** START-HERE → this block → DECISIONS D109/D108/D107/D106/D102(Q9)/D94*/D92/D74 → RATING-SYSTEM-DESIGN §12 (the depth-chart MOVES — Q9 promote · Q10 corps · Q11 commission shipped; AI-GM/Transfer/division-tier remain) + §15 → `src/35-command.js` (the "Q11 (D109)" block: `cmdCommission`/`_cmdCommissionPool`/`_cmdRosterPlusCommissioned`/`_cmdCommissionHTML` + `_cmdById`/`_cmdByIdRoster` + the GM spine `cmdAppoint`/`cmdSeatCorps`/`cmdPromote`) + `data/generals.json` (`sides[side].commissionPool`) + `data/ratings.json` (`commission` + the 6 commission `generalPersonas`) + `tools/probe-command.mjs` (the Q11 steps) + `.tmp/q11-ab.mjs` (the A/B). **Honor the context-budget rule (≤25% → stop + hand off).**

## ⚡ EARLIER — 2026-06-23 **Q10: THE CORPS DEPTH-CHART — seat your generals into corps command over the Order-of-Battle tree; who you place (and whether his rank fits the billet) shapes how well the whole army fights** (overnight run, D108)

> **The GM depth-chart MOVE is shipped, built on the D106 OOB tree** (RATING-SYSTEM-DESIGN §12.1/§12.2/§12.4 — "an army version of waiver/trading/transfer"). Beyond appointing the ARMY commander (the existing `cmdAppoint` billet), a new President's-Desk **"The Corps Command — Depth Chart"** section lets the President SEAT pool generals into the army's CORPS billets (I–IV Corps), spending **political capital** (`C.clock.capital` — the existing GM currency, no new resource). Each billet shows its seated commander (name + grade + OVR + a below-grade tell) with a Vacate control, or "Vacant" + a pool `<select>` + a Seat control (gated on capital); a live read-out shows the net leadership the staffed corps add. Teaches real command organization — Lee's First (Longstreet) and Second (Jackson) Corps vs the Army of the Potomac's churning corps commands; the rout of Howard's XI Corps at Chancellorsville.
>
> **PICK RATIONALE (decide-&-log within the cleared NEXT menu):** chose the depth-chart MOVES (option A) over the symmetric AI-GM (option B, flagged [hard]) because A extends the proven `cmdAppoint`/`cmdPromote` spine over the D106 substrate and is byte-identical-until-moved like Q9; the new officer-slot/vacancy concept the derived T15 corps lacked is the `cmd.corps` slot map + a side-aware grade-fit.
>
> **HOW IT REACHES THE FIGHT (no new combat path; D74/D94 no-fudge):** seated corps commanders only add a small BOUNDED lift to `commandLeadership(C)` — an INPUT the bridge already reads — via `_cmdCorpsLift(C)` = `clamp( SUM over seated slots of (effectiveCorpsRating − 64) × perSlotWeight, ±liftCap )` (0.05 / cap 4). `effectiveCorpsRating` = the general's GM OVR (carries the Q9 promotion lift) minus `belowGradePenalty` (6) when his CURRENT grade is below the **SIDE-AWARE** preferred grade. It reaches combat ONLY through the existing `commandLeadership → bridge LEADERSHIP facet` pipe, NEVER the scoreboard (build-gate 4d scans `35-command.js` + `tactical/T15-oob.js`). The army commander still weighs most (~±17); the corps lift (±4) is a secondary modifier (§27).
>
> **THE SIDE-AWARE GRADE-FIT (historically precise; the Q9 synergy):** a US corps was a **Maj. Gen.'s** command, a CS corps a **Lt. Gen.'s** (the CS authorized the Lt. Gen. grade Sept. 18, 1862, promoting Longstreet/Jackson that Oct. to command the ANV's new corps). So on the shipped roster only CS **Stuart** (a Maj. Gen.) seats below grade over a corps; promoting him to Lt. Gen. (Q9) removes the penalty. `data/ratings.json` `corpsCommand`: `slots 4 · seatCost 3 · preferredGrade {US:"Maj. Gen.",CS:"Lt. Gen."} · perSlotWeight 0.05 · liftCap 4 · belowGradePenalty 6 · Inferred · 2 sources`.
>
> **BYTE-IDENTICAL until you seat a corps:** no corps seated → lift 0 → `commandLeadership` unchanged → the sandbox + all 9 battles identical to HEAD `2d11bd7` (a vacant billet contributes 0, NOT a penalty). `cmd.corps` rides the save (no `_SAVE_VER` bump); `cmdInit` sanitizes a tampered/imported record on LOAD (drop invalid slots/ids/dead generals/the army commander; one corps per general; array→{}). The army commander can't double-hold (excluded from the pool; `cmdAppoint` clears his corps). The seated commanders also NAME the player's DERIVED corps on the OOB board (pure display — the predicted edge is UNCHANGED; the enemy is never named; a "Garrison" node is never named).
>
> **Bug-hunt: 11 Opus agents (5 diverse lenses × default-refute verify + a completeness critic) → invariantsHold = TRUE** (the critic independently grep-confirmed no combat path reads `cmd.corps`, seat/vacate write only `cmd.corps`+capital, byte-identity airtight, no fabricated officers, the 2 sources real). **3 confirmed (1 MED + 2 LOW), ALL FIXED:** **(MED)** the seat-log said "leads **above** his grade" for a BELOW-grade general → "below". **(LOW)** a real general could be named over a derived **"Garrison"** node (a fort/detachment, not a corps) → the attach now guards to `/ Corps$/` nodes only (probe-locked). **(LOW)** the CS Lt. Gen. grade date ("created Oct 1862") → corrected to the Sept. 18, 1862 authorization + the Oct. promotions + the Nov. 6 reorganization.
>
> **Vet (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓`; **byte-identity STRUCTURAL + EMPIRICAL** — a 132-cell A/B (`.tmp/q10-ab.mjs` vs HEAD `2d11bd7`) over (side×appointee×date) confirms `commandLeadership`/`bridgeArmy.overall`/`leadership`/margin reads byte-identical with no corps seated; `probe-command` **49/49** (+9 Q10 steps), `probe-oob` **16/16** (+the Q10 step: names the derived CORPS, edge unchanged, the Garrison guard, enemy never named); FULL no-regression GREEN — `probe-desk` 13/13, `probe-ratings` 21/21, `probe-camp` 8/8, `probe-officers` 15/15, `probe-field` 13/13, `t1probe` 14/14, `bootprobe` ok, the 9 battles + `probe-csplayer` green; `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`; 0 pageerrors; `wcag-auditor` AA (3 contrast fixes on the new UI). **ENV:** intermittent THREE-CDN load-stalls on the heaviest battle probes cleared by standalone retry; the `domcontentloaded` A/B harness is the authoritative spine.

## ⚡ EARLIER — 2026-06-23 **Q8b: THE CAVALRY RECONNAISSANCE — "scout the enemy" tier-reveals the enemy Order of Battle, scaled by your appointed general's cavalry** (overnight run, D107)

> **The camp loop's deferred SCOUTING (§15) is shipped, built on the D106 OOB board.** An **"Order a reconnaissance"** control inside the Command-desk Order-of-Battle board lets the President spend a small amount of **political capital** (`C.clock.capital` — the existing GM currency, also spent by appoint/promote; no new resource) to reveal the **enemy** order of battle to a TIER scaled by the appointed army commander's persona **`cavalry`** attribute. **Three tiers (§15):** **light** (unscouted — the passive D106 estimate: force grade band + rough count + the CTA) → **better** (scouted, cavalry < 65: the enemy commander NAMED + per-corps grade + a posture/terrain advisory, no per-brigade detail) → **full** (scouted, cavalry ≥ 65: the complete enemy OOB). With the 9 generals' cavalry, Grant (66) + Lee (70) → full, the rest → better; a no-appointee campaign uses the HISTORICAL DEFAULT commander's cavalry. Intel is **fresh per engagement** (the scout record is keyed to the next-battle id → reverts to light when the battle changes). Teaches cavalry-as-the-army's-eyes — Stuart's rides, and the Gettysburg intelligence vacuum when his cavalry was absent (a real Confederate command failure, not glorified).
>
> **PICK RATIONALE (decide-&-log within the cleared NEXT menu):** chose scouting over the GM depth-chart MOVES (both unblocked by D106) because its scaling input (the `cavalry` persona attribute, spread 50–70) + its `reveal` hook in `fldCampaignOOBHtml` BOTH already existed, `36-camp.js` explicitly deferred scouting to exactly this substrate, the D106 board already advertised a dead "Scout the enemy" CTA, and scouting is **pure display + an economy spend (never touches combat)** — the safest byte-identity. The corps-billet MOVES need a new officer-slot concept the derived T15 tree lacks — deferred next.
>
> **ARCHITECTURE (the D106 wall preserved):** the WRITE `cmdScout` (`src/35-command.js`) gates on capital like `cmdAppoint`, reads the appointee's cavalry via `_cmdGenPersona`, computes the tier via the pure `_cmdScoutTierForCavalry`, and writes ONLY `cmd.scout` + debits capital — never the scoreboard. **T15-oob.js stays a PURE READ-OUT** — `fldCampaignOOBHtml(C, {reveal, reconHtml})` is told the tier + the recon-control HTML by `cmdRenderTab`; T15 only ADDS the "better"-tier renderer (`_fldOOBSideScouted` + `_fldScoutPosture`). **No combat/resolve/tick/conditioning path reads `cmd.scout`** (grep-confirmed) → a campaign that never scouts is byte-identical to the pre-Q8b build, and the 9 tactical battles are untouched. `cmd.scout` rides the save (C.president serialized wholesale); `cmdInit` sanitizes a malformed/tampered record (drop→null; valid→bounded) — no `_SAVE_VER` bump. Data: a `scout` block in `data/ratings.json` (cost 3 · cavalryFullThreshold 65 · baselineCavalry 54 · 2 real sources). Build-gate 4d already scans both `35-command.js` + `tactical/T15-oob.js`. NO RNG.
>
> **Bug-hunt: 9 Opus agents (5 diverse lenses [no-fudge/byte-identity · economy/determinism/bounding · citation/anti-Lost-Cause/no-fabricated-officers · XSS/escaping · UI/WCAG/edge] × default-refute verify + a completeness critic) → invariantsHold = TRUE** (the critic independently grep-confirmed no combat path reads `cmd.scout`, cmdScout writes only `cmd.scout`+capital, the 9 battles byte-identical, no fabricated officers, citations real). **1 confirmed LOW + 2 critic-surfaced LOW; actionable ones FIXED:** **(LOW)** `cmdScout` gated on `round(capital)` but debited RAW `clk.capital` — the Q9 gate/debit rounding divergence `cmdPromote` was hardened against (byte-identical under today's integer-only capital paths, a latent + consistency bug) → debit `cap - cost` now. **(LOW, critic)** `_fldScoutPosture` ignored `C.flipAtk` (the alt-history attacker swap the resolver applies) → the "better"-tier posture could mis-describe who attacks after a recovery refight → now honors `C.flipAtk` with the SAME non-mutating `Object.assign({}, bd, {atk:swapped})` swap the resolver uses (strength/edge are attacker-symmetric, so only the posture line needs it; a probe locks it). **(LOW, deferred — critic agreed)** the cosmetic `cmd.scout.turn` stamp nothing reads.
>
> **Vet (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓`; **combat byte-identity STRUCTURAL** (no combat path reads `cmd.scout`) + EMPIRICAL (all 10 battle probes land on their historical results, unchanged); `probe-oob` **15/15** (+the SCOUT TIERS step: light→better→full ladder, "better" names + per-corps + posture + NO per-brigade leak, the flipAtk posture-flip + non-mutation), `probe-command` **40/40** (+6 Q8b steps: tier mapping + fresh="light"; reveal-at-tier + debit-exactly-cost; gate + idempotence; stale→light; save-tamper; no-output-gate + unscouted-offers-button); FULL no-regression GREEN — `probe-desk` 13/13, `probe-ratings` 21/21, `probe-camp` 8/8, `probe-officers` 15/15, `probe-field` 13/13, `t1probe` 14/14, `bootprobe` ok, ALL 9 battles + `probe-csplayer` green (bullrun 15 · antietam 16 · fredericksburg 22 · gettysburg 16 · chancellorsville 24 · malvern 24 · shiloh 29 · vicksburg 18 · chickamauga 18 · csplayer 16); `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`; 0 pageerrors throughout; `wcag-auditor` AA (1 contrast fix `#d66040`→`#e86840` on the new "Needs capital to scout" span — the existing `#d66040` usages elsewhere are the same sub-AA, **pre-existing/out-of-scope, logged for a future pass**). **ENV:** the run was PAUSED mid-vet on an Aaron "pause internet" directive (the battle probes fetch THREE from a CDN; the bug-hunt + wcag are API calls) and RESUMED cleanly; THREE-CDN load-stalls cleared by retry (the documented gotcha).
>
> **NEXT** (the GM §12 follow-up + the strategic arc remain): **(A) the GM depth-chart MOVES** — promote/relieve/**reassign** a named officer into a corps/division billet over the T15 tree (extends `cmdAppoint`/`cmdPromote`; needs a new **officer-slot concept** the derived T15 corps lack — the derived corps are formation-labeled with `commander:null`, so this means letting the player seat a pool general at a corps billet + a vacancy/rank model; the cleanest next GM increment, byte-identical-until-moved like Q9). **(B) the symmetric AI-GM** (the opponent drafts/promotes too, difficulty-scaled, NEVER extra strength — ⚠ [hard]: an enemy-command shadow wired to combat WITHOUT breaking byte-identity / no-fudge; surface if it forks). Cross-theater **Transfer** still needs authored `theater` fields in `generals.json` (all absent). Or PIVOT to the **E strategic arc** (S3 alt-history · S4 codex · the dedicated WCAG-AA pass · victory + graded report + Reconstruction) → Phase F/I/H/J. **Q5 Chattanooga + Q6 USCT battles LAST** (Aaron's queue order). **Resume map:** START-HERE → this block → DECISIONS D107/D106/D105/D104/D94*/D92/D74 → RATING-SYSTEM-DESIGN §15 (scouting SHIPPED) + §12 (the depth-chart MOVES, the next increment) + §13 (matchup) → `src/35-command.js` (`cmdScout`/`_cmdScoutTierForCavalry`/`cmdScoutTier`/`cmdScoutControlHtml`/`cmdInit`-sanitize + `cmdAppoint`/`cmdPromote` — the GM spine to extend) + `src/tactical/T15-oob.js` (`_fldOOBSideScouted`/`_fldScoutPosture`/`fldCampaignOOBHtml` reveal switch — the pure read-out) + `data/ratings.json` (`scout` block) + `tools/probe-command.mjs` (the 6 Q8b steps) + `tools/probe-oob.mjs` (the SCOUT TIERS step). **Honor the context-budget rule (≤25% → stop + hand off).**
