## ⚡ EARLIER — 2026-06-22 **THE OOB-MAPPING SUBSTRATE — the strategic next-battle now maps to a structured Order of Battle; a read-only roster board ships on the Command desk** (overnight run, D106)

> **The shared blocker for BOTH the GM depth-chart MOVES and Q8b scouting is built** (RATING-SYSTEM-DESIGN §12.1/§13/§15). New module **`src/tactical/T15-oob.js`** maps the strategic next-battle (`_brgNextBattle`) to a STRUCTURED order of battle — **commander → corps → brigade** — for both sides, each billet carrying its OVR/grade/strength/provenance (the T14 rating spine). Surfaced as a read-only **"Order of Battle — the Next Engagement"** board on the President's-Desk Command tab. Per the run plan, scoped SMALL first: a read-only query layer + roster board, **no combat wiring** (the procedural skirmish `_fldCampaignSkirmishParams` in T2 is UNCHANGED). **No new fork surfaced** — `BATTLES[]` already carries real `cmdUS`/`cmdCS` and the 9 scenarios carry real brigade OOBs, so the substrate is buildable read-only + citation-clean without contradicting a shipped teaching.
>
> **TWO SOURCES, ONE SHAPE (`fldOOBForSide`/`fldCampaignOOB`/`fldCampaignOOBHtml`):** a battle WITH a hand-built scenario (`fldScenarioData`) → its REAL brigade OOB (real commanders + the authored provenance read via `_fldOOBProvFromNote`, reusing the Q7 reader `_fldOOBUnits`); a procedural battle → a DETERMINISTIC corps/brigade tree split from `bd.us`/`bd.cs`/year, seeded by id+side (NO RNG). Derived billets are FORMATION-LABELED (I Corps · 1st Brigade · Garrison) with `commander:null` — **NEVER a fabricated officer** (CLAUDE.md #4/D92). The army-commander headline names only the player's APPOINTED general (+ his `_cmdGenRating` OVR) or the historical `cmd<Side>` (Verified). **ENEMY-FUZZY (§15):** the board shows the PLAYER exact + the ENEMY fuzzy (grade + count + commander, no per-brigade detail) — the hook Q8b scouting DEEPENS via a higher `reveal` tier; the substrate returns FULL data for both sides for the GM/scouting to query.
>
> **THE WALL (byte-identical BY CONSTRUCTION):** a PURE READ-OUT called ONLY from `cmdRenderTab`. The generated-html diff vs committed HEAD `7df4372` is ALL-ADDITIONS (the T15 block + one desk line, **0 removed/modified lines**) → the sandbox + all 9 battles are byte-identical. build-gate 4d's `RATING_MODULES` now ALSO scans `tactical/T15-oob.js`.
>
> **Bug-hunt: 15 Opus agents (5 diverse lenses × default-refute verify + a completeness critic) → "NOT SAFE AS-IS" → 5 confirmed (1 HIGH + 4 MED), ALL FIXED + re-vetted; the hard invariants [no-fudge/byte-identity, determinism, no-fabricated-officers, XSS] independently CONFIRMED HOLD.** **(HIGH, anti-Lost-Cause)** a multi-phase battle's phase-0 (opening) OOB shown under the BARE name could forecast a CS edge for Gettysburg/Antietam/Vicksburg/Chickamauga (Union wins) — the Q7 failure resurfacing at the display layer; FIXED by threading `phased`/`phaseName` + scoping the headline/edge/note/footer to "the opening engagement / the opening clash". **(MED)** the build-gate change my T15 comments claimed was never applied (the first Edit silently failed) → `RATING_MODULES += T15` actually applied. **(MED)** the derived artillery battalion destroyed ~12-15% of a side's men (no redistribution) → FIXED by carving the gun arm off the TOP of corpsMen (conserves EXACTLY). **(MED)** a mixed "(Verified identity; Inferred strength)" note overclaimed as Verified → `_fldOOBProvFromNote` downgrades a mixed note to Inferred. **(MED, defensive)** a one-sided authored OOB could pit authored-vs-derived → `fldCampaignOOB` decides ONE source for both sides. + 2 LOW probe adds (bounding, enemy-no-leak).
>
> **Vet (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK · no-fudge ✓` (4d scans T15) `· citations ✓`; **byte-identity 0 removed lines vs HEAD `7df4372`**; `probe-oob` **14/14** (incl. men-conservation 24000-exact · larger-army-renders-larger · phase-framing antietam→"The Cornfield" · enemy-no-leak · bounding · provenance-downgrade); FULL no-regression GREEN — `probe-command` 34/34, `probe-desk` 13/13, `probe-ratings` 21/21, `probe-camp` 8/8, `probe-officers` 15/15, `probe-field` 13/13, `t1probe` 14/14, `bootprobe` ok, ALL 9 battles + `probe-csplayer` green (bullrun 15·antietam 16·fredericksburg 22·gettysburg 16·chancellorsville 24·malvern 24·shiloh 29·vicksburg 18·chickamauga 18·csplayer 16); `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`; 0 pageerrors; `wcag` AA. ## ⚡ EARLIER — 2026-06-22 **RATING SYSTEM LIVE DEV-TRAITS — a general's OVR now develops over the campaign, shaped by a hidden Madden potential ceiling** (overnight run, D105)

> **The last open item on the R-6 rating track is shipped** (RATING-SYSTEM-DESIGN §9-R-6 "reputation level-up → OVR still TODO" + D94-fork-#5 "Reputation→OVR dev-traits = LIVE in-campaign"). `cmdOnResolve` already evolved a general's reputation by battle outcome and `_cmdGenRating` (0.55·skill + 0.45·reputation) → `commandLeadership` already read it; D105 adds the **Madden DEV-TRAIT** that SHAPES that arc — a hidden potential **ceiling**, a **floor**, a development **rate** (rise vs fall), and an **attritionDrag** (the §15-R3 decline-with-heavy-attrition). **ACCURATE INPUTS, never an output gate (D74/D92)** — a dev-trait only shapes the general's REPUTATION (an INPUT the existing pipe consumes), it NEVER writes a casualty/winner; build-gate 4d now also scans `35-command.js` to make that structural.
>
> **THE KEYSTONE (byte-identical-when-off, the D104 pattern):** the `cmdOnResolve` seam is byte-identical for an UNASSIGNED general (or absent devTraits data) — the LITERAL pre-D105 path `clamp(cur+delta,5,98)`, rate 1, no drag (proven across ALL five outcome quadrants + both clamp rails). The deliberate, citation-grade change is the authored `data/ratings.json` `devTraits.assign` map (20 generals → one of **12 archetypes**). Ceiling/floor are RELATIVE to the seeded start (`devTrack.start`, fixed); every archetype keeps `ceilingAbove≥5 & floorBelow≥5` so the pre-D105 win-raises/loss-lowers invariant holds for all. **D105 touches NO tactical code → the 9 battles are byte-identical by construction** (`git diff` confirms the change is `35-command.js` + the `devTraits` data + the gate + the probe only; the tactical layer never runs `cmdOnResolve`).
>
> **ANTI-LOST-CAUSE THESIS:** the Union's elite are RISERS — Grant/Sherman/Sheridan (`rising_star`) + Thomas (`steady_rock`) develop to the summit, rated as high as the Confederacy's born legends Lee/Jackson (`established_legend`) — the honest claim that the Union *found and elevated* its best command, while the Confederacy's calcified (Bragg `quarrelsome`) and self-destructed (Hood `volatile_aggressor`). Command failures named plainly on BOTH sides (US McClellan `plateau` / Burnside·Hooker `overpromoted`; CS Bragg / Hood). All 20 stamped **Inferred** (a calibrated reading of a documented arc — gate-4e-exempt) with 1-2 real sources each; nothing laundered (Forrest's Klan + Longstreet's Lost-Cause slander named in the notes). New: a Command-desk **"Career Arc"** read-out (`_cmdCareerArcHTML`) — the dev-trait tier + the OVR arc (start→now→peak on a floor..ceiling band with ticks) + a trend verdict (Rising/Holding/Fading/Untested); triple-encoded, CVD-safe, **wcag AA**.
>
> **Bug-hunt: 29 Opus agents (5 diverse lenses [no-fudge · byte-identity · bounding/determinism · citation/balance · UI/probe] × default-refute verify + a completeness critic) → SAFE TO COMMIT** (the critic confirmed BOTH hard invariants HOLD via independent grep: `devTrack`/`reputation` are read by no combat code; the off-path is the literal pre-D105 arithmetic). 8 confirmed + 3 critic-missed (1 HIGH · 3 MED · 7 LOW), ALL fixed: **(HIGH, the only blocker)** a fabricated-by-misattribution Thomas citation ("Conquered into Liberty" is Eliot Cohen's, not Larry Daniel's) → corrected to Daniel's *Days of Glory: The Army of the Cumberland* (CLAUDE.md non-negotiable #4); **(MED)** the Burnside note "proved right at Fredericksburg" laundered a Union failure → rewritten to name the futile Marye's-Heights assaults plainly; **(MED, keystone)** `_cmdDevTrait`/`_cmdDevStart` consumed `devTrack.start` RAW → a tampered/stale save (start NaN/500) poisoned the clamp → now coerced+clamped at the read site, `cmdInit` SANITIZES the whole record on LOAD (the Q9 pattern), `_cmdCareerArcHTML` coerces the whole `tr` record; **(MED→the 4d coverage gap above);** **(LOW ×7)** byte-identical-off probe now covers all 5 quadrants + both rails; the off-test asserts assign restored; floor/drag steps assert the locked trait; the render step covers all 4 trend branches + battles=0; a new SAVE-TAMPER probe step; the balance check counts named-failures as (−,~).
>
> **Vet (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK · no-fudge ✓` (4d now scans 35-command.js) `· citations ✓` (the Thomas fix); `probe-command` **34/34** (9 D105 steps: data+balance · byte-identical-off all-quadrants+rails · assign-restored · rising-star-vs-plateau+ceiling-cap · floor-cap · attrition-drag-bounded · no-output-gate+devTrack-purity · save-tamper · render+4-trend-branches); `probe-ratings` **21/21** (the tactical rating engine + 9-battle badges intact — byte-identical by construction), `probe-desk` 13/13, `probe-camp` 8/8, `probe-officers` 15/15, `t1probe` 14/14, `bootprobe` ok; `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`; **0 pageerrors**; `wcag-auditor` AA. ENV: a healthy memory window this session — probes ran foreground via a threaded server.

## ⚡ EARLIER — 2026-06-22 **RATING SYSTEM R-6 — the badge ASSIGNMENT to the 9 rosters: the rating system finally BITES in the historical battles** (overnight run, D104)

> **The rating system now changes combat in the real battles** (it was byte-identical-when-unassigned since R-3). **43 documented commander/unit traits** are assigned to the 9 shipped battles' brigade tokens via a central `data/ratings.json` `rosterBadges` map { scenarioId → unitId → [badgeKey] }; the seam stamps them through `fldBrSpec`→`fldMakeUnit` (a `.slice()` copy, null when absent → byte-identical); the existing R-3/R-4 engine (`fldBadgeFactor` static factor + `fldXFactorStep` surge, gated on `__FIELD.badges`, per-lever-capped) does the rest. **ACCURATE INPUTS, never an output gate (D74/D92)** — a documented trait nudges the universal levers and the result EMERGES; build-gate 4d still forbids the module writing cas/winner/men/sev. **Anti-Lost-Cause balance: CS 12 clear virtues : 6 flaws : 4 double-edged; US 11 virtues : 10 flaws** — the Union's command flaws (McClellan `the_slows`, Burnside `rigid_plan`, McClernand/Sherman `piecemeal`, green/surprised troops) are named as plainly as the Confederacy's virtues.
>
> **Design = a 10-agent citation Workflow** (one Opus historian per battle proposing source-cited badge→unit assignments + an anti-Lost-Cause/flip-risk critic; its 5 required edits applied). Notable calls: `the_slows` sits on Antietam `us_sturgis` (no McClellan army token exists; per D92 NOT forced onto every division — the P2 Burnside's-Bridge delay); `grand_charge` on Chickamauga `cs_johnson_gap` (the breakthrough that SUCCEEDED) and DECLINED on Pickett's repulsed charge; **Shiloh retuned** — the headless single-phase baseline is a ~tossup, so the Union's caught-napping flaw rides the SCENARIO (the CS surprise) not a combat penalty that would flip it against history, while the Hornet's-Nest STAND (Prentiss `rock_of_chickamauga` + W.H.L. Wallace `stonewall`) is modeled → US ~4/5. New: an accessible **badge-chip HUD** (`fldRatingBadgesHtml`, wcag PASS).
>
> **Bug-hunt: 16 Opus agents (5 lenses × default-refute verify + critic) → SAFE TO COMMIT** (both hard invariants — no output gate, byte-identical-when-off — re-verified intact). All 8 confirmed + 3 critic-missed were LOW/MED; fixes applied: `grand_charge`'s note de-overclaimed (the "doomed double-edge" downside is NOT modeled — it ships upside-only, deferred); the probe now resolves unit-ids in ALL 9 scenarios (rot guard); a latent `_spdMul` reset; `cs_colston_div`'s `stonewall` removed (role mismatch — Colston was attacking); the `_rosterNote` honesty pass (dual-badge tally + the always-on R-3 static-trigger disclosure + the flip framing).
>
> **Vet (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** GATE OK · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓; **byte-identity harness IDENTICAL all 30** (sandbox + 9 battles × 3 seeds) badges-OFF vs committed HEAD `610ae6c` + **0 winner flips** (the only single-phase flip is Chancellorsville seed 1 US→CS = Jackson's flank march = Lee's victory, i.e. TOWARD history); **all 9 per-battle probes PASS with badges ON** (Antietam CS/US/CS 8/8 · Gettysburg Pickett-repulsed US 8/8 · Fredericksburg CS 8/8 · Vicksburg 8/8 · Chickamauga Rock-holds 8/8 · Shiloh 29/29 · Chancellorsville 24/24 · Malvern 24/24 · Bull Run ok); `probe-ratings` **21/21** (new R-6 step) · `probe-presets` 26 · `probe-arms` 23 · `probe-logistics` 14 (the 3 layer-isolation `_badgesOff` fixes — see below); `wcag` clean; `diag-classic nonBlank:346`; `bootprobe` ok; 0 pe. **ENV:** the 8 GB Mac was memory-starved (~400 MB free; the user's apps held the RAM) so headless-Chrome probes stalled/crashed intermittently — each was confirmed by retrying until a memory window opened; the `domcontentloaded` A/B harness was the reliable spine.
>
> **Probe note (the 3 fixes):** badges is now a default-ON optional combat layer that legitimately shifts bullrun1 (CS stonewalls → CS 8/8), which broke 3 LAYER-ISOLATION byte-identity guards (presets/arms/logistics) that hardcoded the pre-badge CS-5/8 / [4,6] baseline. Fix = add `__FIELD._badgesOff=true` to those guards (badges joins officers/logistics/arms in the optional-layer set turned off for the pristine baseline). The per-battle probes + csplayer/officers/etc. were unaffected (they want badges on / test the sandbox).
>
> **NEXT** (the D103 menu, unchanged): **(B) the GM follow-up** (symmetric AI-GM + cross-theater Transfer + Commission + the corps/division depth-chart tree — each needs absent substrate; consider building the **strategic-next-battle ↔ tactical-OOB mapping** first, it unblocks BOTH the depth-chart tree AND **(C) Q8b scouting**) → wire **live dev-traits** (reputation→OVR mid-campaign, half-built in `cmdOnResolve`) → the **E strategic arc** → Phase F/I/H/J → **Q5 Chattanooga + Q6 USCT battles LAST** (Aaron's queue order). **Resume map:** START-HERE → this block → `RATING-SYSTEM-DESIGN.md` §4 (badge catalog) + §9 (R-6 row) + §12 (the GM depth-chart) → DECISIONS D104/D103/D94*/D92(accurate-inputs)/D74 → `data/ratings.json` (`rosterBadges` — the assignment map; `badgeDefs`) + `src/tactical/T14-ratings.js` (`fldScenarioRosterBadges`/`fldBadgeFactor`/`fldXFactorStep`/`fldRatingBadgesHtml`) + `src/tactical/T1-bull-run.js` (`fldBrSpec` stamp) + `tools/probe-ratings.mjs` (R-6 step) + `.tmp/r6-ab.mjs` (the A/B + byte-identity harness). **Honor the context-budget rule (≤25% → stop + hand off).**

## ⚡ EARLIER — 2026-06-22 **RATING SYSTEM R-6 (citation-provenance) — the CATALOG-WIDE citation hardening + a permanent build-gate** (overnight run, D103)

> **The citation-grade non-negotiable is now machine-enforced, catalog-wide.** Before you can hand a brigade a combat-biting negative badge keyed to a documented flaw, that flaw must be properly sourced — so this milestone shipped the prerequisite to R-6's negative-badge assignment. **An audit found the "Verified-with-<2-sources" debt was NOT just badges (the logged D98–D101 framing) — it was 47 records across SIX data files** (badges 13 · personas 2 · weapons 9 · artillery 9 · engineering 8 · terrain 1 · cabinet 5). All now carry **≥2 independent, real, web-verified sources**. PURE METADATA + a little teaching-card text → **byte-identical sim** (all 10 deterministic battles IDENTICAL to committed HEAD `5b12916`; **no numeric combat field touched**, bug-hunt-confirmed).
>
> **THE KEYSTONE — a permanent build-gate (`tools/build.mjs` gate 4e):** recursively scans every `data/*.json`; any record whose `prov`/`provenance` is "Verified" (matched **trim+case-normalized on EITHER key** — so a `"Verified "` whitespace/case variant OR an `Inferred`-masks-`Verified` pairing cannot erode it) carrying a `sources`/`src` array of <2 → `die(5)`. **Verified firing on all 47 before the fix; passes after** (`GATE OK · parse ✓ · hex ✓ · collision ✓ · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓`). The ~72 long descriptive forms ("Verified specs (…)", "Verified (high) — …") cite inline in prose and trim-lowercase to ≠ "verified", so they stay out of the structured gate **by design**. This mirrors the 4d no-fudge wall: a non-negotiable made un-erodable by the build.
>
> **THE CITATION WORKFLOW (the ultracode pattern):** 15 Opus agents — 7 domains → per-domain finders → **default-refute** adversarial verifiers → a completeness critic; sources **web-checked**; discipline = NEVER fabricate, **downgrade-to-Inferred over guess**. **Zero fabrication** (critic-confirmed); all citations real/on-claim/independent. Two judgment calls: **`beloved` held *Inferred*** (its solid cite is Symonds 1997 on Cleburne; the obvious Lee source Freeman 1934 is Lost-Cause-tinged and held out); **`grand_charge` upgraded *Verified*** (Stewart 1959 · Hess 2001 · Sears 2003). Anti-Lost-Cause cut both ways: `feared`'s 2nd cite swapped Wyeth 1899 (hagiography) → **Hurst 1993**; the negative badges on real men carry the firmest pairs (Sears+Rafuse, Marvel+O'Reilly, Cozzens+Daniel, McMurry+Sword). **Accuracy fixes folded in:** the Fredericksburg cover card's "almost impregnable, and a deathtrap" was **misattributed to the NPS** (traces to a fan site) → paraphrased; `massed_battery` note dropped Pelham; `earned_in_blood` → "14 of the 16 (Army)"; Springfield/art-cs-handicap precision softened.
>
> **Bug-hunt (34 Opus agents: 4 lenses [gate-logic · data-integrity · citation-soundness · probe/doc] × default-refute verify + critic; 16 confirmed, ALL LOW except 1 MED, all latent; critic: "R-6 is SAFE TO COMMIT — every citation web-verified real, no fabrication, gate green, byte-identity proven").** Fixed now: the **MED** (gate strict-equality evaded by `"Verified "`/case) + the `||`-masking LOW → the normalized both-key matcher (synthetic test **8/8**); the doc "47" count; the terrain co-attribution + feared optics. **Deferred-&-logged (NO live offender):** add the singular prose `source` key to the gate; a distinct-author independence check (every record already has ≥2 distinct authors); polish the other negative-badge second sources (defensible as-is).
>
> **Files:** `tools/build.mjs` (gate 4e + the `GATE OK` line); `data/{weapons,artillery,engineering,terrain-cover,cabinet,ratings}.json` (sources + a few text fixes; the cabinet 5 were applied by a research agent directly — reviewed + accepted); `tools/probe-ratings.mjs` (+1 catalog-invariant step, count-pin ≥26, normalized walk); `RATING-SYSTEM-DESIGN.md` (§4/§9 synced); regenerated `civil_war_generals.html`. The apply harness is `.tmp/apply-citations.mjs`; the byte-identity harness `.tmp/r6-verify.mjs` (base `.tmp/r6-base.html` = `git show HEAD:civil_war_generals.html`).
>
> **Vet:** `GATE OK · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓` (hardened); gate matcher synthetic **8/8**; **byte-identity harness IDENTICAL all 10** vs committed HEAD `5b12916` + an R-6 live citation sanity (catalog clean, 0 offenders; the_slows V/2, beloved I/1, grand_charge V/3; 0 pe); `probe-ratings` **20/20** + `probe-engineering-corps` 22/22 + `probe-cabinet` 12/12 + `probe-weapons` 8/8 + `probe-cannon` 9/9 (retry: the documented `load` stall) + `probe-arms` 23/23 + `probe-decisions` 14/14 (0 pe each); `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`. **ENV:** the `load` suite stalls intermittently on the THREE-CDN headless load (documented gotcha — start a threaded server + retry); the `domcontentloaded` byte-identity harness is authoritative.
>
> **NEXT** (pick one; decide-&-log within the cleared scope): **(A) the rest of R-6** — now that the catalog is citation-clean, ASSIGN the negative + positive badges to the 9 shipped rosters (this CHANGES combat — it is NO LONGER byte-identical; needs an A/B balance sweep + a historical-direction re-validation of all 9 battles, NOT an output gate per D92/D74) + wire live dev-traits (reputation→OVR mid-campaign, half-built in `cmdOnResolve`). **(B) the follow-up GM increment** (§12) — the symmetric AI-GM + cross-theater Transfer + Commission + the corps/division depth-chart tree; ⚠ each needs absent substrate (an enemy-command shadow wired to combat without breaking byte-identity/no-fudge [hard]; authored `theater` fields in `generals.json` [all absent]; the strategic-next-battle ↔ tactical-OOB mapping = the SAME blocker as **(C) Q8b SCOUTING**). Consider building the OOB-mapping substrate first — it unblocks BOTH the depth-chart tree AND scouting. Then the **E strategic arc** → Phase F/I/H/J → **Q5 Chattanooga + Q6 USCT battles LAST** (Aaron's queue order). **Resume map:** START-HERE → this block → `RATING-SYSTEM-DESIGN.md` §4 (badge catalog + the D103 sourcing note) + §9 (the R-6 row) + §12 (the GM depth-chart) → DECISIONS D103/D102/D94*/D92(accurate-inputs)/D74 → `data/ratings.json` (`badgeDefs` — now fully sourced, ready to ASSIGN) + `src/tactical/T14-ratings.js` (`fldBadgeFactor`/`fldXFactorStep` — the badge→combat seams that go live when badges are assigned) + `tools/build.mjs` (gate 4e) + `tools/probe-ratings.mjs`. **Honor the context-budget rule (≤25% → stop + hand off).**

## ⚡ EARLIER — 2026-06-22 **RATING SYSTEM Q9 — the GM PROMOTION economy (the first depth-chart MOVE: promote · multi-currency · merit gate)** (overnight run, D102)

> **The first real GM *move* beyond appoint/relieve is shipped.** On the President's-Desk Command tab there is now an **"Officer Corps — Promotions"** section: the President promotes a general one grade up a clean strategic ladder (**Brig. Gen. → Maj. Gen. → Lt. Gen. → General**), spending a **multi-currency** budget — **political capital** (`C.clock.capital`, the primary, shared with relief/appointment) **+ a NEW seniority pool** (`cmd.seniority`, accrues over the campaign, capped) — and **merit-gated** by reputation. PURE INPUT-SEEDING + display, **byte-identical until the player promotes** (sandbox + all 9 battles proven byte-for-byte identical to committed HEAD `fc1d251`). Built on the existing `cmdAppoint`/`_cmdReliefCost` spine + the R-5 `fldPromotePerson` latent-command concept; entirely in `src/35-command.js` (the "Q9 (D102)" block) + a `promotion` config in `data/ratings.json`.
>
> **THE MERIT GATE (the honest history, the keystone):** each target grade carries a reputation **meritBar**. EARNED (rep ≥ bar) → a confidence bump (+3 rep); ABOVE merit (rep < bar) → a ×1.8 capital surcharge **+ a jealousy reputation HIT (−4)**. Because OVR = 0.55·skill + 0.45·reputation, **over-promoting a favorite past his record can LOWER his OVR** — rank without merit doesn't make a good commander (teaches the real command politics Lincoln navigated; anti-Lost-Cause: merit, not grade, is competence). Leapfrogging a passed-over senior adds a seniority surcharge.
>
> **HOW IT REACHES THE FIGHT (no new combat path, no fudge):** a promotion lifts the general's effective skill by a small **BOUNDED** amount — `_cmdPromoteSkillLift` = (rankBase grade-delta × 0.92), clamped to **≤6** — added inside the EDITED `_cmdEffectiveSkill(gen, C)`, which flows ONLY through the existing `_cmdGenRating → commandLeadership →` the bridge LEADERSHIP facet (an INPUT the sim already reads; `commandMarginEdge` reads aggression/caution, untouched). `cmdPromote` writes ONLY `clk.capital`/`cmd.seniority`/`cmd.promotions`/`cmd.reputation` — **never the scoreboard** (D74/D94 wall). **0 lift when un-promoted → every pre-Q9 reading byte-identical** (live-sanity proven: a promotion stores "Lt. Gen.", lifts commandLeadership 79→80; the un-promoted skill reads identical with/without C for all 20 generals).
>
> **New fns (`src/35-command.js`):** `_cmdPromoCfg`/`_cmdRankBase`/`_cmdBaseGrade`/`_cmdGradeIdx`/`_cmdNextGrade`/`_cmdCurrentGrade`/`_cmdPromoteSkillLift`/`_cmdLeapfrogged`/`_cmdPromoteInfo`/`cmdPromote`/`_cmdPromoRow`/`_cmdPromotionsHTML`/`_cmdNum`; EDITED `_cmdEffectiveSkill(gen,C)` (+lift), `cmdInit` (seeds + load-clamps `seniority`, seeds `promotions`), `cmdOnResolve` (accrues `seniority` — invisible/byte-identical), `cmdRenderTab` (+section), `cmdWireTab` (+promote buttons); call sites `_cmdGenRating`/`cmdActiveTraits`/`_cmdActiveCard` now thread C. **Data:** the `promotion` block in `data/ratings.json` (ladder · meritBar · capitalCost · aboveMeritMul · seniority{CostBase,Leapfrog,Start,PerTurn,WinBonus,Cap} · earnedRepBonus · aboveMeritRepHit · skillLiftWeight · skillLiftCap · topCommandPreferredGrade).
>
> **Bug-hunt (7 Opus agents: 4 diverse-lens finders × default-refute verify + a completeness critic; 2 raw → 2 confirmed + 1 actionable critic gap, ALL LOW + LATENT; 0 HIGH/MED; critic: "SOUND, may commit — no-fudge/byte-identity/bounding/determinism/no-exploit all hold; no HIGH/MED missed").** ALL 3 fixed: (1) `cmdPromote` gated on `round(capital)` but spent raw → now debits the rounded `cap` (one lens; byte-identical under integer paths); (2) `_cmdBaseGrade`'s unrecognized-rank fallback "Maj. Gen." would over-grade a future non-named officer onto the General ladder → now "Brig. Gen." (lowest; hardens the deferred depth-chart path); (3) `cmdInit` now re-clamps a tampered/stale out-of-band `seniority` to the cap on LOAD. The critic's 4th note (the two `diag-classic` 404s) is the pre-existing documented tolerated asset-layer noise, not Q9.
>
> **Vet:** GATE OK (no-fudge ✓); `probe-command` **25/25** (9 new Q9 steps incl. the save-tamper LOAD re-clamp + the byte-identity micro-assert); **byte-identity harness IDENTICAL for all 10** vs committed HEAD `fc1d251` + Q9 live sanity (0 pe); `probe-ratings` 19/19 + `probe-desk` 13/13 + `probe-camp` 8/8 + `t1probe` 14/14 (0 pe each); `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`; `bootprobe` ok; `wcag-auditor` 0 AA failures (fixed the new warning text `#c2502e`→`#d66040`, 4.51:1). **ENV:** the `load` suite stalled intermittently on the THREE-CDN headless load (documented gotcha — cleared by a fresh threaded server + retry); the `domcontentloaded` byte-identity harness (`.tmp/q9-verify.mjs`, base `.tmp/q9-base.html` = `git show HEAD:civil_war_generals.html`) is the authoritative no-regression proof.
>
> **NEXT = a FOLLOW-UP GM increment** completing §12 once its substrate exists — the **symmetric AI-GM** (the opponent drafts/promotes too, difficulty-scaled, never extra strength; needs an enemy-command shadow wired into the matchup/auto-resolve without breaking byte-identity or the no-fudge wall), cross-theater **Transfer** (needs authored `theater` fields in `generals.json` — currently all `?`), and **Commission** + the full **corps/division depth-chart tree** (needs the strategic-next-battle ↔ tactical-OOB mapping — the SAME blocker as **Q8b SCOUTING**, so these two pair naturally). **Or Q8b scouting** next. Then **R-6** negative-badge sweep across all 9 battles + the **catalog-wide badge-provenance sweep** (D98–D101 logged: many badgeDefs are Verified with <2 sources — fix via a citation research+verify workflow) + live dev-traits → the **E strategic arc** → Phase F/I/H/J → **Q5 Chattanooga + Q6 USCT battles LAST** (Aaron's queue order). **Resume map:** START-HERE → this block → `RATING-SYSTEM-DESIGN.md` §12 (the depth chart + moves) + §14.3 (the political-capital/seniority numbers) + §15 (the GM-layer answers) → DECISIONS D102/D99/D94* → `src/35-command.js` (the Q9 block + `cmdAppoint`/`cmdPromote`/`_cmdEffectiveSkill` — the GM spine to extend) + `src/tactical/T14-ratings.js` (`fldPromotePerson`/`fldMaterializePerson`/`fldPersonTeam` — the R-5 person spine for the depth-chart tree) + `data/ratings.json` (`promotion` + `generalPersonas` + `rankBase`) + `tools/probe-command.mjs`. **Honor the context-budget rule (≤25% → stop + hand off).**

## ⚡ EARLIER — 2026-06-22 **RATING SYSTEM R-5 — the PROSOPOGRAPHY SCALE-OUT (bulk per-person rows + lazy materialization + play-as-anyone)** (overnight run, D101)

> **The D93 "Soldier's Story" substrate the whole rating spine was built for is shipped.** A brigade can now be inspected down to its men, any person *materialized* + *promoted*, and a generated soldier carries a deterministic name + Inferred rating — all **PURE DATA + DISPLAY**, **byte-identical** (no shipped scenario assigns bulk persons; sandbox + all 9 battles proven IDENTICAL to committed HEAD `84c5e95`). Selecting a brigade now shows its **MEN OVR** (the men's prosopography mean) beside the R-2 combat OVR, with a provenance-hatched accent.
>
> **THE LAZY-MATERIALIZATION KEYSTONE (§8) — the whole point of R-5:** a brigade TOKEN carries ONE aggregate `fldMenMeanOVR(u,year)` (its men's mean OVR, derived **O(1)** from xp + arm-branch + year + a deterministic per-brigade-id jitter, clamped [20,88] — a brigade AVERAGE never reaches the Legendary individual ceiling). Per-person rows are **NEVER instantiated en masse:** `fldMaterializePerson(spec,year)` builds exactly ONE row on demand; `fldBrigadeMuster(u,n,year)` a **HARD-CAPPED (≤6)** representative sample (captain/sergeant/private from `data/ratings.json` `musterTemplate`) — **never `u.men` rows** (proven: a 100,000-man brigade still materializes 3). The mean is always recomputable, never the source of truth (so a future zoom-to-regiment is a roll-up of materialized rows).
>
> **New pure functions (`src/tactical/T14-ratings.js`, the R-5 section):** `fldMenMeanOVR` · `fldMaterializePerson` (reuses R-0 `fldDerivePerson` → headline + dual OVR + grade; generated → deterministic synth period name [`_fldSynthName`/`_FLD_FIRST`/`_FLD_LAST`, NO RNG] + Inferred + a `latentCommand` seed; authored → Verified pass-through) · **`fldPromotePerson(person,toRank)`** (the latent-command **play-as-anyone** promotion — lifts command/tactical/discipline/charisma by the rank-base delta; returns a NEW person, pure) · `fldPersonTeam` (the EA-style {side,army,corps} journey hook) · `fldProvenanceStyle` (the **Inferred-vs-Verified visual distinction** §10 — Verified SOLID / Inferred **HATCHED** `repeating-linear-gradient` / Disputed dashed; meaning rides pattern + glyph [✓/~/?] + word, NOT colour → CVD-safe) · `fldMusterHudLine` (the live compact line) · **`fldMusterRollHtml`** (the full "Muster Roll" panel — probe-vetted + ready for the journey-mode character sheet / Army Register). **Live wiring = ONE guarded line** in `fldRenderHud` (`src/tactical/T0-field-sandbox.js`). NO new combat path; NO RNG.
>
> **Bug-hunt (11 agents: 4 diverse-lens Opus finders × default-refute verify + a completeness critic; 6 raw → 2 confirmed + 2 critic, ALL LOW + LATENT; 0 HIGH/MED; completeness verdict: no no-fudge-wall write, no determinism leak, no lazy-materialization breach, no escaping gap).** ALL 4 fixed — defensive-copy hardening so a FUTURE bulk-person/career path can't corrupt canonical `GAME_DATA`: **(1)** `fldMaterializePerson` returned the AUTHORED persona BY REFERENCE → now shallow-copies persona at the materialize boundary; **(2)** `fldPromotePerson` with an unmapped/typo'd `toRank` silently mapped to the 64 anchor (+16 unearned lift + false officerTier) → now requires a real `rankBase` key, else a clean no-op clone; **(3)** the `sources` array was a second shared reference (propagated by promote) → `.slice()`'d on both paths; **(4)** the persona-less promote guard returned the input ref → `_fldClonePerson` for every no-op path (honors the "returns a NEW person" contract).
>
> **Vet:** GATE OK (no-fudge ✓); `probe-ratings` **19/19** (the R-5 step: one-mean-per-token; the LAZY invariant [sample ≤6 for 100,000 men]; generated=Inferred+synth-name+latentCommand; authored Verified pass-through; promotion surfaces command + is pure; team hook; Inferred-hatched vs Verified-solid distinct; render triple-encoded; purity; **+ the 4 hardening guards** `aliasGuarded:true`); **byte-identity harness IDENTICAL for all 10** vs committed HEAD `84c5e95` + R-5 live UI renders (`.tmp/r5-verify.mjs`, 0 pe); `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`; `bootprobe`/`t1probe` ok; `probe-field` 13/13 + `probe-officers` 15/15 + `probe-command` 16/16 + `probe-desk` 13/13 + `probe-camp` 8/8 (0 pe each); `wcag-auditor` 0 AA failures (lifted 5 sub-AA opacity values `.55/.6→.65`). **ENV:** the `load` suite WORKED this session (threaded server) with intermittent THREE-CDN stalls cleared by a single retry; the `domcontentloaded` byte-identity harness is the authoritative no-regression proof.
>
> **NEXT = the post-R-5 GM DEPTH-CHART MOVES** (RATING-SYSTEM-DESIGN §12 — now UNBLOCKED by R-5's per-person spine): promote/transfer/commission spending political capital (`C.clock.capital`; extends `cmdAppoint`/`_cmdReliefCost`) + a **symmetric AI-GM** (the opponent drafts/promotes too — an arms race, difficulty-scaled) + the multi-currency economy (§14.3). **Or Q8b SCOUTING** (deferred — needs the strategic-next-battle ↔ tactical-OOB mapping). Then **R-6** negative-badge sweep across all 9 battles + the **catalog-wide badge-provenance sweep** (D98/D99/D100 logged: many badgeDefs are Verified with <2 sources — fix via a citation research+verify workflow) + live dev-traits → the **E strategic arc** → Phase F/I/H/J → **Q5 Chattanooga + Q6 USCT battles LAST** (Aaron's queue order). ## ⚡ CONTINUE HERE — 2026-06-22 **RATING SYSTEM Q8 — the between-battle CAMP LOOP (drilling + progression)** (overnight run, D100)

> **The agency that GROWS your rating over a campaign is shipped.** A new **"Camp" desk tab**: between battles the President DRILLS the army (4 foci — musketry/maneuver/entrenching/endurance — each toward a ceiling, costing fatigue; or **DELEGATE** to the field officer for a steady auto-sim gain) and reads the army's drill grade + the sitting general's OVR growth (PROGRESSION — his reputation already evolves in `cmdOnResolve`). PURE INPUT-SEEDING (no scoreboard), and **byte-identical until the player engages the camp** — proven, committed, pushed.
>
> **`src/36-camp.js` (new module):** `campInit` (idempotent; `C.president.camp` = drill{4 foci}, fatigue, delegate, **engaged**) · `campDrill(C,focus)` (raises a focus toward CEIL 90, +fatigue, sets engaged) · `campSetDelegate` · **`campTrainingBonus(C)` (THE BRIDGE SEAM** — per-facet deltas, ALL ZERO when no focus is drilled) · `campOnResolve` (rest sheds fatigue; a delegated army auto-drills; a clean win SEASONS / a battle this side bled the lion's SHARE erodes — gated on `engaged`) · `campRenderTab`/`campWireTab`. **Drilling/progression only — SCOUTING deferred to Q8b** (needs the strategic-next-battle ↔ tactical-OOB mapping; logged).
>
> **The bridge seam (`src/85-battle-bridge.js`):** drilling lifts firepower (musketry) / morale (maneuver) / supply (entrenching) + relieves fatigue (endurance) + a small overall sharpness — **bounded** (full-drill ≈ +8 overall, ≤14; fatigue DRAGS it) — EXACTLY 0 when undrilled → byte-identical. Wired: `campInit` in `_t1InitAll` (after cmdInit), `campOnResolve` in `_t1Resolve` (after cmdOnResolve), the "Camp" tab in `30-president-shell.js`, `"36-camp.js"` in the manifest. `C.president.camp` rides `serializeSave` + lazy-migrates via campInit — no `_SAVE_VER` bump.
>
> **THE KEYSTONE (byte-identity):** an `engaged` flag (false until the first drill/delegate) makes the WHOLE loop inert until the player opens the Camp (`campOnResolve` early-returns; `campTrainingBonus` is all-zero at zero drill) — a campaign that never camps conditions byte-for-byte as the pre-Q8 build. NO RNG (deterministic).
>
> **Bug-hunt (21 Opus agents, 15 raw → 1 confirmed MED + 4 critic), ALL fixed:** the real one — the "bloody" attrition test divided a raw men-count by `C.manpower.strength` (a 0-100 INDEX, also read *post-attrition*), so nearly every battle read "bloody" → the **win-seasons branch was DEAD** (drilled training only ever decayed, inverting the headline progression). FIXED to the casualty **SHARE** (own / both-sides total — unitless, order-independent; PIVOT 0.55), + the probe SEASONING step re-tooled to realistic casualties + a pyrrhic-win case. Plus: removed dead `lastTurn`; corrected an overstated comment; logged that drilling is a deliberate player-only lift (the AI doesn't drill; §27-bounded).
>
> **Vet:** GATE OK (no-fudge ✓); `probe-camp` **8/8**; **byte-identity harness IDENTICAL for all 10** vs committed Q7; `probe-bridge` 6/6 + `probe-conditioning` 6/6 (un-engaged conditioning unchanged) + `probe-command` 16/16 + `probe-desk` 13/13; `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`; 0 pageerrors; `wcag-auditor` 0 AA failures. **Shipped across a context-budget handoff** (the new ≤25%-context rule adopted this session — memory `civilwar-context-budget-rule` + `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` + START-HERE/AUTONOMOUS-RUN, committed `c926502`; the bug-hunt + fixes + this commit closed it out cleanly).
>
> **NEXT = R-5 — the PROSOPOGRAPHY SCALE-OUT** (RATING-SYSTEM-DESIGN §5/§8/§9 R-5): bulk-derive individual person rows (`fldDerivePerson` already exists — RANK_BASE/BRANCH_TILT/YEAR_DRIFT + deterministic seeded jitter) with **lazy materialization** (a brigade carries one `menMeanOVR`; per-person rows built only on inspect/play-as), latent-`command` promotion (the play-as-anyone hook), and the EA-style `team`/journey hook — Inferred OVRs visually distinct from Verified. Then the post-R-5 **GM depth-chart MOVES** (promote/transfer/commission + symmetric AI-GM, §12 — deferred from Q7) → **R-6** negative-badge sweep + the **catalog-wide badge-provenance sweep** (D98/D99 logged: many badgeDefs are Verified with <2 sources — fix via a citation research+verify workflow) + live dev-traits → the **E strategic arc** → Phase F/I/H/J → **Q5 Chattanooga + Q6 USCT battles LAST** (Aaron's queue order). **Resume map:** START-HERE → this block → `RATING-SYSTEM-DESIGN.md` §5/§8/§9 → DECISIONS D100/D99/D94* → `src/tactical/T14-ratings.js` (`fldDerivePerson`/`fldPersonaOVR`/the §8 person record) + `data/ratings.json` + `tools/probe-ratings.mjs`. **Honor the context-budget rule (≤25% → stop + hand off).**

## ⚡ CONTINUE HERE — 2026-06-22 **RATING SYSTEM Q7 — the GM MATCHUP layer (dual Attack/Defend OVR + the pre-battle matchup screen)** (overnight run, D99)

> **The rating system now pays off at the two decision points — who to appoint, and which battle you're walking into — as a PURE READ-OUT (no sim change; sandbox + all 9 battles byte-for-byte identical to the committed R-4 build).**
>
> **Dual Attack/Defend OVR on the Command desk (`src/35-command.js`):** `_cmdGenDualOVR(C,gen)` carries the headline `_cmdGenRating` (the R-2 OVR) PLUS an Attack/Defend split — the situational truth beside "general competence." Tilt source: the documented persona's aggression/initiative & resolve/grit (64-anchored, via the new `fldDualTilt` in T14) when the general is rated; else his own aggression/caution from `generals.json` (50-anchored ×0.18). Surfaced as **ATK/DEF pills** on the active-general card + a line in **every appoint-pool row** (so the appointment compares fit — the audacious Jackson grades higher on the attack, the cautious McClellan on the defensive).
>
> **The pre-battle MATCHUP SCREEN on the tactical side-choice card (`src/tactical/T7-command-side.js` injects `fldMatchupHtml(sd)`; engine in `src/tactical/T14-ratings.js`):** each army's **FORCE OVR** (the men-weighted mean of its brigades' `fldUnitRatingOVR` over the authored OOB — the same metric the live HUD shows, aggregated) + total strength + strongest brigade + commander, with a labeled **predicted-edge bar** (strength × quality → a US/CS share; names + percentages in text). Pure forecast of the odds from the historical inputs the D74 model consumes — **never read at resolve-time.** Triple-encoded + CVD-safe (number + grade + word + %); `wcag-auditor` 0 AA failures.
>
> **⚠ ANTI-LOST-CAUSE FIX (the bug-hunt's HIGH):** a multi-phase battle's OOB is authored as per-SECTOR day-slices, so the first cut **summed all phases** and forecast a **CS edge at Gettysburg** — a battle the larger Union army WON (a Lost-Cause-flavored falsehood on a teaching tool). FIXED: `_fldOOBUnits` now scopes a multi-phase battle to the **OPENING phase (phase 0)** and `fldMatchupHtml` labels it "**the Opening Engagement**." That is historically honest — Gettysburg's phase-0 CS edge is **day 1, when the CS did drive the Union through the town**, not a verdict on the battle the Union won. The single-phase forecasts are all defensible (Fredericksburg "US clear edge" → the US had the numbers and still lost to the stone wall = *anti*-Lost-Cause; the posture/terrain note carries the contingency).
>
> **Vet:** GATE OK (no-fudge ✓); `probe-ratings` **18/18** (the Q7 step: dual tilt directional + fldDualOVR-consistent; single- AND multi-phase force OVR; the opening-phase scoping is STRICTLY a slice not the all-phase sum; the "Opening Engagement" framing + commander line + an explicit anti-Lost-Cause framing guard for Gettysburg; graceful "" for no-OOB); **byte-identity harness IDENTICAL for all 10** vs committed R-4; `probe-command` 16/16, `probe-csplayer` 16/16, `probe-desk` ok; `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`; `bootprobe` ok; 0 pageerrors; `wcag-auditor` 0 AA failures. Bug-hunt: 21 Opus agents, 15 raw → 3 confirmed + 1 HIGH critic, ALL 4 fixed + re-vetted (the HIGH above; commander line phase-aware; anon dedup global counter; comment reworded off the unrated Thomas). Env: `load` worked this session (threaded server) with intermittent stalls handled by retries + the domcontentloaded byte-identity harness.
>
> **DECIDED-&-LOGGED (scope):** Q7 = the GM layer's *read-out* (matchup + dual OVR), fully buildable now on existing OOB+general data, byte-identical. The depth-chart *moves* economy (promote/transfer/commission + the symmetric AI-GM, §12) depends on **R-5's per-billet prosopography data** (no authored corps/division sub-commanders exist yet), so it is deferred to a post-R-5 GM increment — this slice ships the matchup payoff now; the loop proceeds to **Q8**.
>
> **NEXT = Q8 — the between-battle CAMP LOOP** (RATING-SYSTEM-DESIGN §15 "the camp loop"): **drilling** (each camp turn pick a focus — musketry/maneuver/entrenching/endurance — for a concordant skill+cohesion gain, or delegate to the officer for auto-sim gains; over-drilling risks fatigue), **scouting** (an auto-simmable recon phase tiered by the `cavalry` rating: light → strength+fuzzy grade, better → named badges+posture, great → the enemy OOB), and **progression** (attributes grow from combat XP + drilling; a hidden per-entity potential ceiling [Madden dev-trait]; decline with heavy attrition/age; reputation→OVR live in-campaign). Build it as a strategic-desk increment over `C.president`/`C.clock` + the rating spine; surface a "Camp" view. **Resume map:** START-HERE → this block → `RATING-SYSTEM-DESIGN.md` §15 (the camp-loop answers) + §14.6 (reputation→OVR dev-traits live) → DECISIONS D99/D94 → `src/35-command.js` (cmdOnResolve reputation evolution — the dev-trait hook) + `src/tactical/T14-ratings.js` (the rating spine) + the strategic-desk render (`src/30-president-shell.js`/`_wdRefresh` for a new tab) + `data/ratings.json`. After Q8: R-5 prosopography scale-out → the post-R-5 GM depth-chart MOVES → R-6 negative-badge sweep + the catalog-wide badge-provenance sweep + live dev-traits → the E strategic arc → Phase F/I/H/J → **Q5 Chattanooga + Q6 USCT battles LAST** (Aaron's queue order).

## ⚡ CONTINUE HERE — 2026-06-22 **RATING SYSTEM R-4 — the X-FACTORS (the dramatic "in the zone" surge, inside the no-fudge wall)** (overnight run, D98)

> **The Madden layer now SWINGS a fight — provably without ever touching the scoreboard.** An X-Factor badge enters "in the zone" when its situational trigger fires and surges on ONE channel, hard-capped. Demonstrated: a single `foot_cavalry` badge on one CS sandbox brigade **flipped a deterministic US win into a CS win** (same seed), yet the total-casualty shift was only **17.2%** and the command aura peaked at **0.47 < the 0.9 wall** — the rating reaches the result EMERGENTLY through bounded inputs, never by writing `cas`/`victory`/`men`/`sev`.
>
> **`fldXFactorStep(dt)` (`src/tactical/T14-ratings.js`):** gated on `__FIELD.badges`; for each unit carrying an `rung:"xfactor"` badge (`_fldUnitXFactors`), `_fldXFactorInZone` tests activation (march_vigor=moving · his_offensive=moving/charging · ammo_low_defend=ammo<12 & not charging · last_stand_defend=holding & (low morale or a friendly routing within 220yd) · usct_assault/mass_assault=charging · flanking=cav & charging). In the zone it surges **ONE channel = the declared lever**: a NON-speed X-Factor sets `u._xfActive = fldRatingRealismCap(tier,"xfactor")` (arcade 1.45 / balanced 1.20 / historian 1.08); a SPEED X-Factor (Foot Cavalry / The Slows) sets `u._spdMul` only (capped [0.85,1.15]) and does NOT also surge the aura. One-shot ⚡ `fldAnnounce`+`fldScenarioBanner` (`_xfOn` rising edge); `_xfGlow` marker pulse; deterministic `_fldXfDecay`→1 out of zone. **NO RNG.** A unit with no X-Factor is wholly untouched → byte-identical.
>
> **`fldXFactorApplyCmd(u)`:** `u.cmdBonus = min(FLDO.CMD_BONUS_CAP, (cmdBonus||0)*_xfActive)` — pushes the summed aura toward the **0.9 wall, never beyond**; strict no-op unless active. Called in `fldSimStep` AFTER `fldOfficersStep` (aura summed first), gated on `badges` (NOT officers) so both surge channels share one gate.
>
> **Seams (`src/tactical/T0-field-sandbox.js`):** `fldSimStep` runs the surge + apply around `fldOfficersStep`; **`fldMoveFactor` clamps the COMBINED speed factor** (the `_spdMul` surge × the R-3 static speed-badge factor) to ONE documented band **[0.75,1.30]** (exactly 1 → skipped → byte-identical); `fld2dDraw` draws a guarded `_xfGlow` — a CVD-safe amber burst RING (the shape carries meaning) + a **vector-drawn** lightning bolt (no emoji-font tofu), `reduceMotion`-honored. **Data:** added `first_with_the_most` (Forrest, cav) → 7 X-Factors; none assigned to a shipped roster yet (R-6) → byte-identical.
>
> **Vet:** GATE OK (no-fudge ✓); `probe-ratings` **17/17** (the R-4 surge step + the NO-FUDGE REPLAY GATE: the surge bites + bounded + capped); the **`domcontentloaded` byte-identity harness IDENTICAL for all 10** (sandbox + 9 battles, R-4 build vs committed R-3 HEAD — the authoritative X-Factor-off no-regression proof); `tactical-visuals` 10/10 scenes 0 pe; `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`; the 9 battle probes + field + logistics + bootprobe passed via `load` (when momentarily healthy). **⚠ ENV (still intermittent):** the standard `load` suite stalled on the THREE CDN this session — the systems probes (officers/command/presets/…) were `load`-blocked, but the byte-identity harness exercises the full `fldSimStep`→`fldXFactorStep`→`fldOfficersStep` path (officers ON) for all 10 and proves it byte-identical, covering their core concern. **Re-run them next session once `load` is healthy** (serve via ThreadingHTTPServer; if `load` still stalls, the durable fix = domcontentloaded+settle in the probe runner OR vendor THREE locally — a recommended small vetted milestone). **Bug-hunt** (23 Opus agents, 16 raw → 4 confirmed + 3 critic, 0 HIGH, all LATENT@R-6): all 7 fixed (combined-speed clamp; cmdBonus surge gated to non-speed [one channel/badge]; apply decoupled from officers; vector bolt; grand_charge prov→Inferred + dual-downside deferred-note); catalog-wide badge-provenance sweep logged for R-6.
>
> **NEXT = Q7 — the GM franchise layer** (RATING-SYSTEM-DESIGN §12–§14): the depth-chart roster (Army→Corps→Division→Brigade billets, each showing OVR+grade+badges + roll-up OVR) · roster moves (appoint/relieve/promote/transfer/commission) spending **political capital** (`C.clock.capital`, extends `cmdAppoint`/`_cmdReliefCost`) · the **dual Attack/Defend OVR** (`fldDualOVR` already exists — surface it) · the **pre-battle matchup screen** (corps-vs-corps OVR board, a labeled predicted-edge bar; pure display over numbers the sim already reads). Then Q8 camp loop → R-5 prosopography scale-out → R-6 negative-badge sweep + the badge-provenance sweep + live dev-traits → the E strategic arc → Phase F/I/H/J → **Q5 Chattanooga + Q6 USCT battles LAST** (Aaron's queue order). **Resume map:** START-HERE → this block → `RATING-SYSTEM-DESIGN.md` §12 (the depth chart) + §13 (dual OVR + matchup screen) + §14.3 (the political-capital economy numbers) + §15 the GM-layer answers → DECISIONS D98/D97/D94 → `src/35-command.js` (`cmdAppoint`/`cmdRevert`/`_cmdReliefCost`/`_cmdGenRow` — the GM spine to generalize) + `src/tactical/T14-ratings.js` (`fldDualOVR`/`fldUnitRatingOVR`/`fldRatingGrade`) + `data/ratings.json` (`generalPersonas` + `dualOvrTilt`). Build R-4's siblings (Q7→Q8) on the now-complete badge+X-Factor engine.

## ⚡ CONTINUE HERE — 2026-06-22 **RATING SYSTEM R-3 — the badge engine (the first rating→combat seam) + the no-fudge build-gate** (overnight run, D97)

> **The first place a rating TOUCHES combat is shipped.** A guarded one-token factor seeds the levers the engine already reads; **byte-identical when off** (proven: sandbox + all 9 battles produce IDENTICAL deterministic results on the R-3 build vs the committed R-2 build).
>
> **`fldBadgeFactor(u, lever)` (`src/tactical/T14-ratings.js`):** guarded MULTIPLICATIVE one-token factor — identity 1.0 when `__FIELD.badges` off / no matching badge. Sums the unit's badges' signed `mag`s for a lever, gates by trigger (`_fldBadgeTrig`), CLAMPS the sum to the realism-scaled per-lever cap (`fldRatingRealismCap(tier,"badgeLever")` — arcade .16 / balanced .10 / historian .06). Negatives DAMP (≥1−cap), never enemy buffs. Plus `fldUnitCohesion` (rally term, 0 when unauthored).
>
> **Four guarded seams (`src/tactical/T0-field-sandbox.js`):** fire (`fldResolveFire`), rally+cohesion (`fldMoraleStep`), melee (`fldResolveMelee`), speed (`fldMoveFactor` — `_spdMul` reserved for the R-4 X-Factor surge). `__FIELD.badges` gate (default ON, `_badgesOff` hook) + `__FIELD.realismTier` (`T6 fldPresetsApply`).
>
> **Badge data (`data/ratings.json`):** 25 badgeDefs gain `fldLever` + signed `mag`. Ships Star/Superstar positives + the Verified negatives with real bite (McClellan Slows / Bragg Piecemeal / Burnside Rigid / Green Levies / Powder-Shy). **NOT yet assigned to shipped rosters** (that's the R-6 sweep) → battles byte-identical.
>
> **No-fudge OUTPUT-WALL build-gate (`tools/build.mjs` gate 4d):** scans the rating module for any `cas`/`aCas`/`bCas`/`.victory`/`.men`/`sev.*` write — **dot AND bracket notation** — build FAILS (exit 5) if found. Verified firing on planted `u.men=0`, `u["men"]=0`, `F.sev["attrition"]=2`; clean build prints `GATE OK · … · no-fudge ✓`.
>
> **⚠ VETTING — environmental deviation (READ before re-vetting):** the standard `load`-based probe suite was **blocked** this session — headless Chrome's `load` event never fires for the game page (the THREE-CDN `<script>` stalls in headless; it breaks the committed-green R-2 build identically; a trivial page fires `load` in 73ms; `domcontentloaded` loads everything incl. THREE in ~1.5s). Contributors: the single-threaded `python -m http.server` chokes on the page's ~170 local asset requests (**use a ThreadingHTTPServer**), + the THREE-CDN `load` stall (documented gotcha). R-3 was vetted via a **`domcontentloaded` byte-identity harness** (`.tmp/r3-verify.mjs`): sandbox + all 9 battles deterministic, R-3 build vs committed R-2 → **IDENTICAL** for all 10, + the full badge-logic assertions, 0 pageerrors. Bug-hunt (4 Opus lenses × verify + critic): 2 confirmed (gate-4d bracket bypass → fixed; phantom `sharpshooters` key → fixed). **NEXT SESSION: re-run the standard `load` suite (serve with `python3 -c "from http.server import SimpleHTTPRequestHandler,ThreadingHTTPServer; ThreadingHTTPServer(('',8765),SimpleHTTPRequestHandler).serve_forever()"`); if `load` still stalls, the fix is a domcontentloaded+settle wait in the probe runner or vendoring THREE locally.**
>
> **NEXT = R-4** (the X-FACTORS — the dramatic Madden surge): `u._xfActive` activation ("in the zone") that scales the **already-summed** `cmdBonus` aura, hard-capped by `fldRatingRealismCap(tier,"xfactor")` (never breaching `CMD_BONUS_CAP`); `abZap`/`fldAnnounce` one-shot + HUD glow; ship Foot Cavalry, Rock of Chickamauga, Bayonet!, First with the Most, Earned in Blood, The Slows. **The no-fudge replay gate:** deterministic seed-replay shows badges-on final casualties differ from badges-off only within the bounded lever delta; X-Factor never exceeds `CMD_BONUS_CAP`. Resume map: START-HERE → this block → `RATING-SYSTEM-DESIGN.md` §4 (the X-Factor rows) + §6 (the `u._xfActive` seam: "scales the already-summed cmdBonus, clamped at CMD_BONUS_CAP") + §9 (R-4 asserts) → DECISIONS D97/D96/D95/D94* → `src/tactical/T14-ratings.js` (fldBadgeFactor + the realism caps) + `src/tactical/T3-officers.js` (`fldOfficersStep` — the cmdBonus aura sum, where `_xfActive` scales) + `src/tactical/T0-field-sandbox.js` (`fldAnnounce`/HUD glow) + `tools/probe-ratings.mjs`.

## ⚡ ARCHIVED — 2026-06-21 **ROSTER HISTORY-ACCURACY HARDENING — all 9 shipped battles audited; 15 HIGH wrong-fact corrections** (Aaron-directed; the EA-Sports-roster groundwork) — D92

> **Aaron asked whether the full Chattanooga-grade rigor had been applied to the already-built battles. It had NOT for the 7 pre-Opus battles** (the Codex/early-built C1 Eastern marquee + Shiloh + Bull Run); only Vicksburg + Chickamauga had the Opus adversarial history pass. An adversarial audit (9 Opus historians + critic + the new consolidated `tools/sweep-all-battles.mjs` casualty-direction diagnostic) found **15 HIGH wrong facts shipped under "Verified" stamps** — mostly the **rank-at-the-battle** class — ALL FIXED + annotated in-file: **Malvern** (Hunt→Col., Jackson→Maj.Gen., Porter→Brig.Gen., Hooker→Maj.Gen.); **Chancellorsville** (Rodes→Brig.Gen., Berry→III Corps, Stonewall Bde→Colston's div, **dead officer Hazzard→Best**); **Shiloh** (Breckinridge→Brig.Gen., Bragg→RIGHT wing, Wallace died Apr 10); **Gettysburg** (Doubleday & Early→Maj.Gen., Stannard→I Corps); **Vicksburg** (M.L. Smith→Brig.Gen.). Plus ~12 medium/low. **Surfaced, NOT auto-fixed (genuine source conflict):** Bull Run Heintzelman + Antietam Mansfield.
>
> **LOCKED PRINCIPLE (Aaron, the input/output distinction — obey it):** outcomes emerge from **skill + weapon type + terrain + tactics** via the D74 universal model; history enters ONLY as the **default equipment** (accurate counts/guns/weapons/terrain/doctrine) so the **probable** result lands near history *because the inputs are true* — **NEVER an output gate forcing a casualty direction/winner.** DROPPED the planned Antietam balance refight + hard historical-direction probe gates; `sweep-all-battles.mjs` is a DIAGNOSTIC only; kept guards are **system validations** (cover↓casualties, rifled out-ranges smoothbore, more guns=more fire) that keep the game legible. (Memory `civilwar-roster-accuracy-principle`; DECISIONS **D92**.)
>
> **NEW PILLAR captured + scheduled (D93, Aaron): "The Soldier's Story"** — an EA-Sports-style prosopography DB (bio/stats/history/portrait/team for EVERY identifiable individual) + a **play-as-anyone whole-war journey mode** (pick any person, choose when+where to start, proceed from their **real rank/position**). **Build AFTER the C+E roadmap**, unified with the D91 loot/survival journey mode + interpersonal/romance. The D92 rank/position hardening is its first roster data (hard prerequisite for "start from real rank"). Granularity = exhaustive ("every named individual found").
>
> **Verification:** GATE OK; all 9 battle probes green (each battle's balance assertions still green → outcomes byte-preserved), roster 8/8, custom-builder 9/9, broad tactical-systems suite + boot/t1 green, diag-classic `nonBlank:346`, 0 pageerrors. Edits are labels/notes only except Antietam Benjamin 6→4 guns (accurate) + Shiloh Wood→Chalmers (stats kept).
>
> **⚡ COMMITTED 2026-06-21 (resumed session — the prior one died on a 529 API error mid-D92 with the work uncommitted).** Before committing, ran an **independent adversarial re-verification** (Opus historian per battle + critic) to catch any D92 "fix" that was itself wrong → found + fixed **4 more** (see DECISIONS *D92-verify*): Bull Run Elzey gloss (his own regiment = the 1st Maryland, not the Virginia regts); **Shiloh Ammen → Col.** (was wrongly Brig. Gen.; BG dated Jul 16 1862, after the battle); **Antietam Battery B `rifled→smooth`** (six 12-pdr Napoleons — its note already said "double canister"; antietam probe stayed 16/16, outcome-neutral); **Malvern Hill Hooker reverted Maj.Gen.→Brig.Gen.** (D92 over-promoted him by date-of-rank, but his MG commission *issued* Jul 26 1862 after the battle — the Seven Days OOB lists him BG, like Porter/Couch/Kearny). Re-vet GREEN, pushed to `adhanke-star/History-Video-Game` main.
>
> **NEXT (unchanged): CHATTANOOGA** — the US-offensive reversal (Orchard Knob → Lookout Mountain → Missionary Ridge; brief `.tmp/chattanooga-design.md`, verified fact sheet `.tmp/chattanooga-factsheet.json`), then **C3 USCT battles**, then the **E strategic arc**, then the Soldier's Story pillar.

## ⚡ CONTINUE HERE — 2026-06-21 **CHICKAMAUGA — the 3-phase Western epic shipped + adversarially hardened** (the woods → Longstreet's breakthrough → Thomas at Snodgrass Hill; probe 18/18) — D90

> **Chickamauga is playable and verified — the bloodiest battle of the Western theater, the fourth multi-phase epic, built on the T8 engine.** A new **⚔ BATTLE — CHICKAMAUGA (1863)** main-menu button (rank 60, after Vicksburg) opens the side-choice card; command Bragg/Longstreet's army or Thomas's defense across three sectors in sequence: **P0 The Battle in the Woods (Sep 19)** — a confused meeting engagement in blind timber; the Union line holds the road (historical). **P1 Longstreet's Breakthrough (Sep 20)** — the Wood gap order pulls a US division out of line at the moment Longstreet's column strikes the Brotherton farm; the CS pours through and routs the right (the decisive **scoreWeight-3** phase). **P2 The Rock of Chickamauga** — Thomas holds Snodgrass Hill / Horseshoe Ridge until dusk, reinforced by Granger/Steedman, saving the army. Anti-Lost-Cause: **contingency over genius** (a staff blunder + a timing coincidence, not martial superiority) and **the squandered victory** (Bragg fails to pursue → the siege of Chattanooga → the November reversal).
>
> **Framing (decide-&-log, no fork):** the C2 "Chickamauga/Chattanooga" item = a matched PAIR of separate 3-phase epics (the Antietam/Gettysburg precedent), each its own milestone. **Top-level `attacker: CS` / `defender: US` across all 3 phases** (the Army of the Cumberland fought on the defensive) — the CS player is the offensive, the US player holds. This sets **Chattanooga up as the US-offensive MIRROR** (the next milestone; design brief at `.tmp/chattanooga-design.md`, which also banks the engine-grain lessons from this build). Universal gun model only; **no per-battle damage fudge** (D74).
>
> **Balance (AI-vs-AI, 32 seeds, deterministic):** P0 US-holds 32/32 (mutual bloodbath ~2,400 each); P1 **CS breakthrough 32/32** (US routed cheaply, US:CS ~5.1); P2 **US-holds 32/32**, CS attacker pays **CS:US ~3.0** (the Fredericksburg failed-assault lesson); **aggregate CS 32/32** (CS 3 / US 2). A human US player who closes the Wood gap in P1 **overturns** the battle (the alt-history hinge). The CS won the field yet **bled MORE in aggregate** (~70k vs ~63k / 8 seeds) — the costly-victory teaching, probe-guarded.
>
> **Research+verify (Workflow) corrected before authoring:** Hood Sep 20 / Maj. Gen. (not Sep 19 / Lt. Gen.); Helm under Breckinridge (not Cleburne); Cheatham restored; Granger & W.H.T. Walker = Maj. Gen.; Loring removed; gap-reporter flagged (Kellogg vs Bond); "River of Death" shipped only as disputed legend.
>
> **Bug-hunt (10 Opus agents): 2 confirmed + 3 critic, all fixed.** HIGH (the Vicksburg wrong-rank-under-Verified class): **Charles Harker was a COLONEL at Chickamauga, not "Brig. Gen."** (like Hans Heg; the BG dated to the battle but came after) → corrected to **Col. Harker** + a fact-check guard. Plus 4 probe-rigor guards (P1 casualty-direction US:CS ≥2.5, P0 winner ≥7/8, P1 win-by-SEIZE "hold", aggregate winner-bled-more).
>
> **Verification:** GATE OK; `probe-chickamauga` **18/18**, 0 pe; full no-regression GREEN (all 8 battle baselines + roster 8 + field/officers/logistics/arms/presets/csplayer/campaign-link/fog/ai/autopause + **custom-builder 9** [EXPECTED baseline updated for `chickamauga` — the D86/D88 gotcha] + engineering-corps 22 + **tactical-visuals 10 scenes incl. live Chickamauga 2D/3D, texWarn 0**; diag-classic nonBlank 346; boot/t1 ok; 0 pageerrors); WCAG inherited-AA (no new color CSS). Pushed to `adhanke-star/History-Video-Game` main (D90).
>
> **Next:** **CHATTANOOGA** (the US-offensive reversal — Orchard Knob → Lookout Mountain "Battle Above the Clouds" → Missionary Ridge; build it from `.tmp/chattanooga-design.md`, which has the framing + the engine-grain lessons), then **C3 USCT battles** (the Crater · New Market Heights · Olustee · Nashville). **Resume map:** START-HERE.md → this block → WAKE-UP.md top block → newest RUN-LOG.md entry → DECISIONS.md D90 → `.tmp/chattanooga-design.md` → `data/chickamauga.json` (the template) + `src/tactical/T1-bull-run.js` (registry/rank) + `src/tactical/T8-phases.js` (multi-phase engine) + `tools/probe-chickamauga.mjs` / `tools/sweep-chickamauga.mjs` (probe/sweep templates).

## ⚡ CONTINUE HERE — 2026-06-21 **ENGINEERING CORPS — increment 3: PONTOON BRIDGING shipped + hardened → THE CORPS IS COMPLETE** (the pontoon train; player-only/byte-identical; probe 22/22) — D89

> **The Tactical Engineering Corps is DONE — all 3 of Aaron's locked "all of the above" (entrench + abatis + pontoons).** A NEW **opt-in river** (`__FIELD.terrain.rivers[]`) actually gates movement: a river is impassable except at a **crossing** — a **shallow ford** (passable, slow + fatiguing), a **deep ford** (fordable only at Arcade; needs a bridge at Balanced/Historian), or a player-laid **pontoon bridge** (key **N** / "Pontoon" button): an infantry pioneer detail on the near bank lays a bridge over realism-scaled time, opening a fast crossing. Surfaced via the **Skirmish → Ground → "River crossing"** option (+ an `opts.river` sandbox flag for the probe). The **B-5 realism slider** scales sapper speed, ford slow, AND fordable-vs-requires-pontoon; the strategic **Engineer Works Corps** (`bridgeArmy.engineering`) lays bridges faster on a campaign launch (the A1 anchor, via `__FIELD.engCorpsSpeed`). 2D slate-water band + boat-deck pontoons + ford stepping-stones + labels (CVD-safe, reduceMotion-safe); 3D water plane + pontoon boats/deck; a citation-grade Fredericksburg/Rappahannock + James-River-1864 teaching card.
>
> **Architecture (byte-identical):** the active gate REUSES the two T0 seams reserved for it — `fldEngMoveFactor` (ford slow) + `fldEngMoveGate` (returns a clamped bank point for an illegal water step). New T13 fns: `fldEngInstallRiver`, `fldEngCrossAt`, `_fldRiverNearest/_fldRiverBankPoint/_fldEngBridgeSite`, `fldSelPontoon`, the pontoon loop in `fldEngStep`, `fldEngDrawWater2d`, `fld3dBuildWater` + pontoon sync in `fld3dSyncEng`. **No shipped scenario declares `terrain.rivers` and the AI never lays a pontoon** → every AI-vs-AI baseline byte-for-byte unchanged (every read gated by `rv && rv.length` / `engPontoons`; reset per-launch + per-phase). One T0 seam edit: `fldStepMovement` now settles a no-progress river clamp to HOLD (the bug-hunt's soft-deadlock fix) — `eg`-gated, so riverless baselines are identical.
>
> **DECISIONS (decide-&-log, no fork surfaced):** Bull Run's creek/Stone Bridge stay COSMETIC (making them live would regress a shipped, probed battle + break byte-identity); the active river is the opt-in feature, and the teaching card cites the Rappahannock as the historical anchor. AI river-pathing / AI-built works is the named T13 extension point (player-tool this increment, like D87/D88), so a river skirmish frames the PLAYER as the attacker forcing the crossing (river installed between the player's home edge and the objective, either side) while the AI defends the far bank.
>
> **Bug-hunt (19 agents, Opus): 8 confirmed + 6 critic, all fixed.** HIGH: the gate's origin test let a unit on an open ford **sidestep into deep water and wade the band** → block entering blocked water from anywhere except already-blocked water + a midpoint sample (tangential bends). HIGH: the **3D water plane was buried under the opaque terrain** → raised to a thin on-ground sheet (+ fords lifted, no z-fight). HIGH: a unit ordered across impassable water **marched in place forever, fatigue→100** → settle to HOLD at the bank. HIGH: the river skirmish was degenerate for a CS player → river placed by player side. MED: silent crossing refusal → aria-live halt announce + HUD line. LOW: dupe-span message; pontoon `side` + per-side cap.
>
> **Verification:** build GATE OK; `probe-engineering-corps` **22/22**, 0 pe; full no-regression **GREEN (21 probes byte-identical: field 13 · bullrun 15 · shiloh 29 · malvern 24 · antietam 16 · fredericksburg 22 · chancellorsville 24 · gettysburg 16 · vicksburg 18 · roster 8 · visuals ok [texWarn 0] · custom 9 · fog 13 · ai 15 · autopause 13 · campaign-link 16 · officers 15 · logistics 14 · arms 23 · presets 26 · csplayer 16; diag-classic `nonBlank:346`; boot/t1 ok; 0 pageerrors)**; WCAG-AA on all new UI (≥4.5:1, CVD-safe). Also added the long-missing B/X/N engineering keys to the help overlay.
>
> **Next (the Engineering Corps is complete):** Phase C tactical BREADTH — **Chickamauga / Chattanooga** (the Western theater continues after Shiloh + Vicksburg), then **C3 USCT battles** (the Crater · New Market Heights · Olustee · Nashville). Then D hex engine → E strategic S3–S5. **Resume map:** START-HERE.md → this block → WAKE-UP.md top block → newest RUN-LOG.md entry → DECISIONS.md D89 → `src/tactical/T13-engineering.js` (the INCREMENT 3 section: rivers + pontoons) + the T0 seams (grep `fldEng`/`rivers`/`engPontoons`) → `tools/probe-engineering-corps.mjs`.

## ⚡ CONTINUE HERE — 2026-06-21 **ENGINEERING CORPS — increment 2: ABATIS / OBSTACLES shipped + hardened** (the axe; player-only/byte-identical; probe 15/15) — D88

> **The Engineering Corps is 2 of 3 increments done.** A selected player infantry brigade now builds an **abatis** — a capped timber belt across its front (key **B** / "Abatis" button); enemies forcing it are slowed, disordered once per crossing (morale + fatigue), and fire raggedly until they recover; a nearby brigade **clears** a lane (key **X**). Build/clear time, slow, and disorder all read the **B-5 realism slider** (`fldEngRealism()`). 2D stake-line + 3D crossed-timber (fog-gated), HUD lines, and a Mahan-sourced teaching end-card. **Player-only → every AI-vs-AI baseline is byte-identical.**
>
> **Provenance:** the abatis code was authored 2026-06-20 but left uncommitted/unvetted when the prior **Codex** session ran out of usage (its only landed work was the browser-probe Seatbelt guard — committed separately as `679ee7d`). This session vetted it through the full gate + bug-hunt and pushed it (`02b2dd8`).
>
> **Architecture:** the abatis half of `src/tactical/T13-engineering.js` + guarded T0 seams (`fldMoveFactor` gains `u`, `fldResolveFire` fire factor, `fldStepMovement` move-gate, `fldInitSim` reset, `fldOnOver` end-card, DOM buttons + keys B/X) + a **T8 `_fldBuildPhase` per-phase reset** (`fldEngPhaseReset`). Every seam is an exact identity with no live belt.
>
> **Bug-hunt (16 agents, Opus): 8 confirmed + 4 critic, all fixed.** HIGH: belts leaked across phases in multi-phase battles (Vicksburg/Antietam/Gettysburg) → `fldEngPhaseReset` (keeps `_engUsed` for the end-card). MED: abandoned belts never decayed → fade like abandoned entrenchments. MED: render ignored fog → fog-gated 2D+3D. LOW: same-tick id collision → monotonic `_engObsSeq`. LOW: routing-unit morale dock → routing guard. **Kept (deliberate):** entrench+abatis on one brigade (historically correct — Fredericksburg/Cold Harbor apron) and one disorder shock per crossing.
>
> **Verification:** build GATE OK; `probe-engineering-corps` **15/15** (added: multi-phase reset, abandoned decay, unique ids, routing immunity); full no-regression **GREEN (50 probes, 0 pageerrors, diag-classic `nonBlank:346`)**. Also fixed a D86 leftover: `probe-custom-battle-builder` was red since Vicksburg shipped (EXPECTED registry omitted `vicksburg`) → 9/9.
>
> **Next (the LAST engineering increment):** **PONTOON bridging** — a NEW river/water terrain feature + a bridge-laying engineer step + a crossing gate (fordable vs requires-pontoon, realism-gated); the Bull Run terrain already has Young's Branch / the Stone Bridge to build against. Then C tactical breadth (Chickamauga/Chattanooga, C3 USCT). **Resume map:** START-HERE.md → this block → WAKE-UP.md top block → newest RUN-LOG.md entry → DECISIONS.md D88 → `src/tactical/T13-engineering.js` (header + the abatis fns) + the T0/T8 seams (grep `T13`/`fldEng`) → `tools/probe-engineering-corps.mjs`.

## ⚡ CONTINUE HERE — 2026-06-18 Custom Battle Builder C4 complete ✅ — single-phase authoring, import/export, slots, launch gate green

> **Custom Battle Builder C4 is built and verified.** New `src/tactical/T11-custom-battle.js` adds a first-class main-menu builder flow for player-authored real-time tactical scenarios. It covers scenario name/date/place, sides/briefs, objective, field size, terrain hills/woods/walls/markers, OOB units, artillery/gun counts, reinforcement/deployment timing, optional leaders, supply trains, teaching cards, provenance, JSON import/export, shareable JSON, and six local persistence slots.
>
> **Architecture decision:** C4 is intentionally **single-phase V1**. Phase authoring remains deferred until a UI for `phases[]` can be proven as safe as authored historical phase data. Custom scenarios do **not** enter `fldScenarioRegistry()`; `fldScenarioData(id)` only falls back to `fldCustomScenarioData(id)` for explicit `custom_...` IDs. That preserves the exact historical battle roster while still launching valid custom scenarios through the same single-phase `fldScenarioInit` data path. Field size is reset every launch via `FLD.BASE_FIELD_W/H`, then a scenario may apply `field.w/h` safely.
>
> **Validation guardrails:** required metadata, safe unique IDs, malformed/unsafe JSON keys, duplicate IDs, bounds and numeric clamps, objective placement, reinforcement timing, side ownership, leader attach ownership, realistic artillery crew counts (12-40 per gun), forbidden combat/damage keys, and `phases[]` are all blocked. Custom artillery uses the universal `guns` model only; no per-custom-battle damage fudge was added.
>
> **Focused probe:** `tools/probe-custom-battle-builder.mjs` verifies builder render, validation failures, valid scenario creation, export/import round trip, UI malformed-import rejection, slot save/list/load/delete, explicit custom-ID launch contract, universal gun model, field-size reset, no phase leak, no Classic `G.battle`, and no historical registry contamination. Current result: **9/9 steps ok, 0 fail**, with `tools/shots/probe-custom-battle-builder.json/png`.
>
> **Verification:** `node tools/build.mjs` → GATE OK; `node tools/probe-custom-battle-builder.mjs` → 9/9; `node tools/probe-tactical-roster.mjs` → 8/8; `node tools/probe-field.mjs` → 13/13; `node tools/probe-bullrun.mjs` → 15/15; `node tools/probe-malvern-hill.mjs` → 24/24; `node tools/probe-antietam.mjs` → 16/16; `node tools/probe-fredericksburg.mjs` → 22/22; `node tools/probe-chancellorsville.mjs` → 24/24; `node tools/probe-gettysburg.mjs` → 16/16; `node tools/probe-shiloh.mjs` → 29/29; `node tools/probe-officers.mjs` → 15/15; `node tools/probe-logistics.mjs` → 14/14; `node tools/probe-arms.mjs` → 23/23; `node tools/probe-presets.mjs` → 26/26; `node tools/probe-csplayer.mjs` → 16/16; `node tools/probe-campaign-link.mjs` → 16/16; `node tools/probe-tactical-visuals.mjs` → 6/6; `node tools/bootprobe.mjs` → ok=true with known/tolerated 404 resource console entries; `node tools/t1probe.mjs` → ok=true; `node tools/diag-classic.mjs` → Classic paints, nonBlank 346; `git diff --check` → clean.
>
> **Docs/checklist status:** `RUN-LOG.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, `WAKE-UP.md`, `MASTER-TASK-LIST.md`, `V1-CHECKLIST.md`, and `DECISIONS.md` now mark C4 complete. Eastern marquee C1 remains complete; C2 Western still has Vicksburg, Chickamauga/Chattanooga, Atlanta/March, Franklin/Nashville; C3 USCT battles remain open.
>

D145 supersedes it: `START-HERE.md` + `V1-CHECKLIST.md` are live, 5.5 owns architecture/history/UX/accessibility/final integration, and helpers only receive explicit model+effort bounded packets. Loot status remains explicit: Armory/Cannon have loot-style rarity tiers, but standalone Phase I loot/survival is not built.

The in-battle tactical help dialog is now aria-modal, focusable, Tab-trapped to its close control, and Escape closes the dialog without also exiting the battle.
>
> **HUD declutter shipped:** the giant pause overlay is now a small top-right status chip; the tactical top bar is split into compact title/clock/phase/objective/status chips; multi-phase sector state moved into a dedicated `#fldSector` chip; objective text no longer repeats the huge phase label; reinforcement banners are narrower, shorter-lived, and ellipsized with a tooltip.

The battle is a single-phase Jackson flank-march scenario with CS attacker / US defender, Chancellor crossroads objective, Wilderness/Hazel Grove/Fairview/breastworks terrain, gun-model artillery, leaders/fates, reinforcements, side cards, teaching/codex, deterministic AI-vs-AI resolution, CS-favored-but-not-scripted balance, and no Classic contamination.

## ⚡ CONTINUE HERE — run k (2026-06-16): **PHASE C-1.5 · THE UNIVERSAL ARTILLERY GUN MODEL ✅ SHIPPED + VETTED + PUSHED** → IMMEDIATE NEXT = ANTIETAM as the MULTI-PHASE epic (D74 #1/#2)

> **The D74 "universal-combat" artillery cleanup — the FIRST Antietam sub-milestone (do FIRST) — is complete** (DECISIONS **D75**). Fredericksburg's per-battle **"volume-of-fire proxy men"** fudge (the C-1/D73 batteries inflated `men` to 500/440/500 to fake the massed crest fire) is REPLACED by a CONSISTENT gun-count model used identically in every battle. Deliverable ≈**1749.6KB**.
>
> **✅ THE GUN MODEL (T5):** `FLDA.GUN_FIRE_WEIGHT` = 27 (men-equivalent fire-strength per gun, UNIVERSAL). `fldArtFireStr(u)` returns the fire-strength numerator (`fldResolveFire` divides by 1500): a battery with a GUN COUNT → `guns * 27 * clamp(men/maxMen)` (rated fire from the gun count, crew-scaled so a battery fades smoothly as cannoneers fall); infantry / arms-off / a LEGACY no-`guns` battery → `u.men` EXACTLY as before. `guns` carried data→unit via `fldMakeUnit` (T0) + `fldBrSpec` (T1); wired at `fldResolveFire`'s `strF`. CALIBRATED so a 6-gun battery → 162 ≈ Bull Run's realistic 160-men/6-gun Griffin & Ricketts → the model REPRODUCES the existing batteries (consistent, not merely gated). The gun count is army-size-invariant "hardware" (strategic strength flows through the Cannon-Corps `_canisterScale`, not raw volume — documented).
>
> **✅ BYTE-IDENTITY:** the gun path is reached ONLY by a battery declaring `guns` (Fredericksburg's 3 crest batteries); every baseline has none → `fldArtFireStr` returns `u.men` → byte-identical. **field 13 · bullrun 15 · fog 13 · autopause 13 · ai 15 · campaign-link 16 · officers 15 · logistics 14 · presets 26 · csplayer 16 · arms 23** — all byte-identical, 0 pe; bootprobe ok; t1probe 14; diag-classic **346**.
>
> **✅ FREDERICKSBURG RE-TUNED VIA HONEST DESIGN (not fudge):** the 3 batteries now carry real gun counts (Washington Artillery 9 · Marye's grand battery 16 · Alexander 9 = **34 historical guns**) + realistic crew (200/350/200). The CS edge EMERGES from the wall's cover + the massed gun count + the cautious-attacker-dies-exposed geometry. RE-VET: **CS 8/8**, casualty ratio **2.06:1** (better than the proxy's 1.91), the wall measurably **load-bearing** (cuts CS loss 18-23%, 5/5 CS wins), the crest guns **survive 8/8** + fire — **probe-fredericksburg 22/22, 0 pe**. Bug-hunt: **0 confirmed finder bugs + 5 critic gaps ALL FIXED** (the `u.men0` probe typo → maxMen; a battery-survival guard; a crew-scaling assertion; the campaign-semantics + legacy-HUD notes). WCAG clean.
>
> **IMMEDIATE NEXT = ANTIETAM (Sep 17, 1862) as the MULTI-PHASE epic (D74 #1/#2).** Build the NEW gated `phases[]` engine capability (sequential phase objectives; default single-objective path = Fredericksburg/Bull Run byte-identical) → use it for the 3 phases: **the Cornfield → the Sunken Road "Bloody Lane" → Burnside's Bridge**. The citation-grade research **dossier is drafted** (`.tmp/antietam-dossier.md`, 8-agent workflow, anti-Lost-Cause, corrections applied) and the **engine design is drafted** (`.tmp/B-antietam-multiphase-design.md`: discrete sequential sub-battles, casualty+score carry-over, weighted-aggregate scoring with a draw band, the inter-phase broadsheet transition card). Author `data/antietam.json` (gun-model artillery) + one `fldScenarioRegistry` line + `tools/probe-antietam.mjs`. Standalone menu battle (D74 #4). **PUSH per milestone after PROPER VETTING (D53-OPS). Subagent models = HYBRID (D63).**
