## ⚡ CONTINUE HERE — run k (2026-06-16): **PHASE C-1 · FREDERICKSBURG (Marye's Heights) ✅ SHIPPED + VETTED + PUSHED** → NEXT = the next Eastern marquee battle (Antietam or Gettysburg, D69)

> **C-1 — Fredericksburg, the SECOND data-driven tactical battle — is complete** (DECISIONS **D73**). The tactical engine (mature after B1–B6) is now BATTLE-AGNOSTIC: a new historical battle = a `data/<battle>.json` file + one registry line, NO new combat code. New `data/fredericksburg.json` + `tools/probe-fredericksburg.mjs` + the T1/T7 data-driven scenario-UI generalization + a small gated T0 engine seam. Deliverable ≈**1743.6KB**.
>
> **✅ C-1 — the data-driven scenario spine + Fredericksburg:** `fldScenarioRegistry()`/`fldScenarioData(id)` (T1) map any id → `GAME_DATA.<battle>`; `fldScenarioInit` builds ANY registered id from the same shape (Bull Run byte-identical). The UI is generalized: `fldInjectScenarioButtons` iterates the registry (Bull Run keeps its bespoke marquee button; others build from `data.menu`) and returns the last button so the skirmish/preset buttons anchor after the block; `fldLaunchBattle` fires the briefing for ANY scenario; `fldScenarioSideChoice(id,go)` reads `sd.sides` (a per-battle side-choice card; `fldBullRunSideChoice` is now an alias); `fldBriefObjectiveHtml` keeps the keyword skeleton + a data flavor (`sd.brief`); `fldScenarioEndHtml` keeps the Bull Run branch byte-identical (shared `_fldScenarioEndBox`) + a data `sd.endNote` branch. `bullrun.json` gained inert `sides`/`brief`.
>
> **✅ THE DOOMED-FRONTAL-ASSAULT POSTURE (the headline mechanic — a scenario-gated T0 seam):** the B-1 attacker AI is *competent* (fog-off it column-rushes + presses the mass bayonet), which Fredericksburg's assault was NOT. `data.assaultDoctrine:"cautious"` → `__FIELD._atkCautious` makes the AI attacker advance IN LINE across the open glacis and trade but never column-rush nor carry the wall (Burnside's piecemeal disaster). `var aggressive = !__FIELD.fog && !__FIELD._atkCautious` gates the assault/column branches → **default off → Bull Run/sandbox/skirmish/campaign BYTE-IDENTICAL.** A HUMAN Union player is unbound (the alt-history hook). Fredericksburg keeps **fog OFF** (the morning fog had LIFTED — clear fields of fire) and wins the defense by the cautious posture + the stone wall (cover 1.7) + massed crest canister — a DIFFERENT, battle-faithful lever from Bull Run's fog (no contradiction of "fog aids the defender").
>
> **VETTING CAUGHT A REAL HIGH (fixed pre-commit):** the bug-hunt (5 diverse-lens Opus finders × default-refute verify + a completeness critic → 4 confirmed + 5 critic gaps) empirically traced that `fldAiDefender` holds the objective's FORWARD FACE (`obj.z+obj.r*0.55`) — my first geometry put that forward of the wall, so the AI marched the defending line OFF the wall into the open on tick 1 (the 1.7 cover unused; the static-terrain probe missed it). **Fixed (the Bull Run pattern):** `obj.z` 375→331 (the forward face now lands ON the wall in cover) + `ATK_CAUTIOUS_HOLD` 0.6→0.85 (the cautious attacker holds in the OPEN just outside the wall's cover band — only the attacker exposed). Added a LIVE cover assertion to the probe. Also fixed: the side-card aria role hardwired US=attack → threaded the data `attacking` flag.
>
> **✅ FULLY VETTED:** GATE OK (1743.6KB) → **probe-fredericksburg 20/20, 0 pe** → full no-regression GREEN (field 13 · bullrun 15 · fog 13 · autopause 13 · ai 15 · campaign-link 16 · officers 15 · logistics 14 · arms 23 · presets 26 — all BYTE-IDENTICAL; **csplayer 16/16**; bootprobe ok; t1probe 14/14; diag-classic nonBlank **346**; strategic spot-check green) → balance A/B (**CS 8/8**; the slaughter **≈1.91:1** Union casualties; the wall load-bearing; the defending line holds the wall in cover live while the attacker dies exposed on the open glacis) → bug-hunt fixed + re-verified → wcag clean (0 fixes; 1 pre-existing global `.title-sub`/`--rule` contrast issue noted for the Phase-E WCAG pass). Committed + pushed.
>
> **What's playable:** a new main-menu **⚔ BATTLE — FREDERICKSBURG (1862)** button opens a side-choice card — lead the Union assault across the open glacis (and try to do what the Army of the Potomac could not), or hold the stone wall for the Confederacy as charge after charge breaks before it. The AI assault is the doomed frontal attack; the wall + the pre-sighted crest guns repel it. (The ⚔ FIRST BULL RUN button + the ⚔ SKIRMISH + ⚙ COMMAND & REALISM menus are unchanged.)
>
> **IMMEDIATE NEXT = ANTIETAM (Sep 17, 1862) as a MULTI-PHASE epic (DECISIONS D74).** Four Aaron-locked decisions clear the next runs: **(1)** next battle = **Antietam** (chronological; the Cornfield → the Sunken Road "Bloody Lane" → Burnside's Bridge); **(2)** scope = **multi-phase epics** — build a NEW gated engine capability (sequential phases/objectives in one scenario, a `phases[]` data block; default = the single-objective path → Fredericksburg/Bull Run byte-identical) and use it for Antietam's three phases; **(3)** the **UNIVERSAL-COMBAT BALANCE PRINCIPLE** — ONE shared, consistent per-unit damage model across EVERY battle (never tuned per-battle); outcomes EMERGE from scenario DESIGN (real OOB/terrain/cover/tactics); casualty ratios follow the player's/AI's tactics, not a forced historical number, but historical tactics → ~the historical result. **DO FIRST:** replace Fredericksburg's inflated artillery "volume-of-fire proxy men" (a per-battle fudge that violates D74) with a CONSISTENT gun-count/battery-weight artillery model, re-tune FB via geometry/OOB (not fudge), and RE-VET; **(4)** new battles stay **standalone menu battles** (no new campaign wiring). Then the rest of C1 → **C2 Western · C3 USCT · C4 the custom-battle builder**. **PUSH per milestone after PROPER VETTING (D53-OPS). Subagent models = HYBRID (D63): set model+effort EXPLICITLY — Sonnet low/med on mechanical legs (Explore/wcag/summarize), Opus high ONLY on bug-hunt finders/verifiers/critic.**
>
> **Resume map:** `START-HERE.md` → `V1-CHECKLIST.md` Phase C (C1 partially ticked — Fredericksburg ✅) → `DECISIONS.md` **D73** (C-1) + **D69** (Phase-C order) + **D72** (B-6) + **D67** (fog-ON precedent) → the data-driven spine in `src/tactical/T1-bull-run.js` (`fldScenarioRegistry`/`fldScenarioData`/`fldScenarioInit`/`fldInjectScenarioButtons`/`fldScenarioEndHtml`) + `src/tactical/T7-command-side.js` (`fldScenarioSideChoice`/`fldBriefObjectiveHtml`) + the cautious-attacker seam in `src/tactical/T0-field-sandbox.js` (`fldAiAttacker` + `__FIELD._atkCautious` + `FLD.ATK_CAUTIOUS_HOLD`) + `data/fredericksburg.json` (the authored template) + `data/bullrun.json` (the reference) + `tools/probe-fredericksburg.mjs`. ## ⚡ CONTINUE HERE — run k (2026-06-16): **PHASE B-6 · CS-PLAYER "COMMAND EITHER SIDE" ✅ SHIPPED + VETTED + PUSHED — PHASE B COMPLETE** → NEXT = Phase C tactical breadth (Eastern marquee first)

> **PHASE B-6 — command EITHER army — is complete** (DECISIONS **D72**), and with it **Phase B (tactical depth) is DONE (B1–B6).** New module `src/tactical/T7-command-side.js` + surgical seams in T0/T1/T3 + `tools/probe-csplayer.mjs`. Deliverable ≈**1702KB**.
>
> **✅ B-6 — the CS-player tactical mode:** you can now command EITHER side in a real-time battle. Pick the Confederacy and you **DEFEND** Henry House Hill while the **B-1 attacker AI** presses you uphill — which is the whole point of fork #10 (it makes the B-1 doctrine player-facing). B-6 is a GENERALIZATION, not a new sim layer: `__FIELD.playerSide` is made authoritative in `fldInitSim` (precedence: explicit `opts.playerSide` > skirmish `playerSide` > a CS campaign via `_fldCamp().side` > `"US"`); the input layer (`fldPlayerSel`/select-all/cycle/drag) + the render/HUD **fog VIEWER** (2D markers, ghosts, 3D sync, friend/foe line) were a literal `"US"` → now read `fldPlayerSide()`; `fldBrSpec` (T1) flips the scenario AI flags off the player side; `fldPlayerSide()` (T3) resolves a CS **campaign LIVE** from `G.campaign.side` first, then the explicit per-launch side. New T7 UI: a period **side-choice card** (Lead the Union–ATTACK / Hold for the Confederacy–DEFEND), a side-aware briefing/objective + "your war vs history" + end-screen "you" line, a player-home-edge 3D camera, a dignified full-name friend/foe HUD ("4 Confederate vs 1 Union").
>
> **VETTING (the headline):** vetting caught a **real CS-campaign regression** I introduced — the first `fldPlayerSide()` rewrite trusted a stale `__FIELD.playerSide` and broke the officer-roster/fog contract (the roster/fog read it mid-setup, before a relaunch); `probe-officers` flagged it → fixed by resolving a CS campaign LIVE from `G.campaign.side`. Then the **52-agent bug-hunt → 11 confirmed + 7 critic gaps**: fixed **(HIGH)** a skirmish "Fight Again" soft-lock for a CS player (rematch now REPLAYS the stashed launch spec + the sandbox OOB assigns `ai` by playerSide), **(MED)** a US-hardwired 3D camera, **(MED)** a fog-leak via reinforcement banners/announce/auto-pause (now gated on player-visibility, fog-OFF byte-identical), **(MED)** a render-fog probe gap (added a deterministic step), + LOW polish; deferred-with-note the pre-existing/convention items.
>
> **✅ FULLY VETTED:** GATE OK (1702.3KB) → **probe-csplayer 16/16, 0 pe** → full no-regression GREEN (10 tactical baselines byte-identical · bootprobe ok · t1probe 14/14 · diag-classic 346) → CS defense winnable 8/8 fog-ON + **live stacked still CS 8/8 (B-6 did not move the balance)** → wcag clean (2 contrast fixes). Committed + pushed.
>
> **What's playable:** the **⚔ FIRST BULL RUN** menu button now opens a side-choice card — lead the Union assault (history) or **hold the hill for the Confederacy** against the B-1 attacker AI; the ⚔ SKIRMISH side pick is fully wired; a CS campaign commands the CS. The CS defense is winnable, the field renders from your side, fog shows only what YOU scout, and the briefing/HUD/end-screen all speak from the army you command.
>
> **IMMEDIATE NEXT = PHASE C · TACTICAL BREADTH (D69):** more real-time battles on the data-driven engine, **Eastern marquee FIRST** — Antietam, Fredericksburg, Gettysburg, Chancellorsville (then Western C2, USCT C3, the custom-battle builder C4). The engine is mature (B1–B6); C is mostly new scenario DATA (OOBs + terrain + reinforcement timelines + teaching cards) on the proven T0–T7 stack, the bullrun1 pattern. **PUSH per milestone after PROPER VETTING (D53-OPS). Subagent models = HYBRID (D63).** Also this session: the Gemini-report review (D71) — keep our engine/assistant/art choices; 3 Phase-H/publish notes folded in.
>
> **Resume map:** `START-HERE.md` → `V1-CHECKLIST.md` (Phase A + B1–B6 all ticked; Phase C next) → `DECISIONS.md` **D72** (B-6) + **D71** (the Gemini-report review) + **D70/D69/D67/D66/D65/D64/D62/D61** → `src/tactical/T7-command-side.js` (`fldBullRunSideChoice`/`fldBriefObjectiveHtml`/`fldPlayerOutcomeLine`/`_fldSideName`) + the seams (grep `fldPlayerSide`/`__FIELD.playerSide` across T0/T1/T3) + `tools/probe-csplayer.mjs`. ## ⚡ CONTINUE HERE — run k (2026-06-16): **PHASE B-5 · DIFFICULTY/REALISM PRESETS ✅ SHIPPED + VETTED + PUSHED** → NEXT = B-6 CS-player "command either side"

> **PHASE B-5 — the Command & Realism layer — is complete** (DECISIONS **D70**). New gated `src/tactical/T6-presets.js` + guarded seams in T0/T3/T4/T5 + a Difficulty row in the T2 skirmish setup + `tools/probe-presets.mjs`. Deliverable ≈**1684KB**.
>
> **✅ B-5 — difficulty + realism presets:** **TWO AXES** — an **AI tier** (Recruit / Regular / Veteran / Hardee) × a **realism bundle** (Arcade / Balanced / Historian), plus an **"Advanced"** per-lever expander. Read ONCE at `fldInitSim` (`fldPresetsApply`, before the fog/auto-pause precedence) → sets `__FIELD.sev`{attrition·canister·supply·cmdShock·sight·veteran} + aiSkill/aiResolve/aiCushion → **WIRES the already-existing B-1..B-4 knobs** (attrition→fire/melee cas · canister→`fldArtFireMult` · supply→`fldSupplyReserve` · cmdShock→`fldOfficerFalls` · sight→`fldUnitSight` · veteran→`fldMoraleStep` · aiSkill→`fldAiAttacker`/`fldAiDefender` thresholds · the Recruit cushion+brittle-enemy→`fldMoraleStep`). Mostly WIRING + a UI, not new sim (D69).
>
> **SMARTER-NOT-CHEATING, CODE-ENFORCED:** `aiResolve` HARD-capped ≤1.0 (never an enemy steadiness buff — in `_fldClampCfg`, not just the data table); the player cushion exists ONLY at a GENUINE Recruit (dropped if the AI-sharpness chip is tuned off the tier); **Hardee = sharper DECISIONS (aiSkill), not stat bonuses**; **Veteran = aiSkill 1.0 / no handicap = the shipped opponent.** **BYTE-IDENTITY:** no preset → NEUTRAL config (all 1.0 / 0), every seam ×1.0/+0 → the 9 tactical baselines stay byte-identical; **"Balanced" == today's shipped CS 8/8 (live stacked, fog ON) SEED-FOR-SEED.** The tactical **Engineering Corps stays SPLIT** (D69) — B-5 ships the sliders + hooks, not the corps.
>
> **✅ FULLY VETTED:** adversarial bug-hunt **76 agents → 13 confirmed + 6 critic gaps, ALL FIXED** (HIGH: drawer-Escape-tore-down-the-battle → `stopPropagation`+`fldKey` guard; HIGH: picker ARIA radiogroup→group+aria-pressed; MED: a malformed-preset clamp `_fldClampCfg`; MED: the fog-"scenario" V-toggle wipe → no-op at apply; MED: drawer focus-jump/stacked-listener/resume-behind-modal; + 7 probe gaps). **probe-presets 26/26, 0 pe** · full no-regression GREEN (9 tactical baselines byte-identical · bootprobe ok · t1probe 14/14 · diag-classic 346) · wcag clean (3 contrast fixes). Committed + pushed.
>
> **What's playable:** a new main-menu **"⚙ COMMAND & REALISM"** button + a Difficulty row in the ⚔ SKIRMISH setup open a period-broadsheet picker (pick an AI tier × realism bundle, or open Advanced for per-lever chips); the choice persists and governs EVERY real-time battle (campaign "Fight in real time", skirmish, the standalone Sandbox + First Bull Run). In any battle, the control bar's **"⚙ Settings"** (key **G**) opens a focus-trapped drawer with live fog/auto-pause/speed toggles + the current difficulty. Pick **Recruit × Arcade** for the gentle on-ramp, **Hardee × Historian** for the unforgiving real thing — the AI never cheats.
>
> **IMMEDIATE NEXT = PHASE B-6 · CS-player "command either side"** (fork #10 — `fldPlayerSide` already honored from B-2): command EITHER side, so you DEFEND as the CS while the B-1 attacker AI presses you. Makes the attacker doctrine player-facing; doubles tactical replayability. Then **Phase C** breadth (Eastern marquee first — Antietam/Fredericksburg/Gettysburg/Chancellorsville on the data-driven engine, D69). **PUSH per milestone after PROPER VETTING (D53-OPS). Subagent models = HYBRID (D63).**

## ⚡ CONTINUE HERE — run k (2026-06-15): **PHASE B-4 · DISTINCT ARM ROLES ✅ SHIPPED + VETTED + PUSHED** → NEXT = B-5 difficulty/realism presets

> **PHASE B-4 — artillery + cavalry get real ROLES — is complete** (DECISIONS **D67**). New `src/tactical/T5-arms.js` + guarded T0/T1/T2 seams + `data/bullrun.json` (Stuart `role:flank`, `defaultFog:true`) + `tools/probe-arms.mjs`. Deliverable ≈**1646KB**.
>
> **✅ B-4 — the arms of the service:** **Artillery** fires CANISTER up close (a shotgun — murderous in the open, defeated by works/woods) and a long-range BOMBARDMENT beyond; `ARM.art`=0.35 → overrun in melee; the **battery doctrine is ASYMMETRIC** — a DEFENDING battery sits sited-safe + displaces early, an ATTACKING battery pushes FORWARD to a canister post and is catchable (Griffin/Ricketts, whose loss broke the Union attack). **Cavalry** runs scout / flank / screen / **raid** (a raider drains the enemy B-3 ammunition train); mounted shock (1.4) shatters a disordered line but breaks on a braced one (0.9). The **Cannon Corps you BUY** conditions the field battery (Napoleon→canister, Whitworth→long-range, via `_fldArtProfile`/the A1 bridge). Gated behind `__FIELD.arms` (sticky `_armsOff` → the 8 baselines, incl. bullrun1's art+cav, byte-identical).
>
> **TWO THINGS VETTING CAUGHT (the headline):** **(1) a real BYTE-IDENTITY bug the design missed** — the FROZEN base engine defines `const ARM={…melee…}` at `build/base.html:634` (art 0.40 / cav 1.05), so the old melee was reading REAL values; my first refactor dropped them to 1.0 when arms-off → shifted the balance. **Fixed:** arms-OFF now falls back to the base `ARM` table (verified vs the committed build — bare & logistics both CS 5/8, byte-identical). **(2) a stacked-balance FORK (surfaced to Aaron):** the per-layer probes each isolate the others, so the STACKED live config (officers+logistics+arms) was never measured — it's **Union-favoured in clear weather** (committed D66 already CS 3/8 fog-OFF; B-4 → 0/8). Fog-ON is CS 8/8 ("fog aids the defender" intact). **Aaron's call: First Bull Run now DEFAULTS to FOG ON** (the historically faithful battle; live stacked config → CS 8/8). Fog stays a toggle; an explicit setting still wins.
>
> **✅ FULLY VETTED:** adversarial bug-hunt **31 agents → 2 distinct confirmed (3D-mesh-on-reinforcement, fog HUD leak) + 6 critic gaps, ALL FIXED** (incl. 6 new probe steps). **probe-arms 23/23, 0 pe** · full no-regression GREEN (8 baselines byte-identical · bootprobe ok · t1probe 14/14 · diag-classic 346 · strategic spot-check green) · wcag clean. Balance: arms-only fog-OFF CS 7/8 · **LIVE stacked default (fog ON) CS 8/8**. Committed + pushed.
>
> **What's playable:** unchanged entry points (campaign "Fight in real time ⚔" · the ⚔ SKIRMISH menu · the standalone Sandbox + First Bull Run). The difference: **batteries and cavalry fight as themselves** — a battery flays a line in the open with canister but is overrun if you let infantry reach it; the forward Union guns at Henry House Hill are exposed (history); cavalry scouts, screens, flanks, charges a broken line, and can raid the enemy trains. **First Bull Run now opens under fog** (press **V** to clear it) — smoke and broken ground, where the defender holds the hill.
>
> **IMMEDIATE NEXT = PHASE B-5 · difficulty/realism presets — DESIGN CLEARED (DECISIONS D69):** new gated `src/tactical/T6-presets.js` read at `fldInitSim` from `G.settings.tacticalPreset` + per-lever values → sets the per-layer gates/severities (existing knobs → WIRING + a UI). **Curated 4-5 sliders + an "Advanced" expander** (AI level · fog · attrition · supply strictness · canister lethality; expander = command-shock/auto-pause/LOS/AI-leashes/xp). **Ladder = Recruit/Regular/Veteran/Hardee × Arcade/Balanced/Historian; "Balanced" = today's shipped balance (the byte-identical default, incl. Bull-Run-fog-ON).** Smarter-not-cheating (an easy-tier cushion only). `probe-presets.mjs` asserts neutral==today + each preset's gates. **The tactical ENGINEERING CORPS is SPLIT to its OWN later milestone** (Aaron's directive; consumes these sliders; memory `civilwar-directive-tactical-engineering-sliders`). Then **B6** CS-player "command either side" (fork #10) → **Phase C** breadth (Eastern marquee first — D69). **D68 = a 30-question design Q&A**: expansive v1, zoomable scale, wide-open alt-history + deeper citations, custom-builder-early, loot→core-pillar; **hex=Classic (no new engine)**, AA-essentials, tooltips-onboarding. **PUSH per milestone after PROPER VETTING (D53-OPS). Subagent models = HYBRID (D63).**

## ⚡ CONTINUE HERE — run k (2026-06-15): **PHASE B-3 · IN-BATTLE LOGISTICS ✅ SHIPPED + VETTED + PUSHED** → NEXT = B-4 distinct arm roles

> **PHASE B-3 — the ammunition economy on the real-time field — is complete** (DECISIONS **D66**). New `src/tactical/T4-logistics.js` + guarded T0 seams + the Bull Run `supply` block + `tools/probe-logistics.mjs`. Deliverable ≈**1608KB**.
>
> **✅ B-3 — supply trains + resupply + exhaustion:** each side fields a rear **ammunition train** with a finite **reserve**; a DISENGAGED brigade within reach refills (draining the reserve); a brigade OUT of cartridges holding the objective fires dry → bayonet (the 20th Maine); a SPENT brigade (fatigue>84) drags. The **attacker's train sits far behind its start line** (it outruns its supply) and the **defender's just behind the objective** → balance-neutral. **Ties strategic supply:** the reserve scales with `bridgeArmy().supply`; a raid-supply order cuts the enemy's. Gated + BYTE-IDENTICAL behind `__FIELD.logistics` (the 7 baselines + officers set sticky `_logisticsOff`).
>
> **THE FOG FIX (the headline — a HIGH bug-hunt finding, surfaced to Aaron):** with **fog ON**, logistics first INVERTED "fog aids the defender" (D58/D64) — Bull Run AI-vs-AI fog-ON **CS 8/8 → 0/8** (under fog the attacker cycled fresh brigades to its safe far-rear train and out-sustained the long fire-trade). Aaron's first pick (a defender's-depot rule) provably did nothing (the defender wasn't the ammo-constrained side); the fix that works (Aaron-approved) is **choking the ATTACKER's resupply under fog** (`ATK_FOG_RESUPPLY`=0 — an attacker advancing blind can't run its trains forward). **Result: logistics is balance-NEUTRAL in BOTH fog states — fog-OFF CS 5/8, fog-ON CS 8/8** (each = the no-logistics baseline; fog-aids-defender preserved). A FOG REGRESSION GUARD probe step locks it.
>
> **✅ FULLY VETTED:** adversarial bug-hunt **64 agents → 9 confirmed + 2 critic gaps, ALL ADDRESSED** (1 HIGH fog + 6 LOW probe-fidelity/correctness: probe boundary test, two-sided balance band, asymmetry + fog-reveal + supply-curve probe steps, reinforcement-only-side train). **probe-logistics 14/14, 0 pe** · full no-regression GREEN (7 baselines byte-identical · bootprobe ok · t1probe 14/14 · diag-classic 346) · wcag clean (1 HUD-contrast fix). Committed + pushed.
>
> **What's playable:** unchanged entry points (campaign "Fight in real time ⚔" · the ⚔ SKIRMISH menu · the standalone Sandbox + First Bull Run). The difference: each side now has a rear **ammunition train** (a ❈ wagon marker + a faint resupply ring); a brigade out of close action near its train refills, the reserve is finite, and a brigade out of cartridges on the objective fires dry then bayonets; the HUD shows the reserve ("ample / running low / …") + a selected brigade's ammo/resupply/spent status. Turn on Fog (V) and the defender still holds the hill — the attacker outruns its supply.
>
> **IMMEDIATE NEXT = PHASE B (continued):** **B4** distinct arm roles (artillery canister/long-range; cavalry scout/flank/screen/raid) — **design drafted** (`.tmp/B4-arm-roles-design.md`: the `ARM`-melee dangling hook to fill; Bull Run already fields Griffin/Ricketts as `art` + Stuart as `cav`; the Cannon-Corps→field-battery bridge; the cav-raid↔B-3-train tie) · **B5** difficulty/realism presets · **B6** the CS-player "command either side" mode (fork #10). **B5+B6 also sketched** (`.tmp/B5-B6-design-sketch.md`). **PUSH per milestone after PROPER VETTING (D53-OPS). Subagent models = HYBRID (D63).**
>
> **Resume map:** `START-HERE.md` → `V1-CHECKLIST.md` (Phase A + B1 + B2 + B3 ticked; B4 next) → `DECISIONS.md` **D66** (B-3) + **D65/D64/D62/D63/D61** → `src/tactical/T4-logistics.js` (`FLDL` incl. `ATK_FOG_RESUPPLY`; `fldBuildSupply`/`fldSupplyRearPos`/`fldLogisticsStep`/`fldUnitInCloseAction`/`fldLogisticsAiUnit`) + `tools/probe-logistics.mjs`. Everything is on disk and pushed.
>

## ⚡ CONTINUE HERE — run k (2026-06-15): **PHASE B-2 · OFFICERS & COMMAND ✅ SHIPPED + VETTED + PUSHED** → NEXT = Phase B continues (B3 logistics · B4 arm roles · B5 presets · B6 CS-player)

> **PHASE B-2 — leadership ON the field — is complete** (DECISIONS **D65**; charter fork: officers/command). New `src/tactical/T3-officers.js` + guarded T0 seams + the real Bull Run command cast in `data/bullrun.json.leaders`.
>
> **✅ B-2 — `fldOfficersStep` + the officer layer:** named leaders ride with their army and project a **COMMAND RADIUS** — brigades inside it recover morale faster, hold longer against the rout (capped save), and rally sooner — and **CAN BE HIT**: exposure near the firing line → wound (aura halves) → fall (aura dies + a one-time **command shock**, the general-down rout trigger). **Ties the named-generals layer (S2 m5):** a campaign commander's quality + name come from `bridgeArmy().leadership` / `cmdActiveGeneral()`; scenarios carry the cast (McDowell/Heintzelman vs Beauregard/Johnston/Bee/Bartow/Jackson). **Gated + BYTE-IDENTICAL:** behind `__FIELD.officers`; the 6 existing probes set sticky `_officersOff` → `(u.cmdBonus||0)`=0 → all baselines unchanged. **Bug-hunt: 52 agents → 1 confirmed + 7 critic gaps, ALL FIXED** — the big one was an over-lethal death model (army commanders who *survived* First Bull Run died every game); fixed via per-leader **historical `fate`** + risk **decay** + wound recovery + a **cover** term (the Jackson reverse-slope lesson) → army cdrs now survive **10/10**; also the Bee/Jackson anachronism (gated + contested framing), the CS-player hardcoding (`fldPlayerSide`, prep for B6), a balance moderation, and fog memory for fallen leaders. **Balance (officers ON, Bull Run AI-vs-AI):** CS **7/8** fog-OFF (the Henry House Hill command stand; US still breaks 1/8) · **8/8 fog-ON** (fog still aids the defender). **probe-officers 15/15**; full no-regression GREEN (6 baselines byte-identical, diag-classic 346, boot/t1, 0 pageerrors). Deliverable ≈**1585KB**.
>
> **What's playable:** unchanged entry points (campaign "Fight in real time ⚔" · the ⚔ SKIRMISH menu · the standalone Sandbox + First Bull Run). The difference: **named generals now ride the field** — a faint command ring + a mounted-officer marker; brigades under command steady and rally; a general riding the line too long can be **wounded or fall**, and his loss shocks the troops near him. At First Bull Run you see Beauregard, Johnston, Bee, Bartow, and (at 135s) Jackson — and the cost of leading from the saddle. The HUD shows your field officers' status; a selected brigade shows its commander + whether it's in command range.
>
> **IMMEDIATE NEXT = PHASE B (continued):** **B3** in-battle logistics (ammo + fatigue depth + supply) · **B4** distinct arm roles (artillery canister/long-range; cavalry scout/flank/screen/raid) · **B5** difficulty/realism presets · **B6** the **CS-player "command either side" mode** (fork #10 — now that `fldPlayerSide` is honored, this is the natural next). ## ⚡ CONTINUE HERE — run k (2026-06-15): **PHASE B-1 · the smarter ATTACKER AI ✅ SHIPPED + VETTED + PUSHED** → NEXT = Phase B continues (B2 officers · B3 logistics · B4 arm roles · B5 presets · B6 CS-player)

> **PHASE B (tactical depth) is underway. B1 — the smarter DEFENDER-FAVORED ATTACKER AI — is complete** (DECISIONS **D64**; charter fork #2). The complement to the P1b-iii defender: a real attacker doctrine `fldAiAttacker` in `src/tactical/T0-field-sandbox.js`, dispatched to the scenario attacker (the symmetric sandbox + the defender stay byte-identical — unreachable when `__FIELD.attacker===null`).
>
> **✅ B1 — `fldAiAttacker`:** a covered defender wins the long-range fire trade, so the attacker CONCENTRATES on the weaker flank, CLOSES, and ASSAULTS (melee + numbers beat fire + cover). **Two locks (fork #2):** (a) **GRADUAL** per-unit local-concentration commit (no knife-edge global flip — fixed the prototype) and (b) **FOG AIDS THE DEFENDER** — under fog the attacker NEVER mass-assaults / column-rushes (it can't trust its sight of the hidden reserves), only exploits visibly-broken units + probes in line. **Balance A/B (8 seeds, Bull Run AI-vs-AI, CS=defender wins):** both-generic fog-OFF **2/8** (the logged gap) → both-doctrines fog-OFF **6/8** (CS-favored but competitive — US breaks 2/8) / fog-ON **8/8** (fog aids the defender — the prototype's inversion tuned out); defender casualties **4592** vs the passive attacker's 1276 (~3.6×, a real bloody assault). **Bug-hunt 4 finders → 2 findings, BOTH FIXED** (a picket-unblind hole that broke the fog lock → gated the assault directly on `!fog`; a dead-constant doc nit → deleted). **probe-ai 15/15**; tactical no-regression GREEN (field 13/13, bullrun 15/15, fog 13/13, autopause 13/13, diag-classic paints); 0 pageerrors. Deliverable ≈**1552KB**.
>
> **What's playable:** unchanged entry points (campaign "Fight in real time ⚔" · the ⚔ SKIRMISH menu · the standalone Sandbox + First Bull Run). The difference: in any asymmetric battle the **ATTACKER AI now fights for real** — it masses on a flank and presses the bayonet (fog OFF) instead of trading fire it loses; under fog it advances cautiously, so the defender's hidden reserves matter. AI-vs-AI Bull Run is now a bloody, historically-faithful Confederate-favored fight rather than a Union walkover.
>
> **IMMEDIATE NEXT = PHASE B (continued):** **B2** officers/command (leaders: command radius + morale bonus, can be hit; ties named-generals) · **B3** in-battle logistics (ammo + fatigue depth + supply) · **B4** distinct arm roles (artillery canister/long-range; cavalry scout/flank/screen/raid) · **B5** difficulty/realism presets · **B6** the **CS-player "command either side" mode** (fork #10 — makes this attacker AI player-facing: you defend, the AI attacks). Then **Phase C** breadth → **D** full hex → **E** strategic S3–S5 → **H** graphics/footage/audio → **I** loot → **J** polish. **PUSH per milestone after PROPER VETTING (D53-OPS). Subagent models = HYBRID (D63).**
>

# HANDOFF — "The Civil War" — MASTER new-chat handoff (run h → next build)

**Written 2026-06-13 (run h, Opus 4.8). THE single entry point for the next session.** Personal project — NOT MJI. Full autonomy, Ultracode on, $0/free-CC0, build bar 200/100. **To start the next chat: paste the §PASTE block at the bottom.** Everything else here orients you.

---

## 1 · THE PROJECT IN ONE PARAGRAPH
"The Civil War" is becoming **the definitive teaching wargame: Ultimate General: Gettysburg's combat FEEL + Paradox-grade strategic DEPTH + a museum's historical RIGOR** — all three, no compromise. You are the **President (Lincoln or Davis)** running the entire war (economy, politics, diplomacy, morale, generals, the home front) AND commanding the battles in a real-time, gridless, drag-to-maneuver tactical engine — wrapped in a PhD-level history + alternate-history teaching layer where scholarly consensus is the *favored-but-not-guaranteed* path. **The governing design law (Aaron's central tension, resolved): the simple low-micro UG:G battle is the always-fun CORE that stands alone; ALL depth is optional — auto-managed by characterful advisors, delegable per-domain, preset-gated, fluidly dial-able; education is ambient, never blocks play, and confers real in-game mastery (so learning and fun pull the SAME direction).** One engine must serve the pure-battle player, the grand-strategist, and the PhD-historian simultaneously.

## 2 · WHERE THINGS STAND (run h, verified)
- **Classic (hex, turn-based) is FROZEN/finished** — the complete hex lineage + its 3D-skin "Modern." NEVER regress it (`tools/diag-classic.mjs` every splice).
- **Shipped + GPU-verified this run (all reusable):** procedural unit motion; **131 real PD-photo leader portraits** (`assets/portraits/`, license-gated, side-split for Anderson/Gregg) with a closure refactor + upgraded engraving fallback; a fuller period UI pass (drawn order-icons, engraved frames, brass portrait mat). Plus the inherited cinematic 3D foundation (PBR terrain, HDRI, ACES/bloom post-FX, weather, props, camera) and the adaptive period audio score.
- **The shipped "Modern" is only a 3D re-skin of the hex-turn engine** — it does NOT play like UG:G. The real-time tactical engine + the grand-strategy owner-mode are **NOT built yet** — that's the next work.
- **DESIGN: 63 decisions LOCKED** across a 35-round popup design session (Aaron). Fully captured in `GRAND-STRATEGY-PLAN.md` (Parts I–III). **No new-engine code written yet** — the next chat builds from the spec.

## 3 · THE DOCS (read in this order — you have zero prior context)
1. **`GRAND-STRATEGY-PLAN.md`** — THE DESIGN LAW. All 63 locked decisions: Part I (rounds 1–10: player/economy/executive/morale/alt-history/upgrades/education/victory/modes/first-build), Part II (rounds 11–20: diplomacy/slavery/politics/naval/map/generals/soldiers/intel/events/replay), Part III (rounds 21–33: **the depth↔simplicity balance** + presentation), §27 **the balance principle**, §13/§24/§28 decision indexes.
2. **`HISTORICAL-DATA.md`** — citation-grade content (211KB, 18-agent research): First Bull Run full OOB (Union + CSA), weapons/ballistics with ranges, USCT (structure/battles/criticism), the historiography framework, PD image sources. Feeds OOBs, weapon stats, debate cards, the codex.
3. **`MODERN-UGG-PLAN.md`** — the real-time tactical battle engine spec (built SECOND, after the strategic layer).
4. **`RUN-LOG.md` §"run h"** — what shipped + the pivot + this design session; skim §"run g"/§"run f" for the 3D foundation.
5. **`REVIEW-QUEUE.md` top** + **`GRAPHICS-RUN-CONTINUE.md` §TOOLCHAIN/GOTCHAS** — the splice/gate/real-GPU-shot toolchain + the bare-name-globals / async-THREE / override-by-redeclaration lessons.

## 4 · ARCHITECTURE DECISION (run h — important)
**Modularize via a zero-dependency build step.** The scope (50–100k+ lines) outgrows the single 14k-line file. Author the new engine in **source modules**; a tiny **node concat script** assembles the one shippable `civil_war_generals.html` (keep the $0/portable/open-and-play deliverable + `play.command`). Set this up FIRST in S0. Classic + the existing engine stay intact through the transition (move them into the build later, carefully, or keep the current HTML as the base the build appends to). Gate the BUILT output (parse/hex/Classic-regression). This replaces the append-only-single-file splice model for new work — but the gate discipline carries over.

## 5 · BUILD ORDER & PHASES (Aaron: STRATEGIC-FIRST, confirmed twice)
**Phase group S (grand-strategy owner-mode) FIRST, then phase group P (real-time tactical).** Battles use the EXISTING engine via a clean bridge until the UG:G engine swaps in.
- **S0 — scaffold:** set up the module/build system; expand the War Department (`G.campaign.{clock,muster,warroom}`) into the President's-desk shell (period war-room UI); strategic-turn loop; save. Layered scenario onboarding (start ~1861, limited toolset).
- **S1 — economy** (full asymmetric model + finance/inflation/blockade).
- **S2 — executive decisions + 3-layer morale + the 1864 election; the advisor/cabinet system** (the linchpin: auto-manage + recommend + TEACH, per-domain delegation).
- **S3 — alt-history engine** (tiered divergence + hinge-points + emergent toggle + curated scenarios; deterministic-by-performance with consensus framing; the "your war vs history" tracker).
- **S4 — education layer** (multi-voice debate cards + deep-dives + codex from HISTORICAL-DATA.md; ambient teaching; difficulty/realism sliders; play-style presets; full a11y).
- **S5 — victory/defeat** (asymmetric paths incl. **negotiated peace for both sides**, military-only hard-loss) + all campaign scopes; wire existing battles through the bridge → **playable full owner-mode war.**
- **THEN P0–P5** (`MODERN-UGG-PLAN.md`): the real-time UG:G tactical engine (First Bull Run vertical slice first), swapping in behind the bridge.
Each phase: gate + real-GPU self-verify (probes you READ) + Classic no-regression + left in a working, polished state (ship-quality iteratively).

## 6 · THE BALANCE PRINCIPLE (design law — apply to EVERY system)
The simple, low-micro UG:G battle is the always-fun core that works on its own. Every deep system is optional — auto-managed by characterful advisors, delegable per-domain, preset-gated, fluidly dial-able mid-campaign. Education is ambient, never blocks play, and confers real mastery (learning is rewarded by winning, not a chore-score). Presets + sliders let each player set their own balance. **If a feature forces depth on a battle-first player, it's wrong; if it denies depth to a strategist/historian, it's wrong.** (Full statement: GRAND-STRATEGY-PLAN §27.)

## 7 · TOOLCHAIN, GATES, GOTCHAS (proven — reuse)
- **Gates every change:** `.bak` → parse (`node --check` the built/extracted `<script>`) → invalid-hex grep `0x[0-9a-fA-F]*[g-z]` = 0 → collision-grep new fns = 0. With the new build step, gate the assembled output.
- **Real-GPU self-verify (READ the PNGs/probes):** `tools/shot-postfx.mjs`, `tools/shot-gpu.mjs`, `tools/shot-ui.mjs`, `tools/probe-motion.mjs`, `tools/probe-portraits.mjs`, `tools/bootprobe.mjs`, `tools/diag-classic.mjs`. Real GPU = **Intel UHD 617** via headed Chrome `--use-angle=metal`. A real-time sim/strategic loop needs an EMPIRICAL probe (sample state over frames), not just screenshots. Stale server: `lsof -ti tcp:8765 | xargs kill`.
- **Perf target:** smooth on the Intel UHD 617 at Auto tier; instancing/LOD/perf-tiers mandatory.
- **CRITICAL gotchas:** `G`/`HEX`/`PALETTE`/`THREE_BASE` are bare-name lexical globals (never `window.G`); THREE loads async (no top-level `new THREE.*`); override-by-redeclaration (last def wins); never a literal `*/` in a block comment; honor `reduceMotion` + `gfxQuality`; **NEVER regress Classic**; $0/free-CC0; Verified/Inferred provenance on all history.
- **Asset pipeline (historical run-H note; current top block supersedes live setup):** PD images via the run-h portrait pattern (Wikipedia REST summary → Commons license API PD-gate → download → `sips` resize). Extend to weapons/flags/USCT. Blender MCP remains available for future explicit 3D asset jobs (PolyHaven CC0 / Sketchfab), but Blender does not need to stay open unless a Phase-H asset task starts; free Hyper3D trial is drained.

## 8 · WORKING NORMS (Aaron)
- Full autonomy; build continuously; surface **questions only at a genuine design fork** — and Aaron LOVES **popup rounds of 3 questions, Recommended = option 1** (use AskUserQuestion). Aaron tests/feels much later — self-verify everything.
- Terse, direct; recommendation + reason on suggestions; push back on gaps; no emojis unless he uses one.
- The **modes & toggles, all-play-styles** spine governs everything; **protect everything (no cuts)** is the scope ambition (build iteratively, but aim to deliver the full vision).
